paragraphs.text: The above information is a general description of how a Group Insurance policy works. It applies not only to GHS policies, but also to all other types of Health Insurance policies, which you will learn about in the chapters that follow.
paragraphs.textJP: The above information is a general description of how a 団体保険 policy works. It applies not only to GHS policies, but also to all other types of Health Insurance policies, which you will learn about in the chapters that follow.

sections.title: Medical Insurance Requirements For Migrant Workers

paragraphs.text: Since 1 January 2008, subsidies on hospital bills for foreigners have been removed. With this revision, the Ministry of Manpower (MOM) requires every employer to buy, pay and maintain a minimum Medical Insurance coverage for its foreign workers and foreign domestic workers (maids) during their employment stay in Singapore This insurance is compulsory for every S Pass holder and Work Permit holder before MOM issues or renews their work permit.
paragraphs.textJP: Since 1 January 2008, subsidies on hospital bills for foreigners have been removed. With this revision, the 人材開発省 requires every employer to buy, pay and maintain a minimum Medical Insurance 補償 for its foreign workers and foreign domestic workers (maids) during their employment stay in Singapore This insurance is compulsory for every S Pass holder and 労働許可証（ワークパーミット） holder before MOM issues or renews their 労働許可証（ワークパーミット）.

paragraphs.text: From 1 July 2023, this insurance coverage has been enhanced for all new and existing work permit holders in two stages as shown in the table below: To implement for MI policies, renewals or extensions with start date effective from Enhanced MI requirements 1 July 2023 (Stage 1) Higher annual claim limit of at least S$60,000, with a co-payment of 75% by insurers and 25% by employers for claim amounts above S$15,000. 1 July 2025 (Stage 2)  Standardisation of allowable exclusion clauses  Introduction of age-differentiated premiums for those aged 50 and below, and those aged above 50  Requirement for insurers to reimburse hospitals directly upon the admissibility of the claim Source: https://www.mom.gov.sg/passes-and-permits/work-permit-for-foreignworker/ sector-specific-rules/medical-insurance
paragraphs.textJP: From 1 July 2023, this insurance 補償 has been enhanced for all new and existing 労働許可証（ワークパーミット） holders in two stages as shown in the table below: To implement for MI policies, renewals or 拡張担保（特約）s with start date effective from Enhanced MI requirements 1 July 2023 (Stage 1) Higher annual 保険金請求 limit of at least S$60,000, with a 自己負担 of 75% by 保険者s and 25% by employers for 保険金請求 amounts above S$15,000. 1 July 2025 (Stage 2)  Standardisation of allowable 免責事由 clauses  Introduction of age-differentiated 保険料s for those aged 50 and below, and those aged above 50  Requirement for 保険者s to reimburse hospitals directly upon the admissibility of the 保険金請求 Source: https://www.mom.gov.sg/passes-and-permits/work-permit-for-foreignworker/ sector-specific-rules/medical-insurance

paragraphs.text: Employers may purchase a medical insurance coverage of between S$15,000 to S$60,000 per year for each migrant worker which covers medical bill incurred for inpatient and day surgery, including hospital bills for conditions that may not be work related. For coverages above S$15,000, employers copay 25%.
paragraphs.textJP: Employers may pur地域医療支援スキームe a medical insurance 補償 of between S$15,000 to S$60,000 per year for each migrant worker which covers medical bill incurred for 入院患者 and day surgery, including hospital bills for conditions that may not be work related. For 補償s above S$15,000, employers copay 25%.

paragraphs.text: Employers can have a co-payment arrangement with the Work Permit holder (excluding migrant domestic workers) if the following conditions are met:  The co-pay amount is reasonable and does not exceed 10% of the employee’s monthly salary.  The duration of co-payment does not exceed six (6) months (for every 2 years of employment).  The co-payment option is explicitly in the employment contract or collective agreement and has the worker’s full consent.
paragraphs.textJP: Employers can have a 自己負担 arrangement with the 労働許可証（ワークパーミット） holder (excluding migrant domestic workers) if the following conditions are met:  The co-pay amount is reasonable and does not exceed 10% of the employee’s monthly salary.  The duration of 自己負担 does not exceed six (6) months (for every 2 years of employment).  The 自己負担 option is explicitly in the employment contract or collective agreement and has the worker’s full consent.

paragraphs.text: With shorter employment tenure and frequent job changes in the tight labour market, employees will need continuous inpatient medical provision, when they change jobs or during the period of unemployment. With each change, the employee may lose his medical benefits provided by the respective employer.
paragraphs.textJP: With shorter employment tenure and frequent job changes in the tight labour market, employees will need continuous 入院患者 medical provision, when they change jobs or during the period of unemployment. With each change, the employee may lose his medical 給付s provided by the respective employer.

paragraphs.text: Under PMBS, employers, instead of providing their employees with GHS coverage, make additional monthly contributions to the employees' respective MediSave accounts. Employees can then use the MediSave contributions to pay premiums for MediShield Life or Integrated Shield Plans
paragraphs.textJP: Under 民間メディシールド給付表, employers, instead of providing their employees with GHS 補償, make additional monthly contributions to the employees' respective メディセーブ accounts. Employees can then use the メディセーブ contributions to pay 保険料s for メディシールド・ライフ or Integrated Shield Plans

paragraphs.text: Any surplus MediSave contribution will accumulate in the employee’s MediSave Account and generate interest. The savings in the employee’s MediSave Account can be used to meet his future medical needs, as well as the medical needs of his dependants.
paragraphs.textJP: Any surplus メディセーブ contribution will accumulate in the employee’s メディセーブ Account and generate interest. The savings in the employee’s メディセーブ Account can be used to meet his future medical needs, as well as the medical needs of his dependants.

paragraphs.text: The advantage of this scheme is that the employee can continue to be medically insured, even though he may be unemployed or is in-between jobs. This is because the coverage will continue as long as the premium is paid, regardless of his employment status. It also reduces duplication of insurance plans and premium payments.
paragraphs.textJP: The advantage of this scheme is that the employee can continue to be medically 被保険者, even though he may be unemployed or is in-between jobs. This is because the 補償 will continue as long as the 保険料 is paid, regardless of his employment status. It also reduces duplication of insurance plans and 保険料 payments.

paragraphs.text:
The employer will enjoy a 2% tax deduction. To be eligible for the tax deduction, the employer must satisfy the following conditions:
▪ The scheme must cover at least 20% of the local employees1 recruited as at the first day of financial year being assessed, and all local employees who commence their employment during that financial year;
▪ For full-time employees, the additional monthly contributions to MediSave accounts should be at least 1% of each employee’s gross monthly salary, subject to a minimum amount of S$16 per calendar month; and
▪ For part-time employees2, the additional monthly MediSave contributions should be computed based on 1% of their actual gross salary for the calendar month.
paragraphs.textJP:
The employer will enjoy a 2% 税額控除. To be eligible for the 税額控除, the employer must satisfy the following conditions:
▪ The scheme must cover at least 20% of the local employees1 recruited as at the first day of financial year being assessed, and all local employees who commence their employment during that financial year;
▪ For full-time employees, the additional monthly contributions to メディセーブ accounts should be at least 1% of each employee’s gross monthly salary, subject to a minimum amount of S$16 per calendar month; and
▪ For part-time employees2, the additional monthly メディセーブ contributions should be computed based on 1% of their actual gross salary for the calendar month.

paragraphs.text: An employer who wishes to make additional MediSave contribution for his employees will need to apply for a new CPF Submission Number (CSN) with the CPF Board. 1 Local employees refer to Singapore citizens or Singapore Permanent Residents employed on a fulltime or part-time contract of employment, regardless of the number of hours worked. 2 Part-time employees refer to employees who are required under their contract of service with their employers to work regularly for less than 30 hours a week.
paragraphs.textJP: An employer who wishes to make additional メディセーブ contribution for his employees will need to apply for a new CPF（中央積立基金） Submission Number (CSN) with the CPF（中央積立基金） Board. 1 Local employees refer to Singapore citizens or Singapore Permanent Residents employed on a fulltime or part-time contract of employment, regardless of the number of hours worked. 2 Part-time employees refer to employees who are required under their contract of service with their employers to work regularly for less than 30 hours a week.

paragraphs.text: However, for employers who choose not to implement either the PMBS or TMIS, their tax deductibility in respect of medical expenses will be maintained at the present 1% of the total employees’ remuneration.
paragraphs.textJP: However, for employers who choose not to implement either the 民間メディシールド給付表 or 転換可能医療保険制度, their tax deductibility in respect of medical expenses will be maintained at the present 1% of the total employees’ remuneration.

paragraphs.text: The TMIS is an enhanced GHS that extends inpatient coverage for up to 12 months when an employee leaves employment.
paragraphs.textJP: The 転換可能医療保険制度 is an enhanced GHS that extends 入院患者 補償 for up to 12 months when an employee leaves employment.

paragraphs.text:
TMIS has two features:
▪ continuation of coverage; and
▪ transferability of benefits.
paragraphs.textJP:
転換可能医療保険制度 has two features:
▪ continuation of 補償; and
▪ transferability of 給付s.

paragraphs.text: Let us look at how the TMIS works.
paragraphs.textJP: Let us look at how the 転換可能医療保険制度 works.

subsections.label: B1
subsections.title: How To Qualify For The TMIS?

paragraphs.text: To qualify for the TMIS, the employer must:  have a group size of 11 or more employees;  take up a GHS Insurance plan;  insure at least 50% (computed using X/Y x 100%, where X = all local employees who are on the PMBS or TMIS, and Y = total number of local employees in the employing company) of its local employees, subject to a minimum total of 11 employees. The benefits can be provided for predefined categories of employees, e.g. rank-and-file employees or executives;  pay 100% of the premium for the GHS Insurance coverage (Note: Employers are allowed to recover the premiums for continuation benefits after the termination of employment, wholly or partly from the employees, based on agreement with the employees); and  not give their employees the option as to whether they wish to be insured under the GHS Insurance plan.
paragraphs.textJP: To qualify for the 転換可能医療保険制度, the employer must:  have a group size of 11 or more employees;  take up a GHS Insurance plan;  insure at least 50% (computed using X/Y x 100%, where X = all local employees who are on the 民間メディシールド給付表 or 転換可能医療保険制度, and Y = total number of local employees in the employing company) of its local employees, subject to a minimum total of 11 employees. The 給付s can be provided for predefined categories of employees, e.g. rank-and-file employees or executives;  pay 100% of the 保険料 for the GHS Insurance 補償 (Note: Employers are allowed to recover the 保険料s for continuation 給付s after the termination of employment, wholly or partly from the employees, based on agreement with the employees); and  not give their employees the option as to whether they wish to be 被保険者 under the GHS Insurance plan.

subsections.label: B2
subsections.title: How Can The Two TMIS Features Benefit The Employees?

paragraphs.text: Continuation Of Coverage Benefits This feature enables an employee whose employment is terminated either by his employer, or on his own accord, to continue enjoying hospitalisation coverage from the employment termination date up to a period of 12 months (not exceeding the prescribed statutory age). Employees are not given option as to whether they wish to be insured under the Group MEI Plan.
paragraphs.textJP: Continuation Of 補償 給付s This feature enables an employee whose employment is terminated either by his employer, or on his own accord, to continue enjoying 入院 補償 from the employment termination date up to a period of 12 months (not exceeding the prescribed statutory age). Employees are not given option as to whether they wish to be 被保険者 under the Group MEI Plan.

paragraphs.text: Transferability Of Benefits This feature enables an employee who resigns from one employing company which holds a TMIS policy, and joins another which also holds a TMIS policy, to enjoy the following benefits: (a) automatic coverage under the new employer’s GHS Insurance plan, without the need to provide evidence of good health; and (b) waiver of any exclusion on pre-existing medical conditions if the employee has been continuously insured under one or more TMIS plans for 12 months before joining his new employer. If he has not been insured continuously for at least 12 months under the TMIS plans before joining his new employer, he will be entitled to the waiver when he completes the 12 months of continuous coverage under the new employer’s TMIS plan.
paragraphs.textJP: Transferability Of 給付s This feature enables an employee who resigns from one employing company which holds a 転換可能医療保険制度 policy, and joins another which also holds a 転換可能医療保険制度 policy, to enjoy the following 給付s: (a) automatic 補償 under the new employer’s GHS Insurance plan, without the need to provide evidence of good health; and (b) waiver of any 免責事由 on pre-existing medical conditions if the employee has been continuously 被保険者 under one or more 転換可能医療保険制度 plans for 12 months before joining his new employer. If he has not been 被保険者 continuously for at least 12 months under the 転換可能医療保険制度 plans before joining his new employer, he will be entitled to the waiver when he completes the 12 months of continuous 補償 under the new employer’s 転換可能医療保険制度 plan.

paragraphs.text:
However, if the employee is hospitalised for treatment of a condition which is pre-existing when he joins the new employer, he will enjoy a lower benefit within the first 12 months of cover under the new employer’s plan. The cover is the lower entitlement of either the:
▪ new employer’s TMIS plan; or
▪ prior employer’s TMIS plan.
paragraphs.textJP:
However, if the employee is hospitalised for treatment of a condition which is pre-existing when he joins the new employer, he will enjoy a lower 給付 within the first 12 months of cover under the new employer’s plan. The cover is the lower entitlement of either the:
▪ new employer’s 転換可能医療保険制度 plan; or
▪ prior employer’s 転換可能医療保険制度 plan.

paragraphs.text: Example 3.1 illustrates how an employee’s medical fees are covered should he contract an illness while working for his previous employer.
paragraphs.textJP: Example 3.1 illustrates how an employee’s medical fees are covered should he contract an illness while working for his previous employer.

paragraphs.text: Note that the transferability of benefits will not be available, if the employee moves from an employer with a non-TMIS GHS Insurance plan to an employer with a TMIS plan, and vice versa. Example 3.1 Danny changed his job in year 2023. Below are the details: Date of termination with Employer A – 1 January 2023 Date of employment with Employer B – 15 January 2023 Benefit Entitlement Employer A Employer B Daily Room & Board S$250 per day S$350 per day Total of eligible benefits S$5,000 S$8,000 Danny was hospitalised on 1 April 2023 due to an illness he contracted while in Employer A’s employment. He received a total benefit of S$5,000 as he has not been continuously insured by Employer B for 12 months as at 1 April 2023 Should Danny be once again hospitalised for the same condition after 15 January 2024, he will be able to fully utilise Employer B benefits. .
paragraphs.textJP: Note that the transferability of 給付s will not be available, if the employee moves from an employer with a non-転換可能医療保険制度 GHS Insurance plan to an employer with a 転換可能医療保険制度 plan, and vice versa. Example 3.1 Danny changed his job in year 2023. Below are the details: Date of termination with Employer A – 1 January 2023 Date of employment with Employer B – 15 January 2023 給付 Entitlement Employer A Employer B Daily Room & Board S$250 per day S$350 per day Total of eligible 給付s S$5,000 S$8,000 Danny was hospitalised on 1 April 2023 due to an illness he contracted while in Employer A’s employment. He received a total 給付 of S$5,000 as he has not been continuously 被保険者 by Employer B for 12 months as at 1 April 2023 Should Danny be once again hospitalised for the same condition after 15 January 2024, he will be able to fully utilise Employer B 給付s. .

subsections.label: B3
subsections.title: What Are The Medical Expenses That Qualify Under TMIS Special Benefits?

paragraphs.text: All pre/post hospitalisation outpatient, emergency outpatient accident and outpatient kidney dialysis/cancer treatment expenses are not eligible for TMIS benefits, even if they can be reimbursed under the TMIS plan.
paragraphs.textJP: All pre/post 入院 外来, emergency 外来 accident and 外来 kidney dialysis/cancer treatment expenses are not eligible for 転換可能医療保険制度 給付s, even if they can be reimbursed under the 転換可能医療保険制度 plan.

subsections.label: B4
subsections.title: Who Are The Employees Eligible For TMIS Benefits?

paragraphs.text: Claims for continuation of benefits will be payable from the previous employer’s TMIS plan.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険金請求s for continuation of 給付s will be payable from the previous employer’s 転換可能医療保険制度 plan.

paragraphs.text: Claims for transferability of benefits on or after the employee has joined the new employer will be payable from the new employer’s TMIS plan.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険金請求s for transferability of 給付s on or after the employee has joined the new employer will be payable from the new employer’s 転換可能医療保険制度 plan.

subsections.label: B6
subsections.title: How Insurers Verify The Eligibility Of An Employee Under The TMIS?

paragraphs.text: An employer has to issue a Transferable Medical Insurance Certification (TMIC) at the request of an employee upon the termination of his employment. The TMIC must be submitted to the insurer when the employee submits a claim for Continuation Benefit, or to the new employer’s insurer should a claim arising from a pre-existing condition be made within 12 months of joining the new employer.
paragraphs.textJP: An employer has to issue a Transferable Medical Insurance Certification (転換可能医療保険証明書) at the request of an employee upon the termination of his employment. The 転換可能医療保険証明書 must be submitted to the 保険者 when the employee submits a 保険金請求 for Continuation 給付, or to the new employer’s 保険者 should a 保険金請求 arising from a 既存疾病 be made within 12 months of joining the new employer.

subsections.label: B7
subsections.title: Will It Cost More For A Company To Adopt The TMIS?

paragraphs.text: The insurer will charge an additional premium if a policy is issued under the TMIS. However, this will be partially offset by the additional tax deduction (1%) which the employer is entitled to.
paragraphs.textJP: The 保険者 will charge an additional 保険料 if a policy is issued under the 転換可能医療保険制度. However, this will be partially offset by the additional 税額控除 (1%) which the employer is entitled to.

paragraphs.text: The differences between PMBS and TMIS are described below: PMBS TMIS The employer makes an additional contribution to the Employee’s MediSave Account for him to purchase the policy on his own. The employer purchases the policy. Can purchase only a Medical Insurance policy approved under the MediSave Scheme (e.g. an Integrated Shield Plan from an approved private insurer). Can purchase any GHS Insurance policy offered in the market other than those approved under the MediSave Scheme. The coverage is lifetime for most Integrated Shield Plans in the insurance market. The coverage expires at the prescribed statutory retirement age. The coverage continues even when the employee is between jobs or out of a job, as long as the premium is paid. The coverage continues up to a maximum period of 12 months from the date of termination from a job. There are deductibles and coinsurance features. Riders can be purchased to offset the deductibles and co-insurance. It is unlikely to have deductibles and co-insurance features, as GHS Insurance policies rarely have these features. Treatment for pre-existing illnesses is typically excluded. Waiver on pre-existing conditions is allowed, when employees change jobs and where both employers provide TMIS benefits. The employer has no control over the policy which the employee has purchased. The employer continues to have full control over the GHS Insurance policy, and the level of benefits can be structured according to the corporate employee benefit philosophy.
paragraphs.textJP: The differences between 民間メディシールド給付表 and 転換可能医療保険制度 are described below: 民間メディシールド給付表 転換可能医療保険制度 The employer makes an additional contribution to the Employee’s メディセーブ Account for him to pur地域医療支援スキームe the policy on his own. The employer pur地域医療支援スキームes the policy. Can pur地域医療支援スキームe only a Medical Insurance policy approved under the メディセーブ Scheme (e.g. an Integrated Shield Plan from an approved private 保険者). Can pur地域医療支援スキームe any GHS Insurance policy offered in the market other than those approved under the メディセーブ Scheme. The 補償 is lifetime for most Integrated Shield Plans in the insurance market. The 補償 expires at the prescribed statutory retirement age. The 補償 continues even when the employee is between jobs or out of a job, as long as the 保険料 is paid. The 補償 continues up to a maximum period of 12 months from the date of termination from a job. There are 免責金額s and coinsurance features. 特約s can be pur地域医療支援スキームed to offset the 免責金額s and 共同保険. It is unlikely to have 免責金額s and 共同保険 features, as GHS Insurance policies rarely have these features. Treatment for pre-existing illnesses is typically excluded. Waiver on 既存疾病s is allowed, when employees change jobs and where both employers provide 転換可能医療保険制度 給付s. The employer has no control over the policy which the employee has pur地域医療支援スキームed. The employer continues to have full control over the GHS Insurance policy, and the level of 給付s can be structured according to the corporate employee 給付 philosophy.

paragraphs.text:
Employer may choose to pay for a MediShield Life or Integrated Shield Plan (IP) for the employees. An employer providing employees with an IP can claim tax deduction for medical expenses incurred, up to 2% of the total employees’ remuneration, upon meeting the following qualifying conditions:
▪ provide an IP for at least 20% of the local employees as at the first day of the financial year being assessed, and all local employees who commence their employment during that financial year; and
▪ pay IP premiums on behalf of the employees directly to the approved insurer or reimburse the premiums into the respective employees’ MediSave Accounts.
paragraphs.textJP:
Employer may choose to pay for a メディシールド・ライフ or 統合シールドプラン for the employees. An employer providing employees with an IP can 保険金請求 税額控除 for medical expenses incurred, up to 2% of the total employees’ remuneration, upon meeting the following qualifying conditions:
▪ provide an IP for at least 20% of the local employees as at the first day of the financial year being assessed, and all local employees who commence their employment during that financial year; and
▪ pay IP 保険料s on behalf of the employees directly to the approved 保険者 or reimburse the 保険料s into the respective employees’ メディセーブ Accounts.

paragraphs.text: However, the additional tax deduction as mentioned above excludes premiums for the “Riders on IPs” that cover deductibles and co-insurance payments.
paragraphs.textJP: However, the additional 税額控除 as mentioned above excludes 保険料s for the “特約s on IPs” that cover 免責金額s and 共同保険 payments.

subsections.label: E
subsections.title: Ad-hoc Contributions To Employees' MediSave Accounts

paragraphs.text: An employer can make ad-hoc MediSave contributions to the employees' MediSave Accounts, even if they are not adopting any of the portable medical benefits options as described earlier.
paragraphs.textJP: An employer can make ad-hoc メディセーブ contributions to the employees' メディセーブ Accounts, even if they are not adopting any of the portable medical 給付s options as described earlier.

paragraphs.text: To encourage such contributions, the employer can get an additional tax deduction beyond the 1% limit for the amount of ad-hoc MediSave contributions made. Additional contributions are subject to a limit of S$2,730 per employee per year. The overall tax deduction for medical expenses will be subject to the overall cap of 2%.
paragraphs.textJP: To encourage such contributions, the employer can get an additional 税額控除 beyond the 1% limit for the amount of ad-hoc メディセーブ contributions made. Additional contributions are subject to a limit of S$2,730 per employee per year. The overall 税額控除 for medical expenses will be subject to the overall cap of 2%.

paragraphs.text:
If the employer wishes to make additional MediSave contributions for the employees, the employer will need to register with the CPF board for a new CPF Submission Number (CSN). Such contributions are tax-free and are subject to a cap per employee per year. Refer to the MOM website under schemes for employers and employees for the cap amount: http://www.mom.gov.sg GROUP HOSPITAL AND SURGICAL (GHS) INSURANCE IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Criteria for a group to be eligible for Group Insurance cover The requirement is that several people must first be members of a group, before they can be eligible to purchase the insurance. The group must have been formed for some purposes other than to obtain insurance, such as companies, trade unions, professional associations and clubs. Characteristics of a Group Insurance policy
▪ Master Contract.
▪ Minimal Underwriting Requirements.
▪ Experience-rating.
▪ Cost Effectiveness.
▪ Plan Continuation.
▪ Eligibility Requirements. In addition, Group Insurance policies have an actively-at-work clause. GHS Insurance GHS Insurance policies are more often purchased by employers as part of their employee benefits package. The coverage of GHS policies can be extended to the employees’ immediate family members (spouses and children). Differences between a compulsory and a voluntary plan
▪ For a compulsory (also known as non-contributory) plan, all the eligible employees must be covered under the plan, and the premiums have to be paid solely by the employer.
▪ A voluntary (also known as contributory) plan, on the other hand, does not require full participation from the employees who may be expected to pay part of the premiums. Advantages Of A Compulsory (Non- Contributory) Plan
▪ It provides ease of administration, since there is no regular payroll deduction to monitor.
▪ It comes with lower costs owing to less administrative work involved, and the greater pooling effect of risks as a result of many lives insured.
▪ It helps the employer to retain greater control of the benefit structures and provisions. CHAPTER IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Advantages Of A Voluntary (Contributory) Plan
▪ Employer » Participating employees assume part of the costs of the benefits as provided under the Group Insurance plan. » It generates interest and appreciation from the participating employees of the Group Insurance plan.
▪ Employees » It gives the participating employees some control over the Group Insurance plan. » They can obtain coverage at a lower premium rate than buying it individually. Medical Insurance Requirements for Migrant Workers Since 1 January 2008, subsidies on hospital bills for foreigners have been removed. With this revision, the Ministry of Manpower (MOM) requires every employer to buy, pay and maintain a minimum Medical Insurance coverage for its foreign workers and foreign domestic workers (maids) during their employment stay in Singapore This insurance is compulsory for every S Pass holder and Work Permit holder before MOM issues or renews their work permit. Employers may purchase a medical insurance coverage of between S$15,000 to S$60,000 per year for each migrant worker which covers medical bill incurred for inpatient and day surgery, including hospital bills for conditions that may not be work related. For coverages above S$15,000, employers co-pay 25%. Employers can have a co-payment arrangement with the Work Permit holder (excluding migrant domestic workers) if the following conditions are met:
▪ The co-pay amount is reasonable and does not exceed 10% of the employee’s monthly salary.
▪ The duration of co-payment does not exceed six (6) months (for every 2 years of employment).
▪ The co-payment option is explicitly in the employment contract or collective agreement and has the worker’s full consent. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Differences between Individual and Group Medical Expense Insurance
▪ Individual insurance policies are issued to individuals.
▪ Group Medical Expense Insurance policies are more often purchased by employers as part of their employee benefits to attract and retain their employees.
▪ Can be extended to include the individual’s immediate family members (usually spouse and children). Individual Insurance Group Insurance Eligibility Only the individual who applies for the coverage is covered. An individual needs only to be insurable, in order to be granted the coverage. Only members who belong to the group and are actively at work are covered. Members under a group must satisfy the eligibility requirements as stated in the Group Insurance policy, before they are granted the coverage. Contract Each individual policy owner gets a policy contract. Only one master contract is issued to the employer or an affiliated organisation. The number of insured members is more than one. Choice of Plan An individual policy owner has the right to select the amount of coverage that he wants. Insured members may or may not have the right to decide on the amount of coverage that they want. In the employer-paid plans/compulsory plans, the amount of coverage is determined by the employer. Underwriting Individual’s health history, lifestyle and financial status are evaluated. Group as a whole is evaluated depending on the gender and age distribution of the group, occupation mix and past claims experience. Termination of Cover Coverage continues until the individual or insurer chooses to terminate it. The individual life insured’s coverage stops when he leaves the group. However, the insurance plan continues for the remaining members. Premium Cost of coverage is higher because of individual underwriting and higher administrative costs. Premium is age-related. Cost of coverage is lower because of group underwriting and lower administrative costs. Premium is unit-related. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS How the Portable Medical Benefits Scheme (PMBS) works This scheme rides on the Medisave/MediShield Life framework. Under this scheme, the employers, instead of providing their employees with Group Medical Insurance coverage, make additional monthly contributions to the employees' respective Medisave accounts. Employees can then use the Medisave contributions to purchase MediShield Life or any one of the private insurers’ Integrated Shield Plans, to cover their inpatient or hospitalisation needs. Advantages of the PMBS • Any surplus Medisave contribution will accumulate in the employee’s Medisave Account and generate interest.
▪ Employee can continue to be medically insured, even though he may be unemployed or is in-between jobs. This is because the coverage will continue as long as the premium is paid, regardless of his employment status. It also reduces duplication of insurance plans and premium payments.
▪ The employer will enjoy a 2% tax deduction, subject to conditions. How the Transferable Medical Insurance Scheme (TMIS) works The TMIS is a private hospitalisation insurance arrangement among participating insurers. It is to provide continuous Medical Expense Insurance coverage to an employee, starting from the date of leaving the service of his employer for whatever reasons up to a period of 12 months, as long as the insurance premium is paid. A TMIS policy is an enhancement of the existing employer-sponsored Group Medical Expense Insurance outside of the CPF Medisave framework. Areas that an employer must do in order to be eligible for the TMIS To qualify for the TMIS, the employer must:
▪ have a group size of 11 or more employees;
▪ take up a Group Medical Expense Insurance plan;
▪ insure at least 50% (computed using X/Y x 100%, where X = all local employees who are on the PMBS or TMIS, and Y = total number of local employees in the employing company) of its local employees, subject to a minimum total of 11 employees. The benefits can be provided for pre-defined categories of employees, e.g. rank-and-file employees or executives;
▪ pay 100% of the premium for the Group Medical Expense Insurance coverage (Note: Employers are allowed to recover the premiums for continuation benefits after the termination of employment, wholly or partly from the employees, based on agreement with the employees); and
▪ not give their employees the option as to whether they wish to be insured under the Group Medical Expense Insurance plan. Two main features under the TMIS, namely:
▪ continuation benefit
▪ transferability benefit Continuation Of Coverage Benefits
▪ This feature enables an employee whose employment is terminated either by his employer, or on his own accord, to continue enjoying hospitalisation coverage from the employment termination date up to a period of 12 months (not exceeding the prescribed statutory age). Transferability Of Benefits
▪ This feature enables an employee who resigns from one employing company which holds a TMIS policy, and joins another which also holds a TMIS policy, to enjoy the following benefits:
▪ automatic coverage under the new employer’s Group Medical Expense Insurance plan, without the need to provide evidence of good health; and
▪ waiver of any exclusion on pre-existing medical conditions if the employee has been continuously insured under one or more TMIS plans for 12 months before joining his new employer. If he has not been insured continuously for at least 12 months under the TMIS plans before joining his new employer, he will be entitled to the waiver when he completes the 12 months of continuous coverage under the new employer’s TMIS plan. Medical expenses that qualify under the TMIS Special Benefit
▪ Daily room and board charges;
▪ Intensive care unit charges;
▪ Charges for miscellaneous hospital services;
▪ Charges for in-hospital doctor’s visits; and
▪ Charges for inpatient and outpatient benefits. All pre/post hospitalisation outpatient, emergency outpatient accident and outpatient kidney dialysis/cancer treatment expenses are not eligible for TMIS benefits, even if they can be reimbursed under the TMIS plan. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Differences between PMBS and TMIS Provisions of an Integrated Shield Plan on tax deductions An employer providing employees with an IP can claim tax deduction for medical expenses incurred, up to 2% of the total employees’ remuneration, upon meeting the following qualifying conditions:
▪ provide an IP for at least 20% of the local employees as at the first day of the financial year being assessed, and all local employees who commence their employment during that financial year; and
▪ pay IP premiums on behalf of the employees directly to the approved insurer, or reimburse the premiums into the respective employees’ Medisave Accounts. Tax deductions for ad-hoc MediSave contributions An employer can make ad-hoc MediSave contributions to the employees' MediSave Accounts, even if they are not adopting any of the portable medical benefits options as described earlier. To encourage such contributions, the employer can get an additional tax deduction beyond the 1% limit for the amount of ad-hoc MediSave contributions made. Additional contributions are subject to a limit of S$2,730 per employee per year. The overall tax deduction for medical expenses will be subject to the overall cap of 2%. If the employer wishes to make additional MediSave contributions for the employees, the employer will need to register with the CPF board for a new CPF Submission Number. Such contributions are tax-free and are subject to a cap per employee per year. Refer to the MOM website under schemes for employers and employees for the cap amount: http://www.mom.gov.sg PMBS TMIS The employer makes an additional contribution to the Employee’s Medisave Account for him to purchase the policy on his own. The employer purchases the policy. Can purchase only a Medical Insurance policy approved under the Medisave Scheme (e.g. an Integrated Shield Plan from an approved private insurer). Can purchase any Group Medical Expense Insurance policy offered in the market other than those approved under the Medisave Scheme. The coverage is lifetime for most Integrated Shield Plans in the insurance market. The coverage expires at the prescribed statutory retirement age. The coverage continues even when the employee is between jobs or out of a job, as long as the premium is paid. The coverage continues up to a maximum period of 12 months from the date of termination from a job. There are deductibles and coinsurance features. Riders can be purchased to offset the deductibles and co-insurance. It is unlikely to have deductibles and co-insurance features, as Group Medical Expense Insurance policies rarely have these features. Treatment for pre-existing illnesses is typically excluded. Waiver on pre-existing conditions is allowed, when employees change jobs and where both employers provide TMIS benefits. The employer has no control over the policy which the employee has purchased. The employer continues to have full control over the Group Medical Expense Insurance policy, and the level of benefits can be structured according to the corporate employee benefit philosophy.

However, the insurance plan continues for the remaining members. 保険料 Cost of 補償 is higher because of individual 引受審査 and higher administrative costs. 保険料 is age-related. Cost of 補償 is lower because of group 引受審査 and lower administrative costs. 保険料 is unit-related. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS How the Portable Medical 給付s Scheme (民間メディシールド給付表) works This scheme rides on the メディセーブ/メディシールド・ライフ framework. Under this scheme, the employers, instead of providing their employees with Group Medical Insurance 補償, make additional monthly contributions to the employees' respective メディセーブ accounts. Employees can then use the メディセーブ contributions to pur地域医療支援スキームe メディシールド・ライフ or any one of the private 保険者s’ Integrated Shield Plans, to cover their 入院患者 or 入院 needs. Advantages of the 民間メディシールド給付表 • Any surplus メディセーブ contribution will accumulate in the employee’s メディセーブ Account and generate interest.
▪ Employee can continue to be medically 被保険者, even though he may be unemployed or is in-between jobs. This is because the 補償 will continue as long as the 保険料 is paid, regardless of his employment status. It also reduces duplication of insurance plans and 保険料 payments.
▪ The employer will enjoy a 2% 税額控除, subject to conditions. How the Transferable Medical Insurance Scheme (転換可能医療保険制度) works The 転換可能医療保険制度 is a private 入院 insurance arrangement among participating 保険者s. It is to provide continuous 医療費保険 補償 to an employee, starting from the date of leaving the service of his employer for whatever reasons up to a period of 12 months, as long as the insurance 保険料 is paid. A 転換可能医療保険制度 policy is an enhancement of the existing employer-sponsored Group 医療費保険 outside of the CPF（中央積立基金） メディセーブ framework. Areas that an employer must do in order to be eligible for the 転換可能医療保険制度 To qualify for the 転換可能医療保険制度, the employer must:
▪ have a group size of 11 or more employees;
▪ take up a Group 医療費保険 plan;
▪ insure at least 50% (computed using X/Y x 100%, where X = all local employees who are on the 民間メディシールド給付表 or 転換可能医療保険制度, and Y = total number of local employees in the employing company) of its local employees, subject to a minimum total of 11 employees. The 給付s can be provided for pre-defined categories of employees, e.g. rank-and-file employees or executives;
▪ pay 100% of the 保険料 for the Group 医療費保険 補償 (Note: Employers are allowed to recover the 保険料s for continuation 給付s after the termination of employment, wholly or partly from the employees, based on agreement with the employees); and
▪ not give their employees the option as to whether they wish to be 被保険者 under the Group 医療費保険 plan. Two main features under the 転換可能医療保険制度, namely:
▪ continuation 給付
▪ transferability 給付 Continuation Of 補償 給付s
▪ This feature enables an employee whose employment is terminated either by his employer, or on his own accord, to continue enjoying 入院 補償 from the employment termination date up to a period of 12 months (not exceeding the prescribed statutory age). Transferability Of 給付s
▪ This feature enables an employee who resigns from one employing company which holds a 転換可能医療保険制度 policy, and joins another which also holds a 転換可能医療保険制度 policy, to enjoy the following 給付s:
▪ automatic 補償 under the new employer’s Group 医療費保険 plan, without the need to provide evidence of good health; and
▪ waiver of any 免責事由 on pre-existing medical conditions if the employee has been continuously 被保険者 under one or more 転換可能医療保険制度 plans for 12 months before joining his new employer. If he has not been 被保険者 continuously for at least 12 months under the 転換可能医療保険制度 plans before joining his new employer, he will be entitled to the waiver when he completes the 12 months of continuous 補償 under the new employer’s 転換可能医療保険制度 plan. Medical expenses that qualify under the 転換可能医療保険制度 Special 給付
▪ Daily room and board charges;
▪ Intensive care unit charges;
▪ Charges for miscellaneous hospital services;
▪ Charges for in-hospital doctor’s visits; and
▪ Charges for 入院患者 and 外来 給付s. All pre/post 入院 外来, emergency 外来 accident and 外来 kidney dialysis/cancer treatment expenses are not eligible for 転換可能医療保険制度 給付s, even if they can be reimbursed under the 転換可能医療保険制度 plan. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Differences between 民間メディシールド給付表 and 転換可能医療保険制度 Provisions of an Integrated Shield Plan on 税額控除s An employer providing employees with an IP can 保険金請求 税額控除 for medical expenses incurred, up to 2% of the total employees’ remuneration, upon meeting the following qualifying conditions:
▪ provide an IP for at least 20% of the local employees as at the first day of the financial year being assessed, and all local employees who commence their employment during that financial year; and
▪ pay IP 保険料s on behalf of the employees directly to the approved 保険者, or reimburse the 保険料s into the respective employees’ メディセーブ Accounts. 税額控除s for ad-hoc メディセーブ contributions An employer can make ad-hoc メディセーブ contributions to the employees' メディセーブ Accounts, even if they are not adopting any of the portable medical 給付s options as described earlier. To encourage such contributions, the employer can get an additional 税額控除 beyond the 1% limit for the amount of ad-hoc メディセーブ contributions made. Additional contributions are subject to a limit of S$2,730 per employee per year. The overall 税額控除 for medical expenses will be subject to the overall cap of 2%. If the employer wishes to make additional メディセーブ contributions for the employees, the employer will need to register with the CPF（中央積立基金） board for a new CPF（中央積立基金） Submission Number. Such contributions are tax-free and are subject to a cap per employee per year. Refer to the MOM website under schemes for employers and employees for the cap amount: http://www.mom.gov.sg 民間メディシールド給付表 転換可能医療保険制度 The employer makes an additional contribution to the Employee’s メディセーブ Account for him to pur地域医療支援スキームe the policy on his own. The employer pur地域医療支援スキームes the policy. Can pur地域医療支援スキームe only a Medical Insurance policy approved under the メディセーブ Scheme (e.g. an Integrated Shield Plan from an approved private 保険者). Can pur地域医療支援スキームe any Group 医療費保険 policy offered in the market other than those approved under the メディセーブ Scheme. The 補償 is lifetime for most Integrated Shield Plans in the insurance market. The 補償 expires at the prescribed statutory retirement age. The 補償 continues even when the employee is between jobs or out of a job, as long as the 保険料 is paid. The 補償 continues up to a maximum period of 12 months from the date of termination from a job. There are 免責金額s and coinsurance features. 特約s can be pur地域医療支援スキームed to offset the 免責金額s and 共同保険. It is unlikely to have 免責金額s and 共同保険 features, as Group 医療費保険 policies rarely have these features. Treatment for pre-existing illnesses is typically excluded. Waiver on 既存疾病s is allowed, when employees change jobs and where both employers provide 転換可能医療保険制度 給付s. The employer has no control over the policy which the employee has pur地域医療支援スキームed. The employer continues to have full control over the Group 医療費保険 policy, and the level of 給付s can be structured according to the corporate employee 給付 philosophy.

paragraphs.text: While an individual should plan for his retirement and premature death, it is also critical for one to protect his earning capacity, whether he is an employee or a self-employed individual. This is because he could lose his earning power should he become disabled from an accident or illness and be incapable of paying his living and medical expenses, including the premiums to keep his insurance policies in force. On top of this, his family’s ongoing expenses continue including repayment of any outstanding loans. Expenses may even increase due to his medical treatments or added cost of a caregiver. The family would be financially strained with the incapacity of the breadwinner.
paragraphs.textJP: While an individual should plan for his retirement and premature death, it is also critical for one to protect his earning capacity, whether he is an employee or a self-employed individual. This is because he could lose his earning power should he become disabled from an accident or illness and be incapable of paying his living and medical expenses, including the 保険料s to keep his insurance policies in force. On top of this, his family’s ongoing expenses continue including repayment of any outstanding loans. Expenses may even increase due to his medical treatments or added cost of a caregiver. The family would be financially strained with the incapacity of the breadwinner.

paragraphs.text: Disability Income Insurance helps to safeguard a person’s income when he cannot work for medical reasons. Let’s look at both Individual and Group Disability Income Insurance, including their underwriting and claim procedures.
paragraphs.textJP: Disability Income Insurance helps to safeguard a person’s income when he cannot work for medical reasons. Let’s look at both Individual and 団体所得補償保険 Insurance, including their 引受審査 and 保険金請求 procedures.

paragraphs.text: Disability Income Insurance is an income-protection in the event of incapacitation due to accident or illness. It helps replace a portion of lost income when the insured is unable to work. It is subject to certain conditions which will be discussed later on. It is also known as Permanent Health Insurance because the insurer cannot cancel the policy, no matter how many times the insured makes a claim. The policy will continue to pay out until he returns to work, dies, when the benefit period ends or when the policy ends, whichever happens first.
paragraphs.textJP: Disability Income Insurance is an income-protection in the event of incapacitation due to accident or illness. It helps replace a portion of lost income when the 被保険者 is unable to work. It is subject to certain conditions which will be discussed later on. It is also known as 恒久健康保険 because the 保険者 cannot cancel the policy, no matter how many times the 被保険者 makes a 保険金請求. The policy will continue to pay out until he returns to work, dies, when the 給付 period ends or when the policy ends, whichever happens first.

sections.title: Disability Income Insurance & Total And Permanent Disability

paragraphs.text: While both Disability Income Insurance and Total and Permanent Disability (TPD) Benefits provide the insured with a cash benefit on disability arising from an accident or illness, there are distinct differences between the two benefits.
paragraphs.textJP: While both Disability Income Insurance and Total and Permanent Disability (TPD) 給付s provide the 被保険者 with a cash 給付 on disability arising from an accident or illness, there are distinct differences between the two 給付s.

paragraphs.text: The main difference between Disability Income Insurance and Total and Permanent Disability Benefit lies in the purpose of the insurance and the definition of “disability”. Disability Income Insurance provides an income replacement if the insured cannot work. On the other hand, TPD Benefit serves to accelerate the death benefit payable under a Life Insurance policy, to alleviate the financial burden or hardship of the insured and his dependants. TPD Benefit is payable in one lump sum whereas, Disability Income Insurance benefits are payable on a monthly basis for up to an agreed benefit period. The differences are shown in Table 4.1. Table 4.1: Differences Between Disability Income Insurance & Total And Permanent Disability Benefit Disability Income Insurance Total And Permanent Disability Benefit Disability means the reduced/limited capacity at which the insured can fulfil the duties of his occupation. Disability is defined to be total and permanent, such as loss of limbs or loss of eyesight. It can be purchased as a standalone policy or as a rider. It is embedded into the Life Insurance policies or as a rider. Benefit is payable monthly Benefit is payable in one lump sum Maximum monthly benefit is mostly up to 75% of salary. Sum assured is not pegged to salary. Escalation benefit is available. No escalation of benefit. Partial disability benefit is available. No partial disability benefit Choice of deferred/elimination period is available (e.g. one month, three months, six months, etc.). No deferred period as specified (but usually there is a 6-month waiting period requirement as proof of permanent disability). Available only to someone with earned income/salary. No restriction on non-working people, e.g. children and housewives, as it is bundled with the death benefit under a Life Insurance policy.
paragraphs.textJP: The main difference between Disability Income Insurance and Total and Permanent Disability 給付 lies in the purpose of the insurance and the definition of “disability”. Disability Income Insurance provides an 所得代替 if the 被保険者 cannot work. On the other hand, TPD 給付 serves to accelerate the death 給付 payable under a Life Insurance policy, to alleviate the financial burden or hardship of the 被保険者 and his dependants. TPD 給付 is payable in one lump sum whereas, Disability Income Insurance 給付s are payable on a monthly basis for up to an agreed 給付 period. The differences are shown in Table 4.1. Table 4.1: Differences Between Disability Income Insurance & Total And Permanent Disability 給付 Disability Income Insurance Total And Permanent Disability 給付 Disability means the reduced/limited capacity at which the 被保険者 can fulfil the duties of his occupation. Disability is defined to be total and permanent, such as loss of limbs or loss of eyesight. It can be pur地域医療支援スキームed as a standalone policy or as a 特約. It is embedded into the Life Insurance policies or as a 特約. 給付 is payable monthly 給付 is payable in one lump sum Maximum monthly 給付 is mostly up to 75% of salary. 保険金額 is not pegged to salary. Escalation 給付 is available. No escalation of 給付. Partial disability 給付 is available. No partial disability 給付 Choice of deferred/免責期間 is available (e.g. one month, three months, six months, etc.). No 据置期間 as specified (but usually there is a 6-month 待機期間 requirement as proof of permanent disability). Available only to someone with earned income/salary. No restriction on non-working people, e.g. children and housewives, as it is bundled with the death 給付 under a Life Insurance policy.

sections.title: Computation Of Disability Income Insurance Benefit

paragraphs.text: Disability Income Insurance policy pays a monthly income to the insured upon his disability. Let us look at how it actually works, using the case of an employed individual. See Example 4.1. Example 4.1: Example Of Computation Of Disability Income Insurance Benefit Tommy Tang, aged 34 years, is an assistant accountant in a finance company. His monthly income is S$6,000. He wishes to purchase a Disability Income Insurance policy to provide maximum coverage. However, the maximum amount of monthly benefit that he can purchase cannot be 100%. Why? Insurers will allow the benefit amount to cover only up to 75% of the insured’s average income over the past 12 months immediately prior to his disability. A ceiling on the income to be covered may also be imposed and a self-employed person may be granted a lower benefit, The purpose of replacing only a certain percentage of the insured’s income is to ensure that the insured will not be better off financially from the benefit which he receives under his Disability Income Insurance policy, so that he will have the incentive to go back to work. Details of his policy are as follows: Male, Age Next Birthday 34 years old Monthly Income S$6,000 Monthly Benefit S$4,500 (75% of his monthly income being the maximum amount as allowed by Alpha Insurance Company and assuming that he has no other disability benefits). Escalation Benefit 5% per year Benefit Period Up to the age of 65 years next birthday Deferred/Elimination Period 6 months Monthly Premium S$125.50
paragraphs.textJP: Disability Income Insurance policy pays a monthly income to the 被保険者 upon his disability. Let us look at how it actually works, using the case of an employed individual. See Example 4.1. Example 4.1: Example Of Computation Of Disability Income Insurance 給付 Tommy Tang, aged 34 years, is an assistant accountant in a finance company. His monthly income is S$6,000. He wishes to pur地域医療支援スキームe a Disability Income Insurance policy to provide maximum 補償. However, the maximum amount of monthly 給付 that he can pur地域医療支援スキームe cannot be 100%. Why? 保険者s will allow the 給付 amount to cover only up to 75% of the 被保険者’s average income over the past 12 months immediately prior to his disability. A ceiling on the income to be covered may also be imposed and a self-employed person may be granted a lower 給付, The purpose of replacing only a certain percentage of the 被保険者’s income is to ensure that the 被保険者 will not be better off financially from the 給付 which he receives under his Disability Income Insurance policy, so that he will have the incentive to go back to work. Details of his policy are as follows: Male, Age Next Birthday 34 years old Monthly Income S$6,000 Monthly 給付 S$4,500 (75% of his monthly income being the maximum amount as allowed by Alpha Insurance Company and assuming that he has no other disability 給付s). Escalation 給付 5% per year 給付 Period Up to the age of 65 years next birthday Deferred/免責期間 6 months Monthly 保険料 S$125.50

paragraphs.text: If Tommy becomes disabled (according to the definition of “disability” of the policy) and is out of work, he will receive the benefit amount every month after the Deferred/Elimination Period, as long as he is unable to work. Assuming he is disabled throughout the Benefit Period (30 years) (Benefit period 65 years – Age next birthday 35 years), he would receive a total of S$3,587,695 (S$4,500 x 12 x 66.43881), although he would have paid only five years of premiums amounting to S$7,530 (S$125.50 x 60). Premiums are waived upon disability.
paragraphs.textJP: If Tommy becomes disabled (according to the definition of “disability” of the policy) and is out of work, he will receive the 給付 amount every month after the Deferred/免責期間, as long as he is unable to work. Assuming he is disabled throughout the 給付 Period (30 years) (給付 period 65 years – Age next birthday 35 years), he would receive a total of S$3,587,695 (S$4,500 x 12 x 66.43881), although he would have paid only five years of 保険料s amounting to S$7,530 (S$125.50 x 60). 保険料s are waived upon disability.

paragraphs.text: Let us look at each of the definitions in turn.
paragraphs.textJP: Let us look at each of the definitions in turn.

paragraphs.text: Considering the insured’s occupation, total disability is defined as: “the insured’s inability to perform the material duties of his own occupation”
paragraphs.textJP: Considering the 被保険者’s occupation, total disability is defined as: “the 被保険者’s inability to perform the material duties of his 自己職業基準”

paragraphs.text: This refers to the insured’s own occupation at the time that the disability begins. For example, Penny, a pianist, had an accident which caused the severing of three fingers on her left hand, which, in turn, resulted in her inability to work as a pianist. Thus, she had met the above definition of “disability” and would be able to claim under her Disability Income Insurance policy.
paragraphs.textJP: This refers to the 被保険者’s 自己職業基準 at the time that the disability begins. For example, Penny, a pianist, had an accident which caused the severing of three fingers on her left hand, which, in turn, resulted in her inability to work as a pianist. Thus, she had met the above definition of “disability” and would be able to 保険金請求 under her Disability Income Insurance policy.

paragraphs.text: The policy will state the insured’s occupation, and the insurer has to be notified of any occupation change while the policy is in force. Some insurers specify that the insured must either be unable to work in the pre-disabilty occupation or any other occupation.
paragraphs.textJP: The policy will state the 被保険者’s occupation, and the 保険者 has to be notified of 全職業基準 change while the policy is in force. Some 保険者s specify that the 被保険者 must either be unable to work in the pre-disabilty occupation or any other occupation.

paragraphs.text: Here “Total Disability” is defined as: “the insured’s inability to perform any gainful occupation or a similar occupation for which the insured is reasonably suited by reason of education, training or experience”
paragraphs.textJP: Here “Total Disability” is defined as: “the 被保険者’s inability to perform any gainful occupation or a similar occupation for which the 被保険者 is reasonably suited by reason of education, training or experience”

paragraphs.text: This definition is more stringent than ”Own Occupation” as it includes “a similar occupation”. Thus, if an insured manages to find a job which is similar in nature to the previous occupation after his recovery, he will not be able to meet the definition of “Total Disability”.
paragraphs.textJP: This definition is more stringent than ”自己職業基準” as it includes “a similar occupation”. Thus, if an 被保険者 manages to find a job which is similar in nature to the previous occupation after his recovery, he will not be able to meet the definition of “Total Disability”.

paragraphs.text: This is a more restrictive definition of Total Disability. It means: “the insured’s inability to perform any occupation”
paragraphs.textJP: This is a more restrictive definition of Total Disability. It means: “the 被保険者’s inability to perform 全職業基準”

paragraphs.text: Consider Penny, the pianist, whose fingers were severed. Under the previous definition, she was totally disabled because she was unable to perform the duties of her own occupation. However, suppose that she is able to teach, i.e. prepare students for the piano theory examination. Under “Any Occupation” definition, she will no longer be considered as being totally disabled.
paragraphs.textJP: Consider Penny, the pianist, whose fingers were severed. Under the previous definition, she was totally disabled because she was unable to perform the duties of her 自己職業基準. However, suppose that she is able to teach, i.e. prepare students for the piano theory examination. Under “全職業基準” definition, she will no longer be considered as being totally disabled.

paragraphs.text: The wording used may vary from insurer to insurer, and there are many hybrids in the insurance market, which may not be easy to classify. Therefore, it is important that you find out the exact definition as used by your insurer and be able to explain it clearly to your prospective clients.
paragraphs.textJP: The wording used may vary from 保険者 to 保険者, and there are many hybrids in the insurance market, which may not be easy to classify. Therefore, it is important that you find out the exact definition as used by your 保険者 and be able to explain it clearly to your prospective clients.

paragraphs.text: This definition follows the Long-Term Care Insurance definition where the insured is unable to perform at least three of the six Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) – washing, dressing, feeding, toileting, mobility, and transferring.
paragraphs.textJP: This definition follows the Long-Term Care Insurance definition where the 被保険者 is unable to perform at least three of the six 日常生活動作 – washing, dressing, feeding, toileting, mobility, and transferring.

paragraphs.text: The insurer uses this definition when the insured’s disability occurred during a non-working period (a period of time in-between jobs, where the insured is still covered under the plan). When a disability occurs during the non-working period, the benefit amount will not be based on the last drawn salary, but will be a smaller pre-determined amount as stated in the policy.
paragraphs.textJP: The 保険者 uses this definition when the 被保険者’s disability occurred during a non-working period (a period of time in-between jobs, where the 被保険者 is still covered under the plan). When a disability occurs during the non-working period, the 給付 amount will not be based on the last drawn salary, but will be a smaller pre-determined amount as stated in the policy.

paragraphs.text: This is when the insured recovers from a total disability and can start to do some duties of his occupation, but at a salary which is at least 25% lower than the pre-disability salary. For example, Walter was paid S$2,500 a month as a warehouse worker. His work involved moving materials both with a mechanical forklift and by hand.
paragraphs.textJP: This is when the 被保険者 recovers from a total disability and can start to do some duties of his occupation, but at a salary which is at least 25% lower than the pre-disability salary. For example, Walter was paid S$2,500 a month as a warehouse worker. His work involved moving materials both with a mechanical forklift and by hand.

paragraphs.text: After an accident, Walter’s spine was injured resulting in him having movement difficulties. Since then, he could operate only the forklift, but was not able to move the materials using his hands. As such, he was given a lower pay of S$1,800 (S$1,800 ÷ S$2,500 x 100 = 72% i.e. 28% less than his original pay). In this case, Walter was considered to be partially disabled.
paragraphs.textJP: After an accident, Walter’s spine was injured resulting in him having movement difficulties. Since then, he could operate only the forklift, but was not able to move the materials using his hands. As such, he was given a lower pay of S$1,800 (S$1,800 ÷ S$2,500 x 100 = 72% i.e. 28% less than his original pay). In this case, Walter was considered to be partially disabled.

paragraphs.text: Partial Disability benefit may be payable to the insured who works in other occupation (not related to his own occupation) on a part-time or full-time basis, and which pays him a salary of 75% (or less) of his Pre-disability Earnings.
paragraphs.textJP: Partial Disability 給付 may be payable to the 被保険者 who works in other occupation (not related to his 自己職業基準) on a part-time or full-time basis, and which pays him a salary of 75% (or less) of his Pre-disability Earnings.

paragraphs.text: If the insured who has been receiving the disability benefits returns to work but suffers a relapse from the same cause within a specified period of time (usually 180 days), most insurers will waive the deferred/elimination period, and the benefit payments will re-commence immediately.
paragraphs.textJP: If the 被保険者 who has been receiving the disability 給付s returns to work but suffers a relapse from the same cause within a specified period of time (usually 180 days), most 保険者s will waive the deferred/免責期間, and the 給付 payments will re-commence immediately.

paragraphs.text: In such a case, the claim will be treated as a continuation of the earlier claim for the purpose of determining the duration of the benefit payment period. Income Before Disability Income = S$2,500 After disability, income was reduced to S$1,800 (being 72% of pre-disability income).
paragraphs.textJP: In such a case, the 保険金請求 will be treated as a continuation of the earlier 保険金請求 for the purpose of determining the duration of the 給付 payment period. Income Before Disability Income = S$2,500 After disability, income was reduced to S$1,800 (being 72% of pre-disability income).

paragraphs.text: Benefit period is the maximum period for which disability benefits are payable to the insured for any one episode of disability. It can be short term (e.g. five years) or long term (e.g. up to age of 65 years). The shorter the benefit term, the lower will be the premiums.
paragraphs.textJP: 給付 period is the maximum period for which disability 給付s are payable to the 被保険者 for any one episode of disability. It can be short term (e.g. five years) or long term (e.g. up to age of 65 years). The shorter the 給付 term, the lower will be the 保険料s.

paragraphs.text: A Disability Income Insurance policy will start paying its benefits after the insured has been disabled for a specified period known as the deferred, elimination or pre-benefit period. The condition for a deferred period eliminates costly claims for disabilities which are only short term. Insurers may offer to the insured different deferred/elimination periods (e.g. 45, 90 or 180 days), or by number of months (e.g. three months).
paragraphs.textJP: A Disability Income Insurance policy will start paying its 給付s after the 被保険者 has been disabled for a specified period known as the deferred, elimination or pre-給付 period. The condition for a 据置期間 eliminates costly 保険金請求s for disabilities which are only short term. 保険者s may offer to the 被保険者 different deferred/免責期間s (e.g. 45, 90 or 180 days), or by number of months (e.g. three months).

paragraphs.text: Insurers offer these various deferred/elimination periods to meet the different individual’s specific needs. For example, a self-employed person may need a short deferred period, as his income may be immediately affected by his inability to work. An employed person, on the other hand, will usually continue to receive his salary for some specified period (e.g. three or six months). As such, you should advise your prospective client to find out from his employing company or his employment contract to see how long his company will continue to pay him his salary in the event that he is disabled, so that he can select the right deferred period. This will help him to save on the premium, as the longer the deferred period, the cheaper will be the premium, since the duration and frequency of claims are thereby reduced.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者s offer these various deferred/免責期間s to meet the different individual’s specific needs. For example, a self-employed person may need a short 据置期間, as his income may be immediately affected by his inability to work. An employed person, on the other hand, will usually continue to receive his salary for some specified period (e.g. three or six months). As such, you should advise your prospective client to find out from his employing company or his employment contract to see how long his company will continue to pay him his salary in the event that he is disabled, so that he can select the right 据置期間. This will help him to save on the 保険料, as the longer the 据置期間, the cheaper will be the 保険料, since the duration and frequency of 保険金請求s are thereby reduced.

paragraphs.text: You can also advise prospective clients with financial constraints to purchase a policy with a longer deferred period, where the premium is affordable to them, instead of not having the cover at all. = Longer Benefit Premium Period = Shorter Benefit Period = Short Deferred Premium Period = Long Deferred Period Premium Premium
paragraphs.textJP: You can also advise prospective clients with financial constraints to pur地域医療支援スキームe a policy with a longer 据置期間, where the 保険料 is affordable to them, instead of not having the cover at all. = Longer 給付 保険料 Period = Shorter 給付 Period = Short Deferred 保険料 Period = Long 据置期間 保険料 保険料

sections.title: Benefits Offered Under Disability Income Insurance

subsections.label: A
subsections.title: Eligibility Criteria For Payment Of Disability Income Insurance Benefit

paragraphs.text: To be eligible for the disability income benefit payment, the insured MUST: (a) keep the policy in force; (b) be employed or in in-between jobs when they become disabled; (c) still be disabled after the deferred/elimination period; (d) meet the definition of total or partial disability as stated in the policy; (e) not have reached the expiry age as stated in the policy; (f) not have resided outside Singapore for more than a certain period of time (usually not more than six months); and (g) not have other sources of income (e.g. payment from Work Injury Compensation Insurance), which when totalled up, is more than the benefits due to him under his Disability Income Insurance policy.
paragraphs.textJP: To be eligible for the disability income 給付 payment, the 被保険者 MUST: (a) keep the policy in force; (b) be employed or in in-between jobs when they become disabled; (c) still be disabled after the deferred/免責期間; (d) meet the definition of total or partial disability as stated in the policy; (e) not have reached the expiry age as stated in the policy; (f) not have resided outside Singapore for more than a certain period of time (usually not more than six months); and (g) not have other sources of income (e.g. payment from 労災補償 Insurance), which when totalled up, is more than the 給付s due to him under his Disability Income Insurance policy.

paragraphs.text: The main benefit offered by Disability Income Insurance is the monthly cash benefit to replace up to 75% of lost income when the insured becomes totally disabled through an accident or illness.
paragraphs.textJP: The main 給付 offered by Disability Income Insurance is the monthly cash 給付 to replace up to 75% of lost income when the 被保険者 becomes totally disabled through an accident or illness.

paragraphs.text: The benefit will be paid regardless of whether the insured is hospitalised. However, his disability has to be certified by a registered medical practitioner2 in order for him to make a claim under his policy.
paragraphs.textJP: The 給付 will be paid regardless of whether the 被保険者 is hospitalised. However, his disability has to be certified by a registered medical practitioner2 in order for him to make a 保険金請求 under his policy.

paragraphs.text: If the insured’s total disability continues after the expiry of the deferred period, the insurer will pay the monthly benefit at the end of each month. For example, if the policy is issued for a monthly benefit of S$1,000 (as stated in the policy schedule), this will be the amount that the insured will get for that month. If the period of disability is less than one month, the insurer will usually pay a pro-rata amount based on a daily rate of one-thirtieth (1/30) of the disability benefit.
paragraphs.textJP: If the 被保険者’s total disability continues after the expiry of the 据置期間, the 保険者 will pay the monthly 給付 at the end of each month. For example, if the policy is issued for a monthly 給付 of S$1,000 (as stated in the policy schedule), this will be the amount that the 被保険者 will get for that month. If the period of disability is less than one month, the 保険者 will usually pay a pro-rata amount based on a daily rate of one-thirtieth (1/30) of the disability 給付.

paragraphs.text: The benefit ceases when the insured recovers and returns to work, either fulltime or part-time, and even to a different occupation. 2 A registered medical practitioner is a doctor who is qualified by degree in western medicine and is legally licensed to practice medicine and surgery in the geographical area of his country, but does not refer to a medical practitioner who is the insured himself, as well as his spouse, child or parent of the insured. Income Insurers usually insure up to 75% of income
paragraphs.textJP: The 給付 ceases when the 被保険者 recovers and returns to work, either fulltime or part-time, and even to a different occupation. 2 A registered medical practitioner is a doctor who is qualified by degree in western medicine and is legally licensed to practice medicine and surgery in the geographical area of his country, but does not refer to a medical practitioner who is the 被保険者 himself, as well as his spouse, child or parent of the 被保険者. Income 保険者s usually insure up to 75% of income

paragraphs.text: Partial Disability Benefit is also known as “Rehabilitation Benefit”. It provides a reduced benefit to the insured if he is able to return to work at a reduced income of 75% of his Pre-disability Earnings, or lower. This applies even though he has not fully recovered. The reduced benefit is computed based on the following formula: Pre-disability Earnings − Present Earnings X Total Disability Benefit Pre-disability Earnings
paragraphs.textJP: Partial Disability 給付 is also known as “Rehabilitation 給付”. It provides a reduced 給付 to the 被保険者 if he is able to return to work at a reduced income of 75% of his Pre-disability Earnings, or lower. This applies even though he has not fully recovered. The reduced 給付 is computed based on the following formula: Pre-disability Earnings − Present Earnings X Total Disability 給付 Pre-disability Earnings

paragraphs.text: Example 4.2 shows how the partial disability benefit is arrived at. Example 4.2: Computation Of Partial Disability Benefit Larry, a lecturer who earns S$8,000 a month suffered brain injury in a car accident. This caused him to be totally disabled for two years during which he received S$6,000 (75% of his Pre-disability Earnings) a month from his Disability Income Insurance policy. In the third year, he managed to find a job in a printing firm that paid him S$1,000 a month. In view of this, the insurer reduced the monthly benefit to S$5,250, calculated as follows: S$(8,000 – 1,000) X S$6,000 = S$5,250 S$8,000 Note: You can see that the total monthly benefit and reduced income after the partial disability is S$6,250 (S$5,250 + S$1,000). This is still lower than the original income of S$8,000 a month before the disability.
paragraphs.textJP: Example 4.2 shows how the partial disability 給付 is arrived at. Example 4.2: Computation Of Partial Disability 給付 Larry, a lecturer who earns S$8,000 a month suffered brain injury in a car accident. This caused him to be totally disabled for two years during which he received S$6,000 (75% of his Pre-disability Earnings) a month from his Disability Income Insurance policy. In the third year, he managed to find a job in a printing firm that paid him S$1,000 a month. In view of this, the 保険者 reduced the monthly 給付 to S$5,250, calculated as follows: S$(8,000 – 1,000) X S$6,000 = S$5,250 S$8,000 Note: You can see that the total monthly 給付 and reduced income after the partial disability is S$6,250 (S$5,250 + S$1,000). This is still lower than the original income of S$8,000 a month before the disability.

paragraphs.text: The insurer will pay a pro-rata amount, based on a daily rate of one-thirtieth (1/30) of the partial disability benefit if the period of the partial disability is less than a month.
paragraphs.textJP: The 保険者 will pay a pro-rata amount, based on a daily rate of one-thirtieth (1/30) of the partial disability 給付 if the period of the partial disability is less than a month.

paragraphs.text:
Insurers encourage participation in rehabilitation programme by reimbursing an insured for the rehabilitation costs incurred. The benefit is limited to a per disability basis and may include items such as:
▪ training courses;
▪ medical aids (e.g. wheelchair, walking frame, crutches); and
▪ workplace modifications.
paragraphs.textJP:
保険者s encourage participation in rehabilitation programme by reimbursing an 被保険者 for the rehabilitation costs incurred. The 給付 is limited to a per disability basis and may include items such as:
▪ training courses;
▪ medical aids (e.g. wheelchair, walking frame, crutches); and
▪ workplace modifications.

paragraphs.text: The insured must first get the insurer’s approval in writing, before going ahead with any rehabilitation programmes, etc. The rehabilitation benefit may be payable in addition to the monthly disability benefit (e.g. up to three times the monthly benefit).
paragraphs.textJP: The 被保険者 must first get the 保険者’s approval in writing, before going ahead with any rehabilitation programmes, etc. The rehabilitation 給付 may be payable in addition to the monthly disability 給付 (e.g. up to three times the monthly 給付).

paragraphs.text: Escalation benefit is a benefit offered by insurers as a hedge against inflation. With an additional premium, the insured can opt for a 3% or 5% escalation benefit per annum.
paragraphs.textJP: Escalation 給付 is a 給付 offered by 保険者s as a hedge against inflation. With an additional 保険料, the 被保険者 can opt for a 3% or 5% escalation 給付 per annum.

paragraphs.text: This benefit is useful for long-term disabilities. Example 4.3 illustrates how the escalation benefit works. Example 4.3: Illustration Of The Working Of The Escalation Benefit Eddy, an engineer, became disabled on 30 June 2023. He was insured for S$6,000 (75% of his monthly salary of S$8,000) and had opted for a 3% per annum escalation benefit and a 6-month deferred period. The events following his disablement were as follows: Date Event 1-1-2023 Insurer made the first monthly payment of S$6,000 1-2-2023 Insurer made the second monthly payment of S$6,000 1-1-2024 Insurer paid S$6,180 [(S$6,000 + S$6,000 x 3% (escalation benefit)] 1-2-2024 Eddy had since found a job as a clerk which paid him S$2,000 a month.
