An ageing population, coupled with increased longevity, will lead to higher national healthcare and long-term care expenditure, particularly for the elderly. With fewer children to support parents and grandparents, and the increasing numbers of elderly people living alone, more paid help, such as domestic workers, home-based caregivers and nurses, will also be needed. This will be a growing concern for the Government, as it will have to ensure that seniors have adequate protection against the cost of long-term care when such needs arise.

paragraphs.text: In this chapter, we will look at how Long-Term Care Insurance can help provide funds to supplement the cost of financing an individual’s long-term care needs.
paragraphs.textJP: In this chapter, we will look at how Long-Term Care Insurance can help provide funds to supplement the cost of financing an individual’s long-term care needs.

paragraphs.text: LTC Insurance is designed to meet the costs of care for a person who, because of an accident, illness, frailty or a combination of these, is unable to function independently. Instead, he must depend on others to help him with the most basic ADLs.
paragraphs.textJP: 介護保険 is designed to meet the costs of care for a person who, because of an accident, illness, frailty or a combination of these, is unable to function independently. Instead, he must depend on others to help him with the most basic ADLs.

paragraphs.text: There are two key advantages of buying LTC, while clients are still young and healthy, with no existing medical disabilities.  Firstly, the product will cost less as the premiums will rise considerably with age.  Secondly, buying at a younger age also means that there is less risk of being rejected for cover owing to existing disabilities or medical history.
paragraphs.textJP: There are two key advantages of buying LTC, while clients are still young and healthy, with no existing medical disabilities.  Firstly, the product will cost less as the 保険料s will rise considerably with age.  Secondly, buying at a younger age also means that there is less risk of being rejected for cover owing to existing disabilities or medical history.

paragraphs.text: LTC Insurance covers care generally excluded by other Medical Insurance. It will pay in addition to the insured’s other insurance policies e.g. MediShield Life, Hospital Income and Critical Illness Insurance policy.
paragraphs.textJP: 介護保険 covers care generally excluded by other Medical Insurance. It will pay in addition to the 被保険者’s other insurance policies e.g. メディシールド・ライフ, Hospital Income and Critical Illness Insurance policy.

paragraphs.text: Two types of LTC policies are available and they are: (a) “Service-based” policy pays a benefit when an insured needs to use the service which is covered under the policy (e.g. home care or nursing care). Benefits can be paid in two ways:  Reimbursement of the cost incurred in using one of the services covered under the policy, up to the covered amount; or  a pre-agreed insured amount regardless of the actual cost incurred for the services. (b) “Disability-based” policy pays a benefit when an insured:  Is diagnosed to be suffering from advanced dementia (including Alzheimer’s disease;  is assessed to be suffering significant limitations in ADLs; or  is evaluated to have a deterioration or loss of intellectual capacity resulting in a significant reduction in mental and social functioning, thus the need for continuous supervision.
paragraphs.textJP: Two types of LTC policies are available and they are: (a) “Service-based” policy pays a 給付 when an 被保険者 needs to use the service which is covered under the policy (e.g. home care or nursing care). 給付s can be paid in two ways:  実費精算・償還払い of the cost incurred in using one of the services covered under the policy, up to the covered amount; or  a pre-agreed 被保険者 amount regardless of the actual cost incurred for the services. (b) “Disability-based” policy pays a 給付 when an 被保険者:  Is diagnosed to be suffering from advanced dementia (including Alzheimer’s disease;  is assessed to be suffering significant limitations in ADLs; or  is evaluated to have a deterioration or loss of intellectual capacity resulting in a significant reduction in mental and social functioning, thus the need for continuous supervision.

paragraphs.text: Benefits are paid monthly (a) to the insured upon being certified by an approved and registered medical practitioner to be unable to perform the specified minimum number of defined ADLs after a prescribed period. Some insurers pay 100% of the LTC monthly benefit if the insured is unable to perform at least the minimum number of ADLs, whereas others pay on a graduated scale. For example, if the insured cannot perform two of the six ADLs, only 50% of the LTC monthly benefit is payable, but if the insured cannot perform four out of the six ADLs, 100% of the LTC monthly benefit will then be payable.
paragraphs.textJP: 給付s are paid monthly (a) to the 被保険者 upon being certified by an approved and registered medical practitioner to be unable to perform the specified minimum number of defined ADLs after a prescribed period. Some 保険者s pay 100% of the LTC monthly 給付 if the 被保険者 is unable to perform at least the minimum number of ADLs, whereas others pay on a graduated scale. For example, if the 被保険者 cannot perform two of the six ADLs, only 50% of the LTC monthly 給付 is payable, but if the 被保険者 cannot perform four out of the six ADLs, 100% of the LTC monthly 給付 will then be payable.

subsections.label: B
subsections.title: Eligibility Criteria For Payment Of LTC Benefits

paragraphs.text: The insured does not need not be hospitalised to be eligible for the LTC benefits. He can use the benefits received under his LTC to cover the costs of engaging a domestic helper (maid), staying in a nursing home, or to participating in an assisted-living facility or home-care programme.
paragraphs.textJP: The 被保険者 does not need not be hospitalised to be eligible for the LTC 給付s. He can use the 給付s received under his LTC to cover the costs of engaging a domestic helper (maid), staying in a nursing home, or to participating in an assisted-living facility or home-care programme.

paragraphs.text: However, he must meet certain other criteria, before he can be eligible to claim for the LTC benefits.
paragraphs.textJP: However, he must meet certain other criteria, before he can be eligible to 保険金請求 for the LTC 給付s.

subsections.label: B1
subsections.title: Inability To Perform Activities Of Daily Living (ADLs) Or Advanced Dementia

paragraphs.text: Inability To Perform ADLs (a) One of the key criteria for paying the benefits in LTC policies is the insured’s inability to perform specific essential actions required for an individual to live independently without support. These essential actions are known as “Activities of Daily Living” (ADLs). (b) The definitions of these ADLs are important. Some policies pay the benefits for not being able to perform one out of six ADLs while others require three out of six ADLs to be affected for benefits . Premium for the former is more expensive than the latter. (c) The ADLs are defined as follows:  Washing: the ability to wash in the bath or shower (including getting into and out of the bath or shower) or wash by other means.  Dressing: the ability to put on, or take off, secure and unfasten all garments and, as appropriate, any braces, artificial limbs, or other surgical or medical appliances.  Feeding: the ability to feed oneself after the food has been prepared and made available.  Toileting: the ability to use the lavatory or manage bowel and bladder function through the use of protective undergarments (e.g. diapers) or surgical appliances (e.g. urinary catheters) if appropriate.  Mobility: the ability to move indoors from room to room on level surfaces.  Transferring: the ability to move from a bed to an upright chair or wheelchair, and vice versa. Washing Dressing Feeding Toileting Mobility Transferring (d) Failure to perform any of the above ADLs means that the insured will be unable to perform the task, even with the aid of assistive devices (e.g. wheelchair, clutches) on his own and always require the assistance of another person throughout the entire activity. Washing Dressing Feeding Mobility Toileting Transferring Activities of Daily Living (ADLs)
paragraphs.textJP: Inability To Perform ADLs (a) One of the key criteria for paying the 給付s in LTC policies is the 被保険者’s inability to perform specific essential actions required for an individual to live independently without support. These essential actions are known as “Activities of Daily Living” (ADLs). (b) The definitions of these ADLs are important. Some policies pay the 給付s for not being able to perform one out of six ADLs while others require three out of six ADLs to be affected for 給付s . 保険料 for the former is more expensive than the latter. (c) The ADLs are defined as follows:  Washing: the ability to wash in the bath or shower (including getting into and out of the bath or shower) or wash by other means.  Dressing: the ability to put on, or take off, secure and unfasten all garments and, as appropriate, any braces, artificial limbs, or other surgical or medical appliances.  Feeding: the ability to feed oneself after the food has been prepared and made available.  Toileting: the ability to use the lavatory or manage bowel and bladder function through the use of protective undergarments (e.g. diapers) or surgical appliances (e.g. urinary catheters) if appropriate.  Mobility: the ability to move indoors from room to room on level surfaces.  Transferring: the ability to move from a bed to an upright chair or wheelchair, and vice versa. Washing Dressing Feeding Toileting Mobility Transferring (d) Failure to perform any of the above ADLs means that the 被保険者 will be unable to perform the task, even with the aid of assistive devices (e.g. wheelchair, clutches) on his own and always require the assistance of another person throughout the entire activity. Washing Dressing Feeding Mobility Toileting Transferring 日常生活動作

paragraphs.text: Advanced Dementia (a) Advanced dementia means a condition where the insured experiences a deterioration or loss of intellectual capacity or abnormal behaviour, arising from Alzheimer’s disease or irreversible organic disorders excluding neurosis and psychiatric illness. This condition results in significant reduction in mental and social functioning requiring the continual supervision of the insured to prevent the insured from harming others or himself. Advanced dementia is confirmed by clinical assessments accepted standardised questionnaires or tests. (b) The dementia must arise from an organic reason, and that the insured requires continual supervision.
paragraphs.textJP: Advanced Dementia (a) Advanced dementia means a condition where the 被保険者 experiences a deterioration or loss of intellectual capacity or abnormal behaviour, arising from Alzheimer’s disease or irreversible organic disorders excluding neurosis and psychiatric illness. This condition results in significant reduction in mental and social functioning requiring the continual supervision of the 被保険者 to prevent the 被保険者 from harming others or himself. Advanced dementia is confirmed by clinical assessments accepted standardised questionnaires or tests. (b) The dementia must arise from an organic reason, and that the 被保険者 requires continual supervision.

paragraphs.text: Besides the requirement of failure to perform the ADLs, the insured may also need to fulfil a deferred period requirement before being eligible to receive the LTC benefits. Most insurers have a deferred period of 90 days commencing from the first day of any continuous period of inability to perform the ADLs. Many seniors may suffer short periods of dependence after a stay in hospital for an illness or injury, and a deferred period will ensure that short-term claims are avoided and, therefore, reduce the premium.
paragraphs.textJP: Besides the requirement of failure to perform the ADLs, the 被保険者 may also need to fulfil a 据置期間 requirement before being eligible to receive the LTC 給付s. Most 保険者s have a 据置期間 of 90 days commencing from the first day of any continuous period of inability to perform the ADLs. Many seniors may suffer short periods of dependence after a stay in hospital for an illness or injury, and a 据置期間 will ensure that short-term 保険金請求s are avoided and, therefore, reduce the 保険料.

paragraphs.text: LTC policy would not pay if the claim arose from a pre-existing condition that was not disclosed in the proposal form. If disclosed, the insurer may accept the application at standard premium rates, or at sub-standard premium rates, i.e. may charge a higher premium, accept the application with exclusions, or reject the application. The decisions taken regarding applications and the rates of premiums application are largely dependent on the severity of the conditions of the proposer (also called the applicant or the intending insured).
paragraphs.textJP: LTC policy would not pay if the 保険金請求 arose from a 既存疾病 that was not disclosed in the 申込書. If disclosed, the 保険者 may accept the application at standard 保険料 rates, or at sub-standard 保険料 rates, i.e. may charge a higher 保険料, accept the application with 免責事由s, or reject the application. The decisions taken regarding applications and the rates of 保険料s application are largely dependent on the severity of the conditions of the proposer (also called the applicant or the intending 被保険者).

paragraphs.text: The purpose of the provision is to guard against people buying the coverage knowing that they have a condition that is likely to lead to a claim. This is also known as anti-selection against the insurer.
paragraphs.textJP: The purpose of the provision is to guard against people buying the 補償 knowing that they have a condition that is likely to lead to a 保険金請求. This is also known as 逆選択 against the 保険者.

paragraphs.text: The insurer may also impose other policy conditions for the insured to comply with, before they are eligible to claim the benefits.
paragraphs.textJP: The 保険者 may also impose other policy conditions for the 被保険者 to comply with, before they are eligible to 保険金請求 the 給付s.

sections.title: Other Benefits Provided Under Ltc Insurance

paragraphs.text: Insurers also usually include a death benefit which is payable to the insured’s beneficiaries upon the death of the insured. However, the quantum is small, ranging from an indicative amount of S$5,000 to six times the LTC monthly benefit for example. Upon payment of the death benefit, the cover ceases. Deferred Period usually 90 Days
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者s also usually include a death 給付 which is payable to the 被保険者’s beneficiaries upon the death of the 被保険者. However, the quantum is small, ranging from an indicative amount of S$5,000 to six times the LTC monthly 給付 for example. Upon payment of the death 給付, the cover ceases. 据置期間 usually 90 Days

paragraphs.text: In addition to LTC and death benefits, some insurers may also package it with other benefits, such as: (a) Extended Care Benefit – an extra specified sum, such as an extra monthly benefit, is payable every three or five years; and (b) Rehabilitation Benefit – a reduced benefit (e.g. 50% of the insured amount) when the insured makes partial recovery (such as if he is unable to do two instead of three ADLs). (c) Dependant care benefit – pays a % of the severe disability benefit for a specific period when the insured has a dependent child at the point of claim. (d) Waiver of premium benefit – premium will be waived after the deferment period when the insured qualifies for the severe disability benefit (inability to do ADLs) claim under the policy.
paragraphs.textJP: In addition to LTC and death 給付s, some 保険者s may also package it with other 給付s, such as: (a) Extended Care 給付 – an extra specified sum, such as an extra monthly 給付, is payable every three or five years; and (b) Rehabilitation 給付 – a reduced 給付 (e.g. 50% of the 被保険者 amount) when the 被保険者 makes partial recovery (such as if he is unable to do two instead of three ADLs). (c) Dependant care 給付 – pays a % of the 重度障害 給付 for a specific period when the 被保険者 has a dependent child at the point of 保険金請求. (d) Waiver of 保険料 給付 – 保険料 will be waived after the deferment period when the 被保険者 qualifies for the 重度障害 給付 (inability to do ADLs) 保険金請求 under the policy.

paragraphs.text: These benefits are subject to limits on the maximum sum that can be claimed, as well as limits on the benefit payment period. Otherwise, the product will become prohibitively expensive. The main purpose of LTC Insurance is not to fulfil the need of income protection, or for reimbursement of medical expenses, e.g. hospital and surgical bills. Such needs are better met by other policies, such as Disability Income Insurance and/or Hospital and Surgical Insurance.
paragraphs.textJP: These 給付s are subject to limits on the maximum sum that can be 保険金請求ed, as well as limits on the 給付 payment period. Otherwise, the product will become prohibitively expensive. The main purpose of 介護保険 is not to fulfil the need of income protection, or for 実費精算・償還払い of medical expenses, e.g. hospital and surgical bills. Such needs are better met by other policies, such as Disability Income Insurance and/or Hospital and Surgical Insurance.

paragraphs.text: For LTC benefits, the amount an insured can claim depends on the chosen plan. For example, it may be between S$300 and S$5,000 per month, provided he is unable to perform the specified number of ADLs, and meets the other policy conditions. Some insurers may limit the maximum benefit sum payable.
paragraphs.textJP: For LTC 給付s, the amount an 被保険者 can 保険金請求 depends on the chosen plan. For example, it may be between S$300 and S$5,000 per month, provided he is unable to perform the specified number of ADLs, and meets the other policy conditions. Some 保険者s may limit the maximum 給付 sum payable.

paragraphs.text: While most insured individuals prefer the policy benefits to be payable as long as they live, the premiums for such plans may be too expensive for them. Some insurers allow customers to choose the benefit payment term. This means that the benefits will be payable for the chosen period (e.g. 12 years of benefits). The premiums payable for a shorter benefit payment term will be cheaper and more affordable. During the benefit payment period, premiums payable are waived.
paragraphs.textJP: While most 被保険者 individuals prefer the policy 給付s to be payable as long as they live, the 保険料s for such plans may be too expensive for them. Some 保険者s allow customers to choose the 給付 payment term. This means that the 給付s will be payable for the chosen period (e.g. 12 years of 給付s). The 保険料s payable for a shorter 給付 payment term will be cheaper and more affordable. During the 給付 payment period, 保険料s payable are waived.

paragraphs.text: Notwithstanding the above, there are LTC insurance schemes (with affordable premiums) that offer lifetime cash pay-outs for as long as the insured is severely disabled. Such schemes include CareShield Life which we shall examine in a later part of this chapter. 7. ELDERSHIELD1
paragraphs.textJP: Notwithstanding the above, there are 介護保険 schemes (with affordable 保険料s) that offer lifetime cash pay-outs for as long as the 被保険者 is severely disabled. Such schemes include ケアシールド・ライフ which we shall examine in a later part of this chapter. 7. エルダーシールド1

paragraphs.text: ElderShield is a long-term care insurance scheme targeted at severe disability, especially during old age.
paragraphs.textJP: エルダーシールド is a long-term care insurance scheme targeted at 重度障害, especially during old age.

paragraphs.text: When it was introduced in 2002, ElderShield provided pay-outs of S$300 per month for up to five years upon severe disability. It was subsequently reviewed in 2007 to provide better benefits of S$400 per month for up to six years. The benefits received depends on the scheme the insured joined. Please note that the ElderShield scheme is no longer open for new applications.
paragraphs.textJP: When it was introduced in 2002, エルダーシールド provided pay-outs of S$300 per month for up to five years upon 重度障害. It was subsequently reviewed in 2007 to provide better 給付s of S$400 per month for up to six years. The 給付s received depends on the scheme the 被保険者 joined. Please note that the エルダーシールド scheme is no longer open for new applications.

paragraphs.text: ElderShield provides monthly cash payouts for up to 72 months to help pay out-of-pocket expenses for the care of those with severe disabilities.
paragraphs.textJP: エルダーシールド provides monthly cash payouts for up to 72 months to help pay out-of-pocket expenses for the care of those with severe disabilities.

paragraphs.text: Premiums are determined when the insured enters the scheme, and the premiums remain fixed. The insured must pay the premium each year until the policy anniversary after his 65th birthday or when the insured makes a successful claim.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険料s are determined when the 被保険者 enters the scheme, and the 保険料s remain fixed. The 被保険者 must pay the 保険料 each year until the policy anniversary after his 65th birthday or when the 被保険者 makes a successful 保険金請求.

paragraphs.text: From 1 November 2021, the Government has taken over the administration of ElderShield from private insurers (Singlife, Great Eastern Life Assurance Co Ltd, and Income Insurance Limited).
paragraphs.textJP: From 1 November 2021, the Government has taken over the administration of エルダーシールド from private 保険者s (Singlife, Great Eastern Life Assurance Co Ltd, and Income Insurance Limited).

paragraphs.text: Until 2019, all Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents with MediSave Accounts were automatically enrolled into ElderShield at the age of 40, unless they opted out.
paragraphs.textJP: Until 2019, all Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents with メディセーブ Accounts were automatically enrolled into エルダーシールド at the age of 40, unless they opted out.

paragraphs.text: Since 2020, auto-enrolments into ElderShield were discontinued. Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents born in 1980 and after were instead enrolled into CareShield Life at age 30. Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents born in 1970 to 1979, who were enrolled on ElderShield 400 and did not have any pre-existing disabilities, were also enrolled into CareShield Life and can choose to opt out by 31 December 2023. 1 https://www.cpf.gov.sg/member/healthcare-financing/eldershield
paragraphs.textJP: Since 2020, auto-enrolments into エルダーシールド were discontinued. Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents born in 1980 and after were instead enrolled into ケアシールド・ライフ at age 30. Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents born in 1970 to 1979, who were enrolled on エルダーシールド 400 and did not have any pre-existing disabilities, were also enrolled into ケアシールド・ライフ and can choose to opt out by 31 December 2023. 1 https://www.CPF（中央積立基金）.gov.sg/member/healthcare-financing/エルダーシールド

paragraphs.text: Those born on 30 September 1932 or before, or had a pre-existing disability as of 30 September 2002, were ineligible to enrol in ElderShield in 2002. Instead, they may be eligible for assistance under the Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (IDAPE) if they develop severe disabilities.
paragraphs.textJP: Those born on 30 September 1932 or before, or had a pre-existing disability as of 30 September 2002, were ineligible to enrol in エルダーシールド in 2002. Instead, they may be eligible for assistance under the Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (傷害・健康保険販売事業体) if they develop severe disabilities.

paragraphs.text: To learn more about IDAPE, please visit this website: https://www.aic.sg/financial-assistance/interim-disability-assistanceprogramme- elderly
paragraphs.textJP: To learn more about 傷害・健康保険販売事業体, please visit this website: https://www.aic.sg/financial-assistance/interim-disability-assistanceprogramme- elderly

paragraphs.text: The following steps should be performed in checking on one’s coverage: (a) Log in to “my CPF Digital Services” using one’s Singpass. (b) Hover over "my CPF" and click on "Healthcare" under "My dashboards". (c) Click on “Long-term care insurance” on the anchor links or scroll down to the “Long-term care insurance” section.
paragraphs.textJP: The following steps should be performed in checking on one’s 補償: (a) Log in to “my CPF（中央積立基金） Digital Services” using one’s Singpass. (b) Hover over "my CPF（中央積立基金）" and click on "Healthcare" under "My dashboards". (c) Click on “Long-term care insurance” on the anchor links or scroll down to the “Long-term care insurance” section.

paragraphs.text: If the person is not covered, no information on ElderShield will be reflected. To check one’s coverage, he or she should visit the CPF website at Central Provident Fund Board (CPFB).
paragraphs.textJP: If the person is not covered, no information on エルダーシールド will be reflected. To check one’s 補償, he or she should visit the CPF（中央積立基金） website at Central Provident Fund Board (CPF（中央積立基金）B).

paragraphs.text: ElderShield provides the following protection and benefits: (a) Lifetime coverage – The insured continues to be covered for life once he has completed paying all his premiums at age 65. (b) Up to 72 months of cash payouts – The insured stands to receive a monthly cash pay-out for up to five or six years, depending on the plan chosen. With ESH400, he will get S$400 per month for up to 72 months, and with ESH300, he will get S$300 per month for up to 60 months. (c) Fully payable with MediSave – The insured can use his MediSave to pay his ElderShield premiums. Family members can help the insured by paying from their own MediSave or topping up the insured’s account with cash. (d) Worldwide coverage – The insured will remain covered, have the ability to make claims, and receive payouts no matter where he lives in the world. Following the closure of CPF accounts for foreigners from 1 April 2024, the person who loses their SC/PR status will lose their ElderShield coverage.
paragraphs.textJP: エルダーシールド provides the following protection and 給付s: (a) Lifetime 補償 – The 被保険者 continues to be covered for life once he has completed paying all his 保険料s at age 65. (b) Up to 72 months of cash payouts – The 被保険者 stands to receive a monthly cash pay-out for up to five or six years, depending on the plan chosen. With ESH400, he will get S$400 per month for up to 72 months, and with ESH300, he will get S$300 per month for up to 60 months. (c) Fully payable with メディセーブ – The 被保険者 can use his メディセーブ to pay his エルダーシールド 保険料s. Family members can help the 被保険者 by paying from their own メディセーブ or topping up the 被保険者’s account with cash. (d) Worldwide 補償 – The 被保険者 will remain covered, have the ability to make 保険金請求s, and receive payouts no matter where he lives in the world. Following the closure of CPF（中央積立基金） accounts for foreigners from 1 April 2024, the person who loses their SC/PR status will lose their エルダーシールド 補償.

paragraphs.text: The premium payment for ElderShield is up to age 65, with a coverage for life. ElderShield premiums are based on the age at which the insured joins the scheme and are payable until the policy anniversary after his 65th birthday.
paragraphs.textJP: The 保険料 payment for エルダーシールド is up to age 65, with a 補償 for life. エルダーシールド 保険料s are based on the age at which the 被保険者 joins the scheme and are payable until the policy anniversary after his 65th birthday.

paragraphs.text: To learn more about ElderShield premiums, please visit this website: https://www.cpf.gov.sg/member/healthcare-financing/eldershield/eldershieldpremiums.
paragraphs.textJP: To learn more about エルダーシールド 保険料s, please visit this website: https://www.CPF（中央積立基金）.gov.sg/member/healthcare-financing/エルダーシールド/エルダーシールド保険料s.

subsections.label: B5
subsections.title: When The Insured Is Eligible to Claim

paragraphs.text: One can apply to receive ElderShield payouts if he is determined by an MOHaccredited assessor to be unable to carry out three of the six activities of daily living (ADL) listed below: (a) Washing – The ability to wash in the bath or shower (including getting into and out of the bath or shower) or wash by other means. (b) Dressing – The ability to put on, take off, secure, and unfasten all garments and, as appropriate, any braces, artificial limbs, or other surgical or medical appliances. (c) Feeding – The ability to feed oneself food after it has been prepared and made available. (d) Toileting – The ability to use the toilet or manage bowel and bladder function using protective undergarments, such as diapers or surgical appliances, if appropriate. (e) Mobility – The ability to move indoors from room to room on level surfaces. (f) Transferring – The ability to move from a bed to an upright chair or wheelchair, and vice versa.
paragraphs.textJP: One can apply to receive エルダーシールド payouts if he is determined by an MOH（保健省）accredited assessor to be unable to carry out three of the six activities of daily living (ADL) listed below: (a) Washing – The ability to wash in the bath or shower (including getting into and out of the bath or shower) or wash by other means. (b) Dressing – The ability to put on, take off, secure, and unfasten all garments and, as appropriate, any braces, artificial limbs, or other surgical or medical appliances. (c) Feeding – The ability to feed oneself food after it has been prepared and made available. (d) Toileting – The ability to use the toilet or manage bowel and bladder function using protective undergarments, such as diapers or surgical appliances, if appropriate. (e) Mobility – The ability to move indoors from room to room on level surfaces. (f) Transferring – The ability to move from a bed to an upright chair or wheelchair, and vice versa.

paragraphs.text: To make a claim, one should complete the claim assessment. First, the insured needs to arrange for a disability assessment which can be conducted by any MOH-accredited severe disability assessor. For a list of assessors, please visit this link: https://www.aic.sg/financialassistance/ Documents/Application%20Forms/IDAPE%20Assessor%20List.pdf
paragraphs.textJP: To make a 保険金請求, one should complete the 保険金請求 assessment. First, the 被保険者 needs to arrange for a disability assessment which can be conducted by any MOH（保健省）-accredited 重度障害 assessor. For a list of assessors, please visit this link: https://www.aic.sg/financialassistance/ Documents/Application%20Forms/傷害・健康保険販売事業体%20Assessor%20List.pdf

paragraphs.text: The assessment costs S$100 for a clinic assessment and S$250 for a house call, both of which are payable to the assessor at the time of assessment. The full fee will be reimbursed if the insured is assessed to be severely disabled and will be reimbursed with the first pay out.
paragraphs.textJP: The assessment costs S$100 for a clinic assessment and S$250 for a house call, both of which are payable to the assessor at the time of assessment. The full fee will be reimbursed if the 被保険者 is assessed to be severely disabled and will be reimbursed with the first pay out.

paragraphs.text: Next, the insured needs to log in to AIC’s e-Service portal (eFASS) with his Singpass to submit a claim. The insured can access the link: AIC eFinance to learn more about submitting a claim. In the event that the insured is not able to complete it on his own e.g. if he lacks mental capacity, his donee/deputy/immediate family member or caregiver may do so on his behalf. 8. CARESHIELD LIFE2
paragraphs.textJP: Next, the 被保険者 needs to log in to AIC’s e-Service portal (eFASS) with his Singpass to submit a 保険金請求. The 被保険者 can access the link: AIC eFinance to learn more about submitting a 保険金請求. In the event that the 被保険者 is not able to complete it on his own e.g. if he lacks mental capacity, his donee/deputy/immediate family member or caregiver may do so on his behalf. 8. ケアシールド・ライフ2

paragraphs.text: CareShield Life was launched on 1 October 2020 to provide enhanced benefits compared to ElderShield. CareShield Life is a long-term care insurance scheme that supports the basic long-term care needs of Singaporeans with severe disability.
paragraphs.textJP: ケアシールド・ライフ was launched on 1 October 2020 to provide enhanced 給付s compared to エルダーシールド. ケアシールド・ライフ is a long-term care insurance scheme that supports the basic long-term care needs of Singaporeans with 重度障害.

paragraphs.text: CareShield Life is compulsory for all Singaporeans born in 1980 or later. Those born 1980 to 1989 were automatically enrolled in 2020 at one go, while those born 1990 onwards will be automatically enrolled under the scheme upon turning 30. Those with pre-existing disability and illnesses will still be covered under CareShield Life.
paragraphs.textJP: ケアシールド・ライフ is compulsory for all Singaporeans born in 1980 or later. Those born 1980 to 1989 were automatically enrolled in 2020 at one go, while those born 1990 onwards will be automatically enrolled under the scheme upon turning 30. Those with pre-existing disability and illnesses will still be covered under ケアシールド・ライフ.

paragraphs.text: For a person born in 1979 or earlier: (a) He would be auto enrolled to CareShield Life from 1 December 2021 if he is born between 1970 and 1979, covered under the ElderShield 400 scheme and did not develop severe disability. If he is not auto enrolled, participation in CareShield Life is optional3 and his current ElderShield plans will continue to protect him. (b) He could choose to join CareShield Life if he does not develop severe disability (defined as the inability to perform at least three out of six ADLs). (c) He can enjoy participation incentives of up to S$3,000 if he joins and is covered under CareShield Life by 31 December 2024. The participation incentives will be spread equally over 10 years and will be used to offset premiums payable for each year.
paragraphs.textJP: For a person born in 1979 or earlier: (a) He would be auto enrolled to ケアシールド・ライフ from 1 December 2021 if he is born between 1970 and 1979, covered under the エルダーシールド 400 scheme and did not develop 重度障害. If he is not auto enrolled, participation in ケアシールド・ライフ is optional3 and his current エルダーシールド plans will continue to protect him. (b) He could choose to join ケアシールド・ライフ if he does not develop 重度障害 (defined as the inability to perform at least three out of six ADLs). (c) He can enjoy participation incentives of up to S$3,000 if he joins and is covered under ケアシールド・ライフ by 31 December 2024. The participation incentives will be spread equally over 10 years and will be used to offset 保険料s payable for each year.

paragraphs.text: For a person born in 1980 or later: (a) If he was born between 1980 and 1990, he would have automatically joined CareShield Life in 2020. (b) If he was born after 1990, he will join CareShield Life when he turns 30. (c) Universal coverage – he will be covered even if he has pre-existing medical conditions and/or severe disabilities. (d) Transitional subsidies are available for the first five years from the launch of CareShield Life from 2020 to 2024 to ease the transition into the scheme. 2 https://www.careshieldlife.gov.sg 3 Except foreigners who become Singapore Citizens or Permanent Residents from 1 October 2020 onwards, for whom participation is mandatory unless they have severe disability.
paragraphs.textJP: For a person born in 1980 or later: (a) If he was born between 1980 and 1990, he would have automatically joined ケアシールド・ライフ in 2020. (b) If he was born after 1990, he will join ケアシールド・ライフ when he turns 30. (c) Universal 補償 – he will be covered even if he has pre-existing medical conditions and/or severe disabilities. (d) Transitional subsidies are available for the first five years from the launch of ケアシールド・ライフ from 2020 to 2024 to ease the transition into the scheme. 2 https://www.careshieldlife.gov.sg 3 Except foreigners who become Singapore Citizens or Permanent Residents from 1 October 2020 onwards, for whom participation is mandatory unless they have 重度障害.

paragraphs.text: The figure below shows an example of Adam, a Singaporean who is enrolled into CareShield Life at 30 years old. Source: https://www.careshieldlife.gov.sg/content/dam/cshl/pdf/CareShield%20Life%20 Welcome%20Booklet.pdf
paragraphs.textJP: The figure below shows an example of Adam, a Singaporean who is enrolled into ケアシールド・ライフ at 30 years old. Source: https://www.careshieldlife.gov.sg/content/dam/cshl/pdf/CareShield%20Life%20 Welcome%20Booklet.pdf

paragraphs.text: CareShield Life provides better protection and assurance in four ways: (a) Lifetime cash payouts for as long as the insured has severe disabilities; (b) Payout starting at S$600 per month in 2020 and increases annually until age 67 or when a successful claim is made; (c) Government subsidies are given to make premiums affordable i.e. no one will lose coverage because of an inability to pay premiums; and (d) Premiums can be fully paid by MediSave.
paragraphs.textJP: ケアシールド・ライフ provides better protection and assurance in four ways: (a) Lifetime cash payouts for as long as the 被保険者 has severe disabilities; (b) Payout starting at S$600 per month in 2020 and increases annually until age 67 or when a successful 保険金請求 is made; (c) Government subsidies are given to make 保険料s affordable i.e. no one will lose 補償 because of an inability to pay 保険料s; and (d) 保険料s can be fully paid by メディセーブ.

paragraphs.text: In addition to CareShield Life, Singaporeans will benefit from two new schemes starting in 2020: (a) MediSave Care which was launched on 1 October 2020. Singaporeans with severely disability and aged 30 years old and above can make a cash withdrawal of up to S$200 per month from MediSave. (b) ElderFund which was launched on 31 January 2020. It serves as an assistance for lower-income Singaporeans who have severe disability, where they can claim up to S$250 per month to help with long-term care needs. CareShield Life/ElderShield/Supplement policyholders are not eligible for ElderFund pay-outs.
paragraphs.textJP: In addition to ケアシールド・ライフ, Singaporeans will 給付 from two new schemes starting in 2020: (a) メディセーブ Care which was launched on 1 October 2020. Singaporeans with severely disability and aged 30 years old and above can make a cash withdrawal of up to S$200 per month from メディセーブ. (b) エルダーファンド which was launched on 31 January 2020. It serves as an assistance for lower-income Singaporeans who have 重度障害, where they can 保険金請求 up to S$250 per month to help with long-term care needs. ケアシールド・ライフ/エルダーシールド/Supplement 保険契約者s are not eligible for エルダーファンド pay-outs.

subsections.label: C
subsections.title: OTHER LONG-TERM CARE FINANCING SCHEMES AND SUBSIDIES

paragraphs.text: The government has enhanced support for long term care through various schemes such as ElderFund and home caregiving grant. For more details, please visit CareShield Life website.5
paragraphs.textJP: The government has enhanced support for long term care through various schemes such as エルダーファンド and home caregiving grant. For more details, please visit ケアシールド・ライフ website.5

subsections.label: C1
subsections.title: Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (IDAPE)

paragraphs.text: Cash assistance of S$150/month or S$250/month for up to 6 years, for lowincome Singapore Citizens who have developed severe disability and were unable to join ElderShield in 2002 due to their age or pre-existing disabilities then.
paragraphs.textJP: Cash assistance of S$150/month or S$250/month for up to 6 years, for lowincome Singapore Citizens who have developed 重度障害 and were unable to join エルダーシールド in 2002 due to their age or pre-existing disabilities then.

paragraphs.text: A discretionary assistance scheme that offers financial support to lower-income Singapore Citizens aged 30 and above with severe disability, who are not eligible for CareShield Life, ElderShield, and/or Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (IDAPE). It provides cash assistance of S$150/month or S$250/month, for long term care needs.
paragraphs.textJP: A discretionary assistance scheme that offers financial support to lower-income Singapore Citizens aged 30 and above with 重度障害, who are not eligible for ケアシールド・ライフ, エルダーシールド, and/or Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (傷害・健康保険販売事業体). It provides cash assistance of S$150/month or S$250/month, for long term care needs.

paragraphs.text: Cash assistance of S$100/month for life, for moderately disabled Pioneers who always require some assistance with at least 3 out of the 6 Activities of Daily Living.
paragraphs.textJP: Cash assistance of S$100/month for life, for moderately disabled Pioneers who always require some assistance with at least 3 out of the 6 Activities of Daily Living.

paragraphs.textJP: Monthly payout of S$250 or S$400 to defray caregiving costs for eligible care recipients living in the community. The care recipient should always require some assistance with at least three of the six Activities of Daily Living.

paragraphs.text:
Annual subsidy of S$200 for caregivers to attend approved courses to better care for their loved ones. 5 https://www.careshieldlife.gov.sg/long-term-care/other-long-term-care-financing-support.html LONG-TERM CARE INSURANCE CHAPTER IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS What is Long-Term Care Insurance (LTC)? LTC Insurance is designed to meet the costs of care for a person who, because of an accident, illness, frailty or a combination of these, is physically unable to function independently. Instead, he must depend on others to help him to perform most basic Activities of Daily Living (ADLs). Benefits offered under Long-Term Care Insurance
▪ “Service-based” policy pays a benefit when an insured incurs costs for a service covered under the policy (e.g. home care or nursing care) and satisfies the benefit trigger. This type of policy can pay benefits in two ways: » Expense incurred method where the insurer will reimburse the insured for the cost incurred in using one of the services covered under the policy, up to the covered amount. » Indemnity method pays the covered amount, regardless of the actual cost of the services received.
▪ “Disability-based” policy pays a benefit when an insured meets the benefit trigger, regardless of the service use. The benefit trigger may be based on limitations in ADLs and/or degree of cognitive impairment. This means that the benefits may be paid if the insured is diagnosed to be suffering from advanced dementia (including Alzheimer’s disease), or when the insured is assessed to be suffering significant limitations in ADLs, or where there is a deterioration or loss of intellectual capacity resulting in a significant reduction in mental and social functioning, and the need for continuous supervision. Eligibility criteria for payment of Long-Term Care Insurance benefit
▪ Meets The Definition Of Inability To Perform Activities Of Daily Living (ADLs) Or Advanced Dementia.
▪ Meets The Deferred Period Requirement.
▪ Does Not Cover Pre-existing Conditions. “Activities of Daily Living (ADLs)” and how they are related to the benefit payment under Long-Term Care Insurance. One of the key criteria for paying the benefits in LTC policies is the insured’s inability to perform certain specific actions, which are essential, if an individual is to be able to live independently without support from others. These are known as “Activities of Daily Living” (ADLs). Other benefits are available under Long-Term Care Insurance
▪ Usually include a death benefit which is payable to the insured’s beneficiaries upon the death of the insured. However, the quantum is small.
▪ Hospital Room And Board Benefit.
▪ Surgical Procedure Benefit.
▪ Financial Assistance With Adaptation Benefit.
▪ Extended Care Benefit.
▪ Rehabilitation Benefit. Toileting Mobility Transferring Washing Dressing Feeding Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Maximum benefit sum payable
▪ For LTC benefits, depending on the plan that the insured chooses, he may claim, for example, between S$300 and S$5,000 per month, as long as he is unable to perform the specified number of ADLs, and meets the other policy conditions. Some insurers may limit the maximum benefit sum payable.
▪ For policies packaged with hospital benefits, there is usually a maximum amount that can be claimed per day, subject to a maximum number of days per year of hospitalisation.
▪ For those with Surgical Benefits, the insured, again depending on the plan that he has purchased, can claim only a maximum amount per surgery. Benefit payment term While most insureds prefer the policy benefits to be payable as long as they live, the premiums for such plans may be too expensive for them to buy. Some insurers allow customers to choose the benefit payment term. This means that the benefits will be payable for the chosen period (e.g. 12 years of benefits). The premiums payable for a shorter benefit payment term will be cheaper and more affordable. During the benefit payment period, premiums payable are waived. ElderShield and CareShield Life Persons covered under ElderShield Until 2019, all Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents with MediSave Accounts were automatically enrolled into ElderShield at the age of 40, unless they opted out. Since 2020, auto-enrolments into ElderShield were discontinued. Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents born in 1980 and after were instead enrolled into CareShield Life at age 30. Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents born in 1979 to 1970, who were enrolled on ElderShield 400 and did not have any pre-existing disabilities, were also enrolled into CareShield Life and can choose to opt out by 31 December 2023. Those born on 30 September 1932 or before, or had a pre-existing disability as of 30 September 2002, were ineligible to enrol in ElderShield in 2002. Instead, they may be eligible for assistance under the Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (IDAPE) if they develop severe disabilities. How to check ElderShield coverage The following steps should be performed in checking on one’s coverage:
▪ Log in to one’s CPF Digital Services using one’s Singpass.
▪ Hover over "My CPF" and click on "Healthcare" under "My dashboards".
▪ Click on “Long-term care insurance” on the anchor links or scroll down to the “Long-term care insurance” section. If the person is not covered, no information on ElderShield will be reflected. To check one’s coverage, he or she should visit the CPF website at https://www.cpf.gov. sg/member ElderShield300 ElderShield400 CareShield Life Pay-out amount $300 $400 Pay-outs start at $600 per month in 2020 and increase over time Pay-out duration 60 months 72 months For entire duration of disability Government subsidies and incentives No Yes, depending on circumstances Payable by MediSave? Yes Pay-out criteria Unable to perform 3 or more of the 6 activities for daily living IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Scope of protection under ElderShield and its premiums ElderShield provides the following protection and benefits:
▪ Lifetime coverage - The insured continues to be covered for life once he has completed paying all his premiums at age 65.
▪ Up to 72 months of cash pay-outs – The insured stands to receive a monthly cash pay-out for up to five or six years, depending on the plan chosen. With ESH400, he will get S$400 per month for up to 72 months, and with ESH300, he will get S$300 per month for up to 60 months.
▪ Fully payable with MediSave – The insured can use his MediSave to pay his ElderShield premiums. Family members can help the insured by paying from their own MediSave or topping up the insured’s account with cash.
▪ Worldwide coverage – The insured will remain covered, have the ability to make claims, and receive pay-outs no matter where he lives in the world.
▪ The premium payment for ElderShield is up to age 65, with a coverage for life. ElderShield premiums are based on the age at which the insured joins the scheme and are payable until the policy anniversary after his 65th birthday. When an insured is eligible to claim under ElderShield One can apply to receive ElderShield pay-outs if he is determined by an MOHaccredited assessor to be unable to carry out three of the six activities of daily living (ADL) listed below: 1. Washing – The ability to wash in the bath or shower (including getting into and out of the bath or shower) or wash by other means. 2. Dressing – The ability to put on, take off, secure, and unfasten all garments and, as appropriate, any braces, artificial limbs, or other surgical or medical appliances. 3. Feeding – The ability to feed oneself food after it has been prepared and made available. 4. Toileting – The ability to use the toilet or manage bowel and bladder function using protective undergarments, such as diapers or surgical appliances, if appropriate. 5. Mobility – The ability to move indoors from room to room on level surfaces. 6. Transferring – The ability to move from a bed to an upright chair or wheelchair, and vice versa. Making a claim under ElderShield To make a claim, one should complete the claim assessment. First, the insured needs to arrange for a disability assessment which can be conducted by any MOHaccredited severe disability assessor. The assessment costs S$100 for a clinic assessment and S$250 for a house call, both of which are payable to the assessor at the time of assessment. The full fee will be reimbursed if the insured is assessed to be severely disabled and will be reimbursed with the first pay out. Next, the insured needs to log in to AIC’s e-Service portal (eFASS) with his Singpass to submit a claim. In the event that the insured is not able to complete it on his own e.g. if he lacks mental capacity, his donee/deputy/immediate family member or caregiver may do so on his behalf. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS CareShield Life CareShield Life was launched on 1 October 2020 to provide enhanced benefits compared to ElderShield. CareShield Life is a long-term care insurance scheme that supports the basic long-term care needs of Singaporeans with severe disability. CareShield Life is compulsory for all Singaporeans born in 1980 or later. Those born 1980 to 1989 were automatically enrolled in 2020 at one go, while those born 1990 onwards will be automatically enrolled under the scheme upon turning 30. Those with pre-existing disability and illnesses will still be covered under CareShield Life. CareShield Life provides better protection and assurance in four ways:
▪ Lifetime cash pay-outs for as long as the insured has severe disabilities;
▪ Pay-out starting at S$600 per month in 2020 and increases annually until age 67 or when a successful claim is made;
▪ Government subsidies are given to make premiums affordable i.e. no one will lose coverage because of an inability to pay premiums; and
▪ Premiums can be fully paid by MediSave. MediSave Care MediSave Care is a long-term care scheme which was launched on 1 October 2020. Under MediSave Care, Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents, aged 30 years old and above, and with severe disabilities, will be able to make cash withdrawals of up to $200/month from their own and/or their spouse’s MediSave. This can be used for their long-term care needs, after setting aside a minimum amount for other medical expenses. A Singaporean Citizen or a Singapore Permanent Resident is eligible for MediSave Care if he or she is assessed by an MOH-accredited assessor to be unable to perform three or more of the six Activities of Daily Living. Other long-term care financing schemes and subsidies
▪ Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (IDAPE)
▪ ElderFund
▪ Pioneer Disability Assistance Scheme (PioneerDAS)
▪ Seniors Mobility and Enabling Fund (SMF)
▪ Home Caregiving Grant (HCG)
▪ Migrant Domestic Worker Levy Concession for Persons with Disabilities [MDW Levy Concession (PWD)]
▪ Caregivers Training Grant (CTG)

Since 2020, auto-enrolments into エルダーシールド were discontinued. Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents born in 1980 and after were instead enrolled into ケアシールド・ライフ at age 30. Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents born in 1979 to 1970, who were enrolled on エルダーシールド 400 and did not have any pre-existing disabilities, were also enrolled into ケアシールド・ライフ and can choose to opt out by 31 December 2023. Those born on 30 September 1932 or before, or had a pre-existing disability as of 30 September 2002, were ineligible to enrol in エルダーシールド in 2002. Instead, they may be eligible for assistance under the Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (傷害・健康保険販売事業体) if they develop severe disabilities. How to check エルダーシールド 補償 The following steps should be performed in checking on one’s 補償:
▪ Log in to one’s CPF（中央積立基金） Digital Services using one’s Singpass.
▪ Hover over "My CPF（中央積立基金）" and click on "Healthcare" under "My dashboards".
▪ Click on “Long-term care insurance” on the anchor links or scroll down to the “Long-term care insurance” section. If the person is not covered, no information on エルダーシールド will be reflected. To check one’s 補償, he or she should visit the CPF（中央積立基金） website at https://www.CPF（中央積立基金）.gov. sg/member エルダーシールド300 エルダーシールド400 ケアシールド・ライフ Pay-out amount $300 $400 Pay-outs start at $600 per month in 2020 and increase over time Pay-out duration 60 months 72 months For entire duration of disability Government subsidies and incentives No Yes, depending on circumstances Payable by メディセーブ? Yes Pay-out criteria Unable to perform 3 or more of the 6 activities for daily living IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Scope of protection under エルダーシールド and its 保険料s エルダーシールド provides the following protection and 給付s:
▪ Lifetime 補償 - The 被保険者 continues to be covered for life once he has completed paying all his 保険料s at age 65.
▪ Up to 72 months of cash pay-outs – The 被保険者 stands to receive a monthly cash pay-out for up to five or six years, depending on the plan chosen. With ESH400, he will get S$400 per month for up to 72 months, and with ESH300, he will get S$300 per month for up to 60 months.
▪ Fully payable with メディセーブ – The 被保険者 can use his メディセーブ to pay his エルダーシールド 保険料s. Family members can help the 被保険者 by paying from their own メディセーブ or topping up the 被保険者’s account with cash.
▪ Worldwide 補償 – The 被保険者 will remain covered, have the ability to make 保険金請求s, and receive pay-outs no matter where he lives in the world.
▪ The 保険料 payment for エルダーシールド is up to age 65, with a 補償 for life. エルダーシールド 保険料s are based on the age at which the 被保険者 joins the scheme and are payable until the policy anniversary after his 65th birthday. When an 被保険者 is eligible to 保険金請求 under エルダーシールド One can apply to receive エルダーシールド pay-outs if he is determined by an MOH（保健省）accredited assessor to be unable to carry out three of the six activities of daily living (ADL) listed below: 1. Washing – The ability to wash in the bath or shower (including getting into and out of the bath or shower) or wash by other means. 2. Dressing – The ability to put on, take off, secure, and unfasten all garments and, as appropriate, any braces, artificial limbs, or other surgical or medical appliances. 3. Feeding – The ability to feed oneself food after it has been prepared and made available. 4. Toileting – The ability to use the toilet or manage bowel and bladder function using protective undergarments, such as diapers or surgical appliances, if appropriate. 5. Mobility – The ability to move indoors from room to room on level surfaces. 6. Transferring – The ability to move from a bed to an upright chair or wheelchair, and vice versa. Making a 保険金請求 under エルダーシールド To make a 保険金請求, one should complete the 保険金請求 assessment. First, the 被保険者 needs to arrange for a disability assessment which can be conducted by any MOH（保健省）accredited 重度障害 assessor. The assessment costs S$100 for a clinic assessment and S$250 for a house call, both of which are payable to the assessor at the time of assessment. The full fee will be reimbursed if the 被保険者 is assessed to be severely disabled and will be reimbursed with the first pay out. Next, the 被保険者 needs to log in to AIC’s e-Service portal (eFASS) with his Singpass to submit a 保険金請求. In the event that the 被保険者 is not able to complete it on his own e.g. if he lacks mental capacity, his donee/deputy/immediate family member or caregiver may do so on his behalf. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS ケアシールド・ライフ ケアシールド・ライフ was launched on 1 October 2020 to provide enhanced 給付s compared to エルダーシールド. ケアシールド・ライフ is a long-term care insurance scheme that supports the basic long-term care needs of Singaporeans with 重度障害. ケアシールド・ライフ is compulsory for all Singaporeans born in 1980 or later. Those born 1980 to 1989 were automatically enrolled in 2020 at one go, while those born 1990 onwards will be automatically enrolled under the scheme upon turning 30. Those with pre-existing disability and illnesses will still be covered under ケアシールド・ライフ. ケアシールド・ライフ provides better protection and assurance in four ways:
▪ Lifetime cash pay-outs for as long as the 被保険者 has severe disabilities;
▪ Pay-out starting at S$600 per month in 2020 and increases annually until age 67 or when a successful 保険金請求 is made;
▪ Government subsidies are given to make 保険料s affordable i.e. no one will lose 補償 because of an inability to pay 保険料s; and
▪ 保険料s can be fully paid by メディセーブ. メディセーブ Care メディセーブ Care is a long-term care scheme which was launched on 1 October 2020. Under メディセーブ Care, Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents, aged 30 years old and above, and with severe disabilities, will be able to make cash withdrawals of up to $200/month from their own and/or their spouse’s メディセーブ. This can be used for their long-term care needs, after setting aside a minimum amount for other medical expenses. A Singaporean Citizen or a Singapore Permanent Resident is eligible for メディセーブ Care if he or she is assessed by an MOH（保健省）-accredited assessor to be unable to perform three or more of the six Activities of Daily Living. Other long-term care financing schemes and subsidies
▪ Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (傷害・健康保険販売事業体)
▪ エルダーファンド
▪ Pioneer Disability Assistance Scheme (PioneerDAS)
▪ Seniors Mobility and Enabling Fund (SMF)
▪ Home Caregiving Grant (HCG)
▪ Migrant Domestic Worker Levy Concession for Persons with Disabilities [MDW Levy Concession (PWD)]
▪ Caregivers Training Grant (CTG)

chapters.title: CRITICAL ILLNESS INSURANCE
