paragraphs.text: When Critical Illness Insurance was first developed, the initial product was known as Dread Disease Insurance, covering four primary health conditions: Cancer, Stroke, Heart Attack and Coronary Artery Bypass. Since then, the coverage has been widely accepted worldwide as people become more aware of the financial implications of serious medical conditions and the need for protection in the event of an unforeseen diagnosis of the illness. Critical Illness Insurance has evolved over the last four decades. In this chapter, we will take a closer look at Critical Illness Insurance, including the recent developments of the product in the market.
paragraphs.textJP: When Critical Illness Insurance was first developed, the initial product was known as 特定疾病 Insurance, covering four primary health conditions: Cancer, Stroke, Heart Attack and Coronary Artery Bypass. Since then, the 補償 has been widely accepted worldwide as people become more aware of the financial implications of serious medical conditions and the need for protection in the event of an unforeseen diagnosis of the illness. Critical Illness Insurance has evolved over the last four decades. In this chapter, we will take a closer look at Critical Illness Insurance, including the recent developments of the product in the market.

paragraphs.text: Critical Illness (also known as Dread Disease) insurance provides a lump sum benefit to the policyholder in the event that the insured is diagnosed to be suffering from one of the critical illnesses or has undergone a surgical procedure covered under the policy. Each critical illness and surgery covered by the policy is defined in the policy, and the benefits will be paid only if he suffers a condition that meets the definition as specified in the policy.
paragraphs.textJP: Critical Illness (also known as 特定疾病) insurance provides a lump sum 給付 to the 保険契約者 in the event that the 被保険者 is diagnosed to be suffering from one of the critical illnesses or has undergone a surgical procedure covered under the policy. Each critical illness and surgery covered by the policy is defined in the policy, and the 給付s will be paid only if he suffers a condition that meets the definition as specified in the policy.

paragraphs.text: Benefits received from a Critical Illness policy can be used to finance the cost of care and treatment, recuperation, medical aids, replacing any income loss or even funding a change in lifestyle.
paragraphs.textJP: 給付s received from a Critical Illness policy can be used to finance the cost of care and treatment, recuperation, medical aids, replacing any income loss or even funding a change in lifestyle.

paragraphs.text: Critical Illness (CI) Insurance policy can be sold as a stand-alone policy (individual or group basis), or as an optional rider to a Whole Life, Endowment, Term Insurance or Investment-Linked Policy (ILP), to provide either an additional sum assured or the advanced payment of a portion, or the entire sum assured payable on death. 3. LIA CRITICAL ILLNESS FRAMEWORK 2019 (“THE CI FRAMEWORK”)
paragraphs.textJP: Critical Illness (CI) Insurance policy can be sold as a stand-alone policy (individual or group basis), or as an optional 特約 to a Whole Life, Endowment, Term Insurance or Investment-Linked Policy (ILP), to provide either an additional 保険金額 or the advanced payment of a portion, or the entire 保険金額 payable on death. 3. LIA CRITICAL ILLNESS FRAMEWORK 2019 (“THE CI枠組み”)

paragraphs.text: The CI Framework seeks to balance the interests of both the consumers and the insurance companies. Offering clear and consistent coverage on the core critical illness benefits will allow for easier product comparison by consumers as well as consistent outcomes on CI claims. It seeks to reduce ambiguity of the same CI claim being paid by one insurer but rejected by another.
paragraphs.textJP: The CI枠組み seeks to balance the interests of both the consumers and the insurance companies. Offering clear and consistent 補償 on the core critical illness 給付s will allow for easier product comparison by consumers as well as consistent outcomes on CI 保険金請求s. It seeks to reduce ambiguity of the same CI 保険金請求 being paid by one 保険者 but rejected by another.

paragraphs.text: Set up in consultation with the Monetary Authority of Singapore, member companies of Life Insurance Association (LIA) and General Insurance Association (GIA) undertake to comply with the framework which governs the provision of CI coverage by insurers. Under the CI framework, severe Critical Illness definitions is standardised for 37 medical conditions by way of common definitions.
paragraphs.textJP: Set up in consultation with the Monetary Authority of Singapore, member companies of Life Insurance Association (LIA) and General Insurance Association (GIA) undertake to comply with the framework which governs the provision of CI 補償 by 保険者s. Under the CI枠組み, severe Critical Illness definitions is standardised for 37 medical conditions by way of common definitions.

paragraphs.text: Since 2003, the industry has standardised definitions for a list of 37 severe critical illnesses for insurers to adopt to provide consistency for policyholders. Subsequent reviews were done in 2014 and 2019 to update the standardised definitions, having considered advancements in medical technology, health trends, diagnosis as well as the experiences gathered by the industry to address any issues that arose due to lack of clarity on the definitions.
paragraphs.textJP: Since 2003, the industry has standardised definitions for a list of 37 severe critical illnesses for 保険者s to adopt to provide consistency for 保険契約者s. Subsequent reviews were done in 2014 and 2019 to update the standardised definitions, having considered advancements in medical technology, health trends, diagnosis as well as the experiences gathered by the industry to address any issues that arose due to lack of clarity on the definitions.

paragraphs.text: Since 2014, insurers could offer any number of medical conditions under a CI plan including single-illness CI plans. There were no restrictions on how many stages of illness progression that insurers could cover.
paragraphs.textJP: Since 2014, 保険者s could offer any number of medical conditions under a CI plan including single-illness CI plans. There were no restrictions on how many stages of illness progression that 保険者s could cover.

paragraphs.text: In the 2019 review, the standardised definitions were updated with some name changes. LIA defines and maintains a list of 37 medical conditions and these common definitions describe the respective medical conditions at the “severe” stage.1
paragraphs.textJP: In the 2019 review, the standardised definitions were updated with some name changes. LIA defines and maintains a list of 37 medical conditions and these common definitions describe the respective medical conditions at the “severe” stage.1

paragraphs.text: An insurer which chooses to cover a medical condition found in the LIA’s list must adopt the LIA’s common definition for the medical condition at severe stage. However, for these conditions, insurers are to use their own definitions in respect of the mildly-moderately severe stage or the extremely severe stage of illness progression.
paragraphs.textJP: An 保険者 which chooses to cover a medical condition found in the LIA’s list must adopt the LIA’s common definition for the medical condition at severe stage. However, for these conditions, 保険者s are to use their own definitions in respect of the mildly-moderately severe stage or the extremely severe stage of illness progression.

paragraphs.text: For medical conditions not found in the LIA’s list, insurers are to use their own definitions for all stages of illness progression.
paragraphs.textJP: For medical conditions not found in the LIA’s list, 保険者s are to use their own definitions for all stages of illness progression.

paragraphs.text: The industry will review the LIA’s common definitions for continued relevance once every three years.
paragraphs.textJP: The industry will review the LIA’s common definitions for continued relevance once every three years.

paragraphs.text: The purpose is to enhance clarity of the coverage intent so customers do not misunderstand what is being covered and what is not.
paragraphs.textJP: The purpose is to enhance clarity of the 補償 intent so customers do not misunderstand what is being covered and what is not.

paragraphs.text: On and after 26 August 2020, CI products using Version 2014 definitions may not be sold in Singapore. Any CI policy purchased from 26 August 2020 will be subjected to the 2019 CI definitions.
paragraphs.textJP: On and after 26 August 2020, CI products using Version 2014 definitions may not be sold in Singapore. Any CI policy pur地域医療支援スキームed from 26 August 2020 will be subjected to the 2019 CI definitions.

paragraphs.text: Critical Illness policies issued before were not impacted by the new definitions.
paragraphs.textJP: Critical Illness policies issued before were not impacted by the new definitions.

paragraphs.text: Financial advisors need to highlight to their clients that CI are covered under the policy only if they meet the definitions.
paragraphs.textJP: Financial advisors need to highlight to their clients that CI are covered under the policy only if they meet the definitions.

paragraphs.text: Appendix 6A shows the Standard Definitions for Severe Stage of 37 Critical Illnesses: Version 2019 extracted from the LIA Website at: www.lia.org.sg.
paragraphs.textJP: Appendix 6A shows the Standard Definitions for Severe Stage of 37 Critical Illnesses: Version 2019 extracted from the LIA Website at: www.lia.org.sg.

paragraphs.text: The list of changes to the standard list of 37 critical illness under the Critical Illness Framework 2019 are shown in Annex 1. 1 https://www.lia.org.sg/industry-guidelines/health-insurance/critical-illness-plan/2019/lia-critical-illness-ciframework- 2019/
paragraphs.textJP: The list of changes to the standard list of 37 critical illness under the Critical Illness Framework 2019 are shown in Annex 1. 1 https://www.lia.org.sg/industry-guidelines/health-insurance/critical-illness-plan/2019/lia-critical-illness-ciframework- 2019/

paragraphs.text: From 16 November 2022, more updates were made on CI framework as follows: (a) Insurers can independently determine suitable waiting periods in all of the insurance policies. Waiting period refers to the period of time stated in the policy which must pass, before some or all of the insured’s Health Insurance coverage can begin. (b) The S$25,000 cap on sum payable in respect of “Angioplasty & Other Invasive Treatment for Coronary Artery” was removed.
paragraphs.textJP: From 16 November 2022, more updates were made on CI枠組み as follows: (a) 保険者s can independently determine suitable 待機期間s in all of the insurance policies. 待機期間 refers to the period of time stated in the policy which must pass, before some or all of the 被保険者’s Health Insurance 補償 can begin. (b) The S$25,000 cap on sum payable in respect of “Angioplasty & Other Invasive Treatment for Coronary Artery” was removed.

paragraphs.text: Although each insurer is free to adopt its own wordings on the waiting period clause, the waiting period is to begin from the date that cover commences, or date of reinstatement and the “date of diagnosis” shall be used to apply to the waiting period.
paragraphs.textJP: Although each 保険者 is free to adopt its own wordings on the 待機期間 clause, the 待機期間 is to begin from the date that cover commences, or date of 復活 and the “date of diagnosis” shall be used to apply to the 待機期間.

paragraphs.text: The following are within the scope of the CI Framework: (a) New individual policies or benefits. (b) New group policies or renewals, including any new CI cover during the policy year. (c) CI benefits issued as a standalone policy or a rider. (d) CI benefits issued on an individual basis or a group basis. (e) CI benefits issued as a rider to an H&S policy or a PA policy. (f) For cases of top-up to existing CI policies, insurers are given the discretion to decide to either apply the policy’s existing definitions to the top-up amount or apply the prevailing current definitions to the top-up amount. Where it is the latter, it is for the insurer to decide on the date of implementation of such a practice. Furthermore, the insurer should disclose this practice to the policyholder in order to avoid any misunderstanding or difficulties in the future. (g) Where an existing individual CI term policy (yearly renewable type) is auto-renewed or has to be cancelled and re-issued due to administrative reasons, for example: change of payment mode or credit cards, the same approach applies as described in Section (f) above. (h) Where an existing CI benefit attached to a health policy (including Integrated Shield Plan) is auto-renewed, the same approach applies as described in Section (f) above).
paragraphs.textJP: The following are within the scope of the CI枠組み: (a) New individual policies or 給付s. (b) New group policies or renewals, including any new CI cover during the policy year. (c) CI 給付s issued as a standalone policy or a 特約. (d) CI 給付s issued on an individual basis or a group basis. (e) CI 給付s issued as a 特約 to an H&S policy or a PA policy. (f) For cases of top-up to existing CI policies, 保険者s are given the discretion to decide to either apply the policy’s existing definitions to the top-up amount or apply the prevailing current definitions to the top-up amount. Where it is the latter, it is for the 保険者 to decide on the date of implementation of such a practice. Furthermore, the 保険者 should disclose this practice to the 保険契約者 in order to avoid any misunderstanding or difficulties in the future. (g) Where an existing individual CI term policy (yearly renewable type) is auto-renewed or has to be cancelled and re-issued due to administrative reasons, for example: change of payment mode or credit cards, the same approach applies as described in Section (f) above. (h) Where an existing CI 給付 attached to a health policy (including Integrated Shield Plan) is auto-renewed, the same approach applies as described in Section (f) above).

paragraphs.text: The following are out of scope of the CI Framework: (a) CI products for Male or Female or Children which do not cover any of the CI conditions in its entire scope. Example, a Female CI product which covers only Female Organs Cancer does not need to apply the Major Cancer definition. (b) Terminal Illness issued under the general benefits of a basic plan.
paragraphs.textJP: The following are out of scope of the CI枠組み: (a) CI products for Male or Female or Children which do not cover any of the CI conditions in its entire scope. Example, a Female CI product which covers only Female Organs Cancer does not need to apply the Major Cancer definition. (b) 末期疾病 issued under the general 給付s of a basic plan.

paragraphs.text: However, the above does not preclude insurers from customising their products by extending coverage for more medical conditions and stages of illness progression, beyond the standard list of 37 critical illnesses.
paragraphs.textJP: However, the above does not preclude 保険者s from customising their products by extending 補償 for more medical conditions and stages of illness progression, beyond the standard list of 37 critical illnesses.

paragraphs.text: An insurer may choose to cover any number of medical conditions under a CI plan, including a single illness type of plan. For each medical condition, subject to “Adoption of LIA’s Common Definitions”, there are no restrictions on how many stages and what the stages of illness progression are, which may be covered.
paragraphs.textJP: An 保険者 may choose to cover any number of medical conditions under a CI plan, including a single illness type of plan. For each medical condition, subject to “Adoption of LIA’s Common Definitions”, there are no restrictions on how many stages and what the stages of illness progression are, which may be covered.

sections.title: Benefits Offered Under Critical Illness Insurance

subsections.label: A
subsections.title: Eligibility Criteria For Payment Of Critical Illness Benefit

paragraphs.text: Although there are two types of CI Insurance covers, each of them shares common features as described below: (a) It pays a lump sum amount upon diagnosis of an insured critical illness. (b) Only one critical illness claim is allowed unless it is a Multiple Pay CI Insurance plan. (c) It has a waiting period. (d) There may be a cap on the total amount of coverage that a policy owner can buy. This is to minimise the risk of moral hazard. (e) The premium can be level or age-banded and is non-guaranteed. (f) There is no restriction on how the CI Insurance benefit payable is to be used. (g) It can be a standalone policy or attached as a rider to an ILP, Term, Endowment or Whole Life Insurance policy. (h) The packaged CI Insurance policy may accumulate cash value (e.g. Whole Life type of CI Insurance policy) and has non-forfeiture options. However, the CI Insurance cover may be terminated once the policy owner exercises any of the non-forfeiture options (excluding the paid-up option). (i) A stand-alone Term CI Insurance policy does not acquire cash value. Hence, it does not have any non-forfeiture option. (j) CI rider does not acquire any cash value. (k) It provides 24-hour, worldwide coverage, unless otherwise endorsed or amended in the policy. (l) Assignment of the policy may or may not be allowed. (m) There is minimum entry age for applying and maximum coverage age. For young lives, it may be subject to lien3. (n) There is a minimum (e.g. S$10,000) and maximum (e.g. S$2,000,000) sum assured restriction. (o) The cover may expire at a maximum age (e.g. 75 years) or a whole life cover may be provided.
paragraphs.textJP: Although there are two types of CI Insurance covers, each of them shares common features as described below: (a) It pays a lump sum amount upon diagnosis of an 被保険者 critical illness. (b) Only one critical illness 保険金請求 is allowed unless it is a 複数回支払CI Insurance plan. (c) It has a 待機期間. (d) There may be a cap on the total amount of 補償 that a policy owner can buy. This is to minimise the risk of モラルハザード. (e) The 保険料 can be level or age-banded and is non-guaranteed. (f) There is no restriction on how the CI Insurance 給付 payable is to be used. (g) It can be a standalone policy or attached as a 特約 to an ILP, Term, Endowment or Whole Life Insurance policy. (h) The packaged CI Insurance policy may accumulate cash value (e.g. Whole Life type of CI Insurance policy) and has non-forfeiture options. However, the CI Insurance cover may be terminated once the policy owner exercises any of the non-forfeiture options (excluding the paid-up option). (i) A stand-alone Term CI Insurance policy does not acquire cash value. Hence, it does not have any non-forfeiture option. (j) CI 特約 does not acquire any cash value. (k) It provides 24-hour, worldwide 補償, unless otherwise endorsed or amended in the policy. (l) Assignment of the policy may or may not be allowed. (m) There is minimum entry age for applying and maximum 補償 age. For young lives, it may be subject to lien3. (n) There is a minimum (e.g. S$10,000) and maximum (e.g. S$2,000,000) 保険金額 restriction. (o) The cover may expire at a maximum age (e.g. 75 years) or a whole life cover may be provided.

paragraphs.text: There are two main types of CI Insurance covers offered in the insurance market. They either provide an: (a) Acceleration Benefit; or (b) Additional Benefit.
paragraphs.textJP: There are two main types of CI Insurance covers offered in the insurance market. They either provide an: (a) Acceleration 給付; or (b) Additional 給付.

paragraphs.text: Some insurers have also introduced Severity-Based and Multiple Pay CI Insurance plans. Let us look at how each of them works.
paragraphs.textJP: Some 保険者s have also introduced Severity-Based and 複数回支払CI Insurance plans. Let us look at how each of them works.

subsections.label: C1
subsections.title: Acceleration Benefit Critical Illness (CI) Insurance Cover

paragraphs.text: This can be issued as a packaged policy or rider to provide for pre-payment of a portion (e.g. 50%) or the full sum assured (i.e. 100%) of the basic policy which it is attached to. For example, an individual buys a S$200,000 Whole Life type of CI Insurance policy with 50% acceleration. In the event that he is diagnosed to be suffering from one of the critical illnesses covered under his policy, the insurer will pay him S$100,000 (i.e. 50% x S$200,000) plus bonuses (if any). The balance of the sum assured plus bonuses (if any) will be paid, when he dies or suffers a Total and Permanent Disability (TPD). On the other hand, if he dies or suffers a TPD without contracting any critical illness, the full S$200,000 plus bonuses (if any) will be paid to him. Figure 6.1 illustrates how this policy works. 3 A lien is a feature which restricts the sum assured payable on the happening of the insured event. Most insurers impose the lien on policies taken on children’s lives for a specified number of years, e.g. three years. In such a case, if the insured child dies within the 1st year after the inception of the policy, the insurer will pay only 25% of the sum assured. This percentage increases on the 2nd and 3rd year to 50% and 75%, respectively. From the 4th year onwards, the full sum assured will be paid, whenever the insured event happens. Figure 6.1: Illustration Of The Working Of A S$200,000 Whole Life Type Of Critical Illness (CI) Insurance Policy With 50% Acceleration Critical Illness Death/TPD
paragraphs.textJP: This can be issued as a packaged policy or 特約 to provide for pre-payment of a portion (e.g. 50%) or the full 保険金額 (i.e. 100%) of the basic policy which it is attached to. For example, an individual buys a S$200,000 Whole Life type of CI Insurance policy with 50% acceleration. In the event that he is diagnosed to be suffering from one of the critical illnesses covered under his policy, the 保険者 will pay him S$100,000 (i.e. 50% x S$200,000) plus bonuses (if any). The balance of the 保険金額 plus bonuses (if any) will be paid, when he dies or suffers a Total and Permanent Disability (TPD). On the other hand, if he dies or suffers a TPD without contracting any critical illness, the full S$200,000 plus bonuses (if any) will be paid to him. Figure 6.1 illustrates how this policy works. 3 A lien is a feature which restricts the 保険金額 payable on the happening of the 被保険者 event. Most 保険者s impose the lien on policies taken on children’s lives for a specified number of years, e.g. three years. In such a case, if the 被保険者 child dies within the 1st year after the inception of the policy, the 保険者 will pay only 25% of the 保険金額. This percentage increases on the 2nd and 3rd year to 50% and 75%, respectively. From the 4th year onwards, the full 保険金額 will be paid, whenever the 被保険者 event happens. As the sum assured reduces, the future premiums may be reduced in the same proportion.

paragraphs.text: If the person has chosen a 100% (instead of 50%) Acceleration Benefit in the above example, the full basic sum assured plus bonuses (if any) will be paid, regardless of whether he contracts a critical illness, dies or suffers a TPD.

paragraphs.text: Policy owners who opt for less than 100% acceleration can consider attaching a CI Waiver of Premium rider to it, so that the future premiums on the balance sum assured will be waived.

paragraphs.text: The cover for this type of CI Insurance policy may be up to the age of 100 years, or for life, depending on the type of Life Insurance policy to which it is attached or packaged. If it is packaged or attached to an ILP or Endowment Insurance policy, then it will follow the same terms of these basic policies. On the other hand, if it is a Whole Life Insurance policy to which it is attached or packaged to, then the cover is usually up to the age of 100 years, so long as the policy owner keeps the policy in force.

If it is packaged or attached to an ILP or Endowment Insurance policy, then it will follow the same terms of these basic policies. On the other hand, if it is a Whole Life Insurance policy to which it is attached or packaged to, then the cover is usually up to the age of 100 years, so long as the policy owner keeps the policy in force.

paragraphs.text: Another factor that affects the tenor of the CI Insurance cover is whether the attached policy will acquire a cash value. When attached to a Term Insurance policy which does not acquire a cash value, the cover will terminate if the Payment of S$200,000 Payment of S$100,000 Payment of S$100,000 Death/TPD S$200,000 Whole Life type of Critical Illness Insurance policy with 50% Acceleration Term Insurance ends. On the other hand, if the CI Insurance cover is attached to an Endowment Insurance or a Whole Life Insurance policy, it will acquire a cash value. Once such a policy has acquired a cash value, the insurer can make use of the automatic premium loan feature to keep the policy in force should the policy owner default in his premium payment after the grace period is over. Alternatively, the policy owner can exercise the paid-up option, in which case, the life insured will still enjoy the CI Insurance cover, but only for a reduced amount.

Alternatively, the policy owner can exercise the paid-up option, in which case, the life 被保険者 will still enjoy the CI Insurance cover, but only for a reduced amount.

subsections.label: C2
subsections.title: Additional Benefit Critical Illness (CI) Insurance Cover

paragraphs.text: This type of cover is available on a stand-alone basis or as a rider.  On a stand-alone basis, the sum assured will be paid upon the diagnosis of a covered critical illness, and the policy will be terminated.  As a rider, it pays an amount in addition to the sum assured of the basic policy to which it is attached. For example, a person purchases a S$100,000 Whole Life Insurance policy with a S$200,000 Additional Benefit CI Insurance rider. If he contracts one of the critical illnesses covered by his policy, the insurer will pay him S$200,000. The Whole Life Insurance sum assured of S$100,000 will be paid only upon his death or TPD. Figure 6.2 illustrates the working of this type of CI Insurance rider. Acceleration Benefit Critical Illness (CI) Coverage Lump sum payment of basic sum assured plus bonuses (if any) upon diagnosis of any covered insured illnesses &/or waiver of future premium. Lump sum payment of basic sum assured plus bonuses (if any) upon death. Payment of the basic sum assured plus bonuses (if any) upon TPD. OR OR Figure 6.2: Illustration Of The Working Of A S$100,000 Whole Life Policy With A S$200,000 Additional Benefit Critical Illness (CI) Insurance Rider
paragraphs.textJP: This type of cover is available on a stand-alone basis or as a 特約.  On a stand-alone basis, the 保険金額 will be paid upon the diagnosis of a covered critical illness, and the policy will be terminated.  As a 特約, it pays an amount in addition to the 保険金額 of the basic policy to which it is attached. paragraphs.text: Note from Figure 6.2, payment of the CI Insurance benefit does not affect the basic sum assured. If he contracts a critical illness followed by death, the total amount that the insurer will pay under this policy will be S$300,000. However, if he does not contract any critical illness, then the amount that the insurer needs to pay is only S$100,000 upon death or TPD.
