This approach targets limited resources to those who need them more and ensures that the Government subsidies are sustainable. 1 https://www.macrotrends.net/countries/SGP/singapore/life-expectancy 2 https://www.statista.com/statistics/624889/singapore-infant-mortality-rate/
paragraphs.textJP: In many healthcare settings, a means-test is used to target subsidies, so that needy patients can receive more help; lower-income patients receive more subsidies than higher-income patients. This approach targets limited resources to those who need them more and ensures that the Government subsidies are sustainable. 1 https://www.macrotrends.net/countries/SGP/singapore/life-expectancy 2 https://www.statista.com/statistics/624889/singapore-infant-mortality-rate/

paragraphs.text: Patients can choose different ward classes if they need to be admitted into a hospital. These ward classes differ only in the type of physical amenities and level of comfort and range from single-bedded rooms (Class A wards) to rooms with multiple beds (Class B/C wards). The standard of medical care is the same regardless of the ward class.
paragraphs.textJP: Patients can choose different ward classes if they need to be admitted into a hospital. These ward classes differ only in the type of physical amenities and level of comfort and range from single-bedded rooms (Class A wards) to rooms with multiple beds (Class B/C wards). The standard of medical care is the same regardless of the ward class.

paragraphs.text: The subsidies provided by the government will differ according to the ward class. The wards with basic amenities (e.g. Class C wards) are heavily subsidised. Those who desire more amenities can choose a higher ward class but will receive less subsidy. Class B2 and C ward admissions (81% of public hospital beds) are heavily subsidised, ranging from 50% to 80% for Singapore Citizens, and 25% to 50% for Permanent Residents.3 Patients who opt for Class B1 or Class A admissions wards will receive little to no subsidy.4
paragraphs.textJP: The subsidies provided by the government will differ according to the ward class. The wards with basic amenities (e.g. Class C wards) are heavily subsidised. Those who desire more amenities can choose a higher ward class but will receive less subsidy. Class B2 and C ward admissions (81% of public hospital beds) are heavily subsidised, ranging from 50% to 80% for Singapore Citizens, and 25% to 50% for Permanent Residents.3 Patients who opt for Class B1 or Class A admissions wards will receive little to no subsidy.4

paragraphs.text: In January 2009, means-testing in public hospitals was introduced to better target the heavy subsidies for Class B2 and Class C wards to the lower-income group. The basis of subsidy is dependent on the patient’s Per Capita Household Income or Annual Value of Residence, for households with no income.
paragraphs.textJP: In January 2009, means-testing in public hospitals was introduced to better target the heavy subsidies for Class B2 and Class C wards to the lower-income group. The basis of subsidy is dependent on the patient’s Per Capita Household Income or Annual Value of Residence, for households with no income.

paragraphs.text: Patients requiring follow-ups at specialist outpatient clinics (SOCs) after their hospitalisation may opt to continue their treatment in either subsidised or private SOCs, regardless of their choice of inpatient ward class.
paragraphs.textJP: Patients requiring follow-ups at specialist 外来 clinics (SOCs) after their 入院 may opt to continue their treatment in either subsidised or private SOCs, regardless of their choice of 入院患者 ward class.

paragraphs.text: In general, the eligibility for higher subsidy is based on the Per Capita Household Income (PCHI), or annual value of residence (for any household with no income).
paragraphs.textJP: In general, the eligibility for higher subsidy is based on the Per Capita Household Income (PCHI), or annual value of residence (for any household with no income).

paragraphs.text: For SOC services, eligible patients can receive subsidies ranging from 30% to 70%, depending on their Per Capita Household Income (PCHI) and Annual Value of Residence (where applicable). Lower- to middle-income SOC patients can receive higher subsidies. Permanent Residents will continue to receive 25% subsidy.
paragraphs.textJP: For SOC services, eligible patients can receive subsidies ranging from 30% to 70%, depending on their Per Capita Household Income (PCHI) and Annual Value of Residence (where applicable). Lower- to middle-income SOC patients can receive higher subsidies. Permanent Residents will continue to receive 25% subsidy.

paragraphs.text: For standard drugs at SOCs, subsidised patients can receive means-tested subsidies of 50% or 75% subsidy, depending on their PCHI. 3 https://www.moh.gov.sg/cost-financing/healthcare-schemes-subsidies/subsidies-for-acute-inpatient-careat- public-healthcare-institutions 4 https://www.moh.gov.sg/home/our-healthcare-system/healthcare-services-and-facilities/hospital-services Medisave Hospital Bills
paragraphs.textJP: For standard drugs at SOCs, subsidised patients can receive means-tested subsidies of 50% or 75% subsidy, depending on their PCHI. 3 https://www.MOH（保健省）.gov.sg/cost-financing/healthcare-schemes-subsidies/subsidies-for-acute-入院患者-careat- public-healthcare-institutions 4 https://www.MOH（保健省）.gov.sg/home/our-healthcare-system/healthcare-services-and-facilities/hospital-services メディセーブ Hospital Bills

paragraphs.text: Other services such as day surgery and Accident & Emergency (A&E) services are subsidised up to 80% and 50% respectively. Day surgeries will be subsidised at between 50% to 80% for Singapore Citizens and 25% to 50% for Permanent Residents. Patients who use A&E services are charged at a flat rate for basic investigations and services.
paragraphs.textJP: Other services such as day surgery and Accident & Emergency (A&E) services are subsidised up to 80% and 50% respectively. Day surgeries will be subsidised at between 50% to 80% for Singapore Citizens and 25% to 50% for Permanent Residents. Patients who use A&E services are charged at a flat rate for basic investigations and services.

paragraphs.text: The services provided at Government polyclinics are subsidised at about 50% (75% if the patient is a child or an elderly person). All lower- to middle-income patients at the polyclinics will enjoy a 75% subsidy for all standard drugs.
paragraphs.textJP: The services provided at Government polyclinics are subsidised at about 50% (75% if the patient is a child or an elderly person). All lower- to middle-income patients at the polyclinics will enjoy a 75% subsidy for all standard drugs.

paragraphs.text: The Community Health Assist Scheme (CHAS) enables eligible Singapore Citizens to receive subsidies for medical and/or dental care* at participating General Practitioner (GP) and dental clinics. With effect from Nov 2019, this scheme has been expanded to include all Singapore Citizens regardless of their income levels. *Only for CHAS Blue, CHAS Orange, MG and PG cardholders.
paragraphs.textJP: The Community Health Assist Scheme (地域医療支援スキーム) enables eligible Singapore Citizens to receive subsidies for medical and/or dental care* at participating General Practitioner (GP) and dental clinics. With effect from Nov 2019, this scheme has been expanded to include all Singapore Citizens regardless of their income levels. *Only for 地域医療支援スキーム Blue, 地域医療支援スキーム Orange, MG and PG cardholders.

paragraphs.text: The colour of the CHAS card indicates the subsidy tier that cardholders are entitled to, namely the CHAS Blue, Orange or Green tier. All Pioneer Generation (PG) and Merdeka Generation (MG) seniors also receive special subsidies at CHAS clinics.
paragraphs.textJP: The colour of the 地域医療支援スキーム card indicates the subsidy tier that cardholders are entitled to, namely the 地域医療支援スキーム Blue, Orange or Green tier. All パイオニア世代 (PG) and ムルデカ世代 (MG) seniors also receive special subsidies at 地域医療支援スキーム clinics.

paragraphs.text: The amount that cardholders pay will be based on the fees charged by the clinic, less the CHAS subsidies. The fees will vary according to each patient’s condition and clinic’s charges.
paragraphs.textJP: The amount that cardholders pay will be based on the fees charged by the clinic, less the 地域医療支援スキーム subsidies. The fees will vary according to each patient’s condition and clinic’s charges.

paragraphs.text: Besides subsidies for care at CHAS clinics, CHAS, MG and PG cardholders enjoy subsidised referrals to public Specialist Outpatient Clinics (SOCs), where eligible. Subsidised referrals from CHAS Dental clinics to the National Dental Centre Singapore and National University Centre for Oral Health Singapore only apply to CHAS Blue/Orange, MG and PG cardholders.
paragraphs.textJP: Besides subsidies for care at 地域医療支援スキーム clinics, 地域医療支援スキーム, MG and PG cardholders enjoy subsidised referrals to public Specialist 外来 Clinics (SOCs), where eligible. Subsidised referrals from 地域医療支援スキーム Dental clinics to the National Dental Centre Singapore and National University Centre for Oral Health Singapore only apply to 地域医療支援スキーム Blue/Orange, MG and PG cardholders.

paragraphs.text: For patients with chronic conditions, CHAS subsidies complement the Chronic Disease Management Programme (CDMP), where MediSave can be used for outpatient treatment (for the same set of chronic conditions) covered under CHAS.
paragraphs.textJP: For patients with chronic conditions, 地域医療支援スキーム subsidies complement the Chronic Disease Management Programme (慢性疾患管理プログラム), where メディセーブ can be used for 外来 treatment (for the same set of chronic conditions) covered under 地域医療支援スキーム.

paragraphs.text: Eligible patients who seek treatment for their chronic conditions at CHAS GP clinics can also receive subsidised rates for healthcare services such as Diabetic Foot Screening (DFS), Diabetic Retinal Photography (DRP) and nurse counselling at Community Health Centres (CHCs) and Primary Care Network (PCN) providers.
paragraphs.textJP: Eligible patients who seek treatment for their chronic conditions at 地域医療支援スキーム GP clinics can also receive subsidised rates for healthcare services such as Diabetic Foot Screening (DFS), Diabetic Retinal Photography (DRP) and nurse counselling at Community Health Centres (CHCs) and Primary Care Network (PCN) providers.

paragraphs.text: Subsidies are also given to long-term care providers which offer healthcare services, such as renal dialysis, mental rehabilitation and nursing home care. MOH provides subsidies of up to 80% depending on the type of intermediate long-term care service required.
paragraphs.textJP: Subsidies are also given to long-term care providers which offer healthcare services, such as renal dialysis, mental rehabilitation and nursing home care. MOH（保健省） provides subsidies of up to 80% depending on the type of intermediate long-term care service required.

paragraphs.text: MediShield Life is a basic health insurance scheme that protects all Singapore Citizens (SCs) and Permanent Residents (PRs) against large medical bills for life, regardless of age or health conditions. By risk-pooling across the entire population, MediShield Life provides universal coverage for all SCs and PRs for life. The claim limits are designed to cover 9 in 10 subsidised bills incurred for hospitalisation in class B2/C wards, day surgeries, and selected costly outpatient treatments (e.g., dialysis and cancer drug treatments) in the public hospitals.
paragraphs.textJP: メディシールド・ライフ is a basic health insurance scheme that protects all Singapore Citizens (SCs) and Permanent Residents (PRs) against large medical bills for life, regardless of age or health conditions. By risk-pooling across the entire population, メディシールド・ライフ provides universal 補償 for all SCs and PRs for life. The 保険金請求 limits are designed to cover 9 in 10 subsidised bills incurred for 入院 in class B2/C wards, day surgeries, and selected costly 外来 treatments (e.g., dialysis and cancer drug treatments) in the public hospitals.

paragraphs.text: There is no need to apply for MediShield Life. All SCs and PRs are automatically enrolled in MediShield Life from 1 Nov 2015. New SCs are covered from birth or from the day they attain citizenship. PRs are covered from the day they attain permanent residency.
paragraphs.textJP: There is no need to apply for メディシールド・ライフ. All SCs and PRs are automatically enrolled in メディシールド・ライフ from 1 Nov 2015. New SCs are covered from birth or from the day they attain citizenship. PRs are covered from the day they attain permanent residency.

paragraphs.text: MediShield Life provides coverage for both subsidised and non-subsidised ward class hospitalisation at all public healthcare institutions and private medical institutions. However, MediShield Life claim limits are designed to cover 9 in 10 subsidised bills incurred for hospitalisation in class B2/C wards, day surgeries, and selected costly outpatient treatments (e.g., dialysis and cancer drug treatments in the public hospitals. This is to keep premiums affordable. Those who would like additional coverage in Class A/B1 wards or private hospitals may consider buying Integrated Shield Plans (IP). As IPs premiums are higher than MediShield Life premiums and increase with age, one should consider the affordability of premiums over the longer-term prior to purchasing an IP.
paragraphs.textJP: メディシールド・ライフ provides 補償 for both subsidised and non-subsidised ward class 入院 at all public healthcare institutions and private medical institutions. However, メディシールド・ライフ 保険金請求 limits are designed to cover 9 in 10 subsidised bills incurred for 入院 in class B2/C wards, day surgeries, and selected costly 外来 treatments (e.g., dialysis and cancer drug treatments in the public hospitals. This is to keep 保険料s affordable. Those who would like additional 補償 in Class A/B1 wards or private hospitals may consider buying Integrated Shield Plans (IP). As IPs 保険料s are higher than メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s and increase with age, one should consider the affordability of 保険料s over the longer-term prior to pur地域医療支援スキームing an IP.

paragraphs.text: MediShield Life premiums are actuarially priced based on the health risks and expected healthcare utilisation of each age group so that each age group’s payouts are broadly supported by their own premiums. This ensures that MediShield Life remains sustainable even as our population ages. Any applicable subsidies are automatically applied to the premium before premium payment.
paragraphs.textJP: メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s are actuarially priced based on the health risks and expected healthcare utilisation of each age group so that each age group’s payouts are broadly supported by their own 保険料s. This ensures that メディシールド・ライフ remains sustainable even as our population ages. Any applicable subsidies are automatically applied to the 保険料 before 保険料 payment.

paragraphs.text: Those with pre-existing medical conditions can enjoy coverage for their conditions. Only those with serious pre-existing medical conditions listed in the MOH website are required to pay Additional Premiums of 30% for the first 10 years of coverage, in addition to the standard MediShield Life premiums.
paragraphs.textJP: Those with pre-existing medical conditions can enjoy 補償 for their conditions. Only those with serious pre-existing medical conditions listed in the MOH（保健省） website are required to pay Additional 保険料s of 30% for the first 10 years of 補償, in addition to the standard メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s.

paragraphs.text: MediShield Life premiums can be fully paid by MediSave. One can pay premiums using their own MediSave or using their family members' MediSave. Parents may also tap on the MediSave Grant for Newborns to pay for their child’s MediShield Life premiums.
paragraphs.textJP: メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s can be fully paid by メディセーブ. One can pay 保険料s using their own メディセーブ or using their family members' メディセーブ. Parents may also tap on the メディセーブ Grant for Newborns to pay for their child’s メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s.

paragraphs.text: The Government provides various premium subsidies and support measures to help SCs and PRs with their MediShield Life premiums, including those who need to pay Additional Premiums: (a) Premium Subsidies for the lower to middle income; (b) Special Premium Subsidies and MediSave top-ups for Pioneer Generation seniors and Additional Subsidies for Merdeka Generation seniors; and (c) Additional Premium Support for those who, even after receiving subsidies and using MediSave are still unable to afford their premiums and have limited family support. No one will lose MediShield Life coverage due to an inability to pay premiums.
paragraphs.textJP: The Government provides various 保険料 subsidies and support measures to help SCs and PRs with their メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s, including those who need to pay Additional 保険料s: (a) 保険料 Subsidies for the lower to middle income; (b) Special 保険料 Subsidies and メディセーブ top-ups for パイオニア世代 seniors and Additional Subsidies for ムルデカ世代 seniors; and (c) Additional 保険料 Support for those who, even after receiving subsidies and using メディセーブ are still unable to afford their 保険料s and have limited family support. No one will lose メディシールド・ライフ 補償 due to an inability to pay 保険料s.

subsections.label: B1
subsections.title: Premium Subsidies for the lower- to middle- income

paragraphs.text: This is a targeted support measure to support lower- to middle- income families with household monthly income per person of S$2,800 and below, and living in homes with an Annual Value (AV) of S$21,000 and below. Those who own more than one property will not be eligible for this subsidy.
paragraphs.textJP: This is a targeted support measure to support lower- to middle- income families with household monthly income per person of S$2,800 and below, and living in homes with an Annual Value (AV) of S$21,000 and below. Those who own more than one property will not be eligible for this subsidy.

paragraphs.text: Those eligible can receive subsidies of up to 50% of their premiums. PRs will receive half the subsidy applicable to SCs.
paragraphs.textJP: Those eligible can receive subsidies of up to 50% of their 保険料s. PRs will receive half the subsidy applicable to SCs.

subsections.label: B2
subsections.title: Pioneer Generation Premium Subsidies and MediSave top-ups

paragraphs.text: Pioneers refer to Singapore Citizens born on or before 31 December 1949 and obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1986.
paragraphs.textJP: Pioneers refer to Singapore Citizens born on or before 31 December 1949 and obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1986.

paragraphs.text: Pioneers receive special Pioneer Generation Subsidies of between 40% - 60% depending on age. This is regardless of their household monthly income or the Annual Value of their home.
paragraphs.textJP: Pioneers receive special パイオニア世代 Subsidies of between 40% - 60% depending on age. This is regardless of their household monthly income or the Annual Value of their home.

paragraphs.text: Pioneers also receive S$250 - S$900 a year in MediSave top-ups (depending on year of birth) for life, which can be used to pay for their MediShield Life premiums.
paragraphs.textJP: Pioneers also receive S$250 - S$900 a year in メディセーブ top-ups (depending on year of birth) for life, which can be used to pay for their メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s.

paragraphs.text: With this, older Pioneers will continue to have their premiums fully covered by the special PG premium subsidies and MediSave top-ups, while younger Pioneers will have about two-thirds of their premiums covered.
paragraphs.textJP: With this, older Pioneers will continue to have their 保険料s fully covered by the special PG 保険料 subsidies and メディセーブ top-ups, while younger Pioneers will have about two-thirds of their 保険料s covered.

paragraphs.text: Additionally, older Pioneer Generation Seniors who have serious pre-existing conditions also receive additional MediSave top-ups of S$50 - S$200 annually from 2021 to 2025, which can be used to pay for their MediShield Life premiums.
paragraphs.textJP: Additionally, older パイオニア世代 Seniors who have serious 既存疾病s also receive additional メディセーブ top-ups of S$50 - S$200 annually from 2021 to 2025, which can be used to pay for their メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s.

subsections.label: B3
subsections.title: Merdeka Generation Premium Subsidies and MediSave top-ups

paragraphs.text: Merdeka Generation seniors refer to: (i) Singapore Citizens who were born from 1 January 1950 to 31 December 1959 and had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996; and (ii) those who were born on or before 31 December 1949, had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996 and did not receive the Pioneer Generation Package.
paragraphs.textJP: ムルデカ世代 seniors refer to: (i) Singapore Citizens who were born from 1 January 1950 to 31 December 1959 and had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996; and (ii) those who were born on or before 31 December 1949, had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996 and did not receive the パイオニア世代 Package.

paragraphs.text: From 1 July 2019, Merdeka Generation seniors receive additional Merdeka Generation Subsidies of 5% of their annual MediShield Life premiums, increasing to 10% after they turn 75 years old, regardless of their household monthly income or the Annual Value of their home. This is on top of the above Premium Subsidies that the seniors may receive.
paragraphs.textJP: From 1 July 2019, ムルデカ世代 seniors receive additional ムルデカ世代 Subsidies of 5% of their annual メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s, increasing to 10% after they turn 75 years old, regardless of their household monthly income or the Annual Value of their home. This is on top of the above 保険料 Subsidies that the seniors may receive.

paragraphs.text: Merdeka Generation members received S$200 a year in MediSave top-ups for five years from 2019 to 2023. These top-ups can be used to pay for their MediShield Life premiums.
paragraphs.textJP: ムルデカ世代 members received S$200 a year in メディセーブ top-ups for five years from 2019 to 2023. These top-ups can be used to pay for their メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s.

paragraphs.text: Individuals who are unable to afford MediShield Life premiums after Government subsidies, MediSave usage and have limited family support will be invited by the Government to apply for Additional Premium Support (APS). If approved, APS will cover the outstanding MediShield Life premiums (if any) as well as the premiums for the next two years. APS would also cover CareShield Life (CSHL) premiums if applicants are covered under CSHL. However, individuals will lose their APS if they purchase an Integrated Shield Plan or ElderShield/CSHL Supplement. This is to ensure that APS is targeted at members who are needy and have financial difficulties.
paragraphs.textJP: Individuals who are unable to afford メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s after Government subsidies, メディセーブ usage and have limited family support will be invited by the Government to apply for Additional 保険料 Support (APS). If approved, APS will cover the outstanding メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s (if any) as well as the 保険料s for the next two years. APS would also cover ケアシールド・ライフ (CSHL) 保険料s if applicants are covered under CSHL. However, individuals will lose their APS if they pur地域医療支援スキームe an Integrated Shield Plan or エルダーシールド/CSHL Supplement. This is to ensure that APS is targeted at members who are needy and have financial difficulties.

paragraphs.text: MediShield Life benefits are reviewed regularly to keep pace with the evolving healthcare landscape and cost inflation so that it remains sustainable and relevant to the needs of Singaporeans. Appendix 9B shows the MediShield Life benefits applicable for admissions or treatments received on or after 1 April 2023.
paragraphs.textJP: メディシールド・ライフ 給付s are reviewed regularly to keep pace with the evolving healthcare landscape and cost inflation so that it remains sustainable and relevant to the needs of Singaporeans. Appendix 9B shows the メディシールド・ライフ 給付s applicable for admissions or treatments received on or after 1 April 2023.

subsections.label: D
subsections.title: How Are MediShield Life Claim Payouts Computed?

paragraphs.text: The MediShield Life Scheme pays on a reimbursement basis, subject to the claim limits imposed on the covered medical expenses, as well as deductible, co-insurance and pro-ration factors.
paragraphs.textJP: The メディシールド・ライフ Scheme pays on a 実費精算・償還払い basis, subject to the 保険金請求 limits imposed on the covered medical expenses, as well as 免責金額, 共同保険 and 按分係数s.

paragraphs.text: The deductible is the fixed amount payable by the insured each policy year (the year following his policy renewal month), before the MediShield Life payout starts. The deductible is payable only once every policy year. It helps to filter out smaller, more affordable bills, which can be paid using MediSave and/or cash and helps to keep the premiums affordable by focusing MediShield Life payouts on larger bills where support is more critical.
paragraphs.textJP: The 免責金額 is the fixed amount payable by the 被保険者 each policy year (the year following his policy renewal month), before the メディシールド・ライフ payout starts. The 免責金額 is payable only once every policy year. It helps to filter out smaller, more affordable bills, which can be paid using メディセーブ and/or cash and helps to keep the 保険料s affordable by focusing メディシールド・ライフ payouts on larger bills where support is more critical.

paragraphs.text: Co-insurance is the patient’s share of the claimable amount which the insured will have to pay, along with the deductible. It decreases from 10% to 3% as the bill size increases. Co-insurance helps to prevent over-consumption of medical services and over-servicing by healthcare providers.
paragraphs.textJP: 共同保険 is the patient’s share of the 保険金請求able amount which the 被保険者 will have to pay, along with the 免責金額. It decreases from 10% to 3% as the bill size increases. 共同保険 helps to prevent over-consumption of medical services and over-servicing by healthcare providers.

paragraphs.text: As private hospital bills and Class A/B1 bills in public hospitals are generally higher than the Class B2/C bills that MediShield Life is designed for, they are pro-rated to the equivalent level of a Class B2/C bill before MediShield Life payouts are computed. This ensures comparable payouts for subsidised and private patients.
paragraphs.textJP: As private hospital bills and Class A/B1 bills in public hospitals are generally higher than the Class B2/C bills that メディシールド・ライフ is designed for, they are pro-rated to the equivalent level of a Class B2/C bill before メディシールド・ライフ payouts are computed. This ensures comparable payouts for subsidised and private patients.

paragraphs.text: Table 9.2 shows an example of how the reimbursement under MediShield Life is arrived at. Table 9.2: Claim Computation Of MediShield Life For Singapore Citizen Hospitalised In Class A Ward Claim computation for a Singapore Citizen aged 60 who stays in a Class A ward Length of Stay: 18 Days Procedure Performed: Hip Replacement Pro-ration Factor based on ward class 35% Hospital Bill 35% of Hospital Bill5 MediShield Life Claim Computation Daily Ward & Treatment Charges (18 days normal ward) S$12,000 S$4,200 (S$12,000 x 35%) S$4,2006 Surgical Procedure (Table 5C) S$8,500 S$2,975 (S$8,500 x 35%) S$2,1807 Implant S$4,000 S$1,400 (S$4,000 x 35%) S$1,4008 Total S$24,500 S$8,575 S$7,780 Less Deductible9 - - (S$2,000) Claimable Amount (less Deductible) - - S$5,780 Less Co-insurance - - (S$439)10 5 As the insured member stayed in Class A ward, the MediShield Life claim is computed based on 35% of the bill 6 Lower of the claim limit in Table A for Daily Ward & Treatment Charges, ($700 x 18 days) = $12,600, on 35% of charges incurred of $12,000 = $4,200. Therefore, the claimable amount is $4,200. 7 Lower of the claim limit in Table A for surgical procedure, $2,180 (Table 5C), or 35% of charges incurred of $8,500 = $2,975. Therefore, the claimable amount is $2,180. 8 Lower of the claim limit in Table A for Implants ($7,000 per treatment) or 35% of charges incurred of $4,000 = $1,400. Therefore, the claimable amount is $1,400. 9 The insured member is below 80 years old, subject to deductible of $2,000 for Class A ward. Medishield Life pays - - S$5,341 MediSave and/or Cash - - S$19,159
paragraphs.textJP: Table 9.2 shows an example of how the 実費精算・償還払い under メディシールド・ライフ is arrived at. Table 9.2: 保険金請求 Computation Of メディシールド・ライフ For Singapore Citizen Hospitalised In Class A Ward 保険金請求 computation for a Singapore Citizen aged 60 who stays in a Class A ward Length of Stay: 18 Days Procedure Performed: Hip Replacement 按分係数 based on ward class 35% Hospital Bill 35% of Hospital Bill5 メディシールド・ライフ 保険金請求 Computation Daily Ward & Treatment Charges (18 days normal ward) S$12,000 S$4,200 (S$12,000 x 35%) S$4,2006 Surgical Procedure (Table 5C) S$8,500 S$2,975 (S$8,500 x 35%) S$2,1807 Implant S$4,000 S$1,400 (S$4,000 x 35%) S$1,4008 Total S$24,500 S$8,575 S$7,780 Less 免責金額9 - - (S$2,000) 保険金請求able Amount (less 免責金額) - - S$5,780 Less 共同保険 - - (S$439)10 5 As the 被保険者 member stayed in Class A ward, the メディシールド・ライフ 保険金請求 is computed based on 35% of the bill 6 Lower of the 保険金請求 limit in Table A for Daily Ward & Treatment Charges, ($700 x 18 days) = $12,600, on 35% of charges incurred of $12,000 = $4,200. Therefore, the 保険金請求able amount is $4,200. 7 Lower of the 保険金請求 limit in Table A for surgical procedure, $2,180 (Table 5C), or 35% of charges incurred of $8,500 = $2,975. Therefore, the 保険金請求able amount is $2,180. 8 Lower of the 保険金請求 limit in Table A for Implants ($7,000 per treatment) or 35% of charges incurred of $4,000 = $1,400. Therefore, the 保険金請求able amount is $1,400. 9 The 被保険者 member is below 80 years old, subject to 免責金額 of $2,000 for Class A ward. メディシールド・ライフ pays - - S$5,341 メディセーブ and/or Cash - - S$19,159

paragraphs.text: Note from Table 9.2 that MediShield Life does not cover the majority of the Class A ward hospital bills. The insured’s hospital bill was pro-rated to the equivalent Class B2/C bill, before being subject to the claim limits. He may use his MediSave (subject to the MediSave limits, based on the number of days stayed in hospital and the surgical table relating to his surgical procedure) and/or cash to pay the remaining portion of his bill (S$19,159).
paragraphs.textJP: Note from Table 9.2 that メディシールド・ライフ does not cover the majority of the Class A ward hospital bills. The 被保険者’s hospital bill was pro-rated to the equivalent Class B2/C bill, before being subject to the 保険金請求 limits. He may use his メディセーブ (subject to the メディセーブ limits, based on the number of days stayed in hospital and the surgical table relating to his surgical procedure) and/or cash to pay the remaining portion of his bill (S$19,159).

paragraphs.text: Therefore, it is important for the insured to consider his insurance coverage carefully, before he chooses the hospital or ward to which he wishes to be admitted. If he wishes to have coverage for Class A/B1 ward and private hospital stays, he may apply to any of the approved insurers for an IP.
paragraphs.textJP: Therefore, it is important for the 被保険者 to consider his insurance 補償 carefully, before he chooses the hospital or ward to which he wishes to be admitted. If he wishes to have 補償 for Class A/B1 ward and private hospital stays, he may apply to any of the approved 保険者s for an IP.

paragraphs.text: Exclusions are imposed on certain medical treatments and expenses. For such expenses incurred, the insured will not be able to claim under his MediShield Life.
paragraphs.textJP: 免責事由s are imposed on certain medical treatments and expenses. For such expenses incurred, the 被保険者 will not be able to 保険金請求 under his メディシールド・ライフ.

paragraphs.text: The following treatment items, procedures, conditions, activities are not covered by MediShield Life and cannot be claimed (Applicable for admissions or treatments received on or after 1 March 2021): (a) Treatment for any contraceptive operation or procedure, infertility, subfertility, or assisted conception or sex re-assignment surgery; (b) Surgical interventions for the following conditions: Trisomy 13, Bilateral Renal Agenesis, Bart’s Hydrops and Anecephaly; (c) Cosmetic surgery; (d) Dental work (except due to accidental injuries); (e) Outright purchase of kidney dialysis machines, iron-lung and other special appliances; (f) Optional items such as television, telephone, special requested meals and other items which are not necessary for the treatment of any illness, condition or any injury or disability; (g) Private nursing charges; (h) Ambulance services; (i) Vaccination; (j) Treatment for injuries and disablement resulting from insured person’s own criminal act; (k) Treatment of injuries arising directly or indirectly from nuclear fallout, war and related risk; 10 Co-insurance = ($3,000 x 10%) + ($2,780 x 5%) = $439. (l) Treatment of injuries arising from direct participation in civil commotion, riot or strike; (m) Expenses incurred after the 7th calendar day from being certified to be medically fit for discharge from inpatient treatment and assessed to have a feasible discharge option by a medical practitioner; (n) Overseas medical treatment; (o) Treatment which has received reimbursement from Workmen’s Compensation and other forms of insurance coverage; and (p) Maternity charges (including Caesarean operations) or abortions, including their related complications, except treatments for serious complications related to pregnancy and childbirth.
paragraphs.textJP: The following treatment items, procedures, conditions, activities are not covered by メディシールド・ライフ and cannot be 保険金請求ed (Applicable for admissions or treatments received on or after 1 March 2021): (a) Treatment for any contraceptive operation or procedure, infertility, subfertility, or assisted conception or sex re-assignment surgery; (b) Surgical interventions for the following conditions: Trisomy 13, Bilateral Renal Agenesis, Bart’s Hydrops and Anecephaly; (c) Cosmetic surgery; (d) Dental work (except due to accidental injuries); (e) Outright pur地域医療支援スキームe of kidney dialysis machines, iron-lung and other special appliances; (f) Optional items such as television, telephone, special requested meals and other items which are not necessary for the treatment of any illness, condition or any injury or disability; (g) Private nursing charges; (h) Ambulance services; (i) Vaccination; (j) Treatment for injuries and disablement resulting from 被保険者 person’s own criminal act; (k) Treatment of injuries arising directly or indirectly from nuclear fallout, war and related risk; 10 共同保険 = ($3,000 x 10%) + ($2,780 x 5%) = $439. (l) Treatment of injuries arising from direct participation in civil commotion, riot or strike; (m) Expenses incurred after the 7th calendar day from being certified to be medically fit for discharge from 入院患者 treatment and assessed to have a feasible discharge option by a medical practitioner; (n) Overseas medical treatment; (o) Treatment which has received 実費精算・償還払い from Workmen’s Compensation and other forms of insurance 補償; and (p) Maternity charges (including Caesarean operations) or abortions, including their related complications, except treatments for serious complications related to pregnancy and childbirth.

paragraphs.text: The MediShield Life Scheme is a non-profit scheme. All premiums collected are placed in the MediShield Life Fund and are used solely for the benefit of policyholders and in the administration of the scheme. The financial accounts for the Fund are audited by an external auditor and submitted to Parliament every year. 4. INTEGRATED SHIELD PLANS (IPs)
paragraphs.textJP: The メディシールド・ライフ Scheme is a non-profit scheme. All 保険料s collected are placed in the メディシールド・ライフ Fund and are used solely for the 給付 of 保険契約者s and in the administration of the scheme. The financial accounts for the Fund are audited by an external auditor and submitted to Parliament every year. 4. INTEGRATED SHIELD PLANS (IPs)

paragraphs.text: Integrated Shield Plans (IPs) are MediSave-approved hospitalisation insurance plans made up of two parts. The first is the MediShield Life component administered by the CPF Board. The second is an additional private insurance coverage component administered by approved private insurers, typically to cover Class A/B1 wards of public hospitals or private hospitals. Following the implementation of MediShield Life on 1 November 2015, MediShield Life automatically replaced the MediShield component of IPs. Thus, those who have IPs are already covered by MediShield Life, and there is no duplication of coverage between IPs and MediShield Life.
paragraphs.textJP: Integrated Shield Plans (IPs) are メディセーブ-approved 入院 insurance plans made up of two parts. The first is the メディシールド・ライフ component administered by the CPF（中央積立基金） Board. The second is an additional private insurance 補償 component administered by approved private 保険者s, typically to cover Class A/B1 wards of public hospitals or private hospitals. Following the implementation of メディシールド・ライフ on 1 November 2015, メディシールド・ライフ automatically replaced the MediShield component of IPs. Thus, those who have IPs are already covered by メディシールド・ライフ, and there is no duplication of 補償 between IPs and メディシールド・ライフ.

paragraphs.text: The following plans are IPs (as at 1 Apr 2023): AIA HealthShield Gold Max, Singlife Shield, HSBC Life Shield, Great Eastern SupremeHealth, Income Insurance Limited’s Enhanced IncomeShield, Prudential’s PRUShield and RHI’s Raffles Shield. To aid your understanding of the different IPs offered, you are advised to refer to the updated comparison tables of IPs across insurers, categorised by ward class, at the MOH Website11.
paragraphs.textJP: The following plans are IPs (as at 1 Apr 2023): AIA HealthShield Gold Max, Singlife Shield, HSBC Life Shield, Great Eastern SupremeHealth, Income Insurance Limited’s Enhanced IncomeShield, Prudential’s PRUShield and RHI’s Raffles Shield. To aid your understanding of the different IPs offered, you are advised to refer to the updated comparison tables of IPs across 保険者s, categorised by ward class, at the MOH（保健省） Website11.

paragraphs.text: The figure below illustrates the components of Integrated Shield Plans (IPs) as outlined above. 11 https://www.moh.gov.sg/cost-financing/healthcare-schemes-subsidies/medishield-life/comparison-ofintegrated- shield-plans With the different IPs available, it is necessary for the sellers of such plans to understand the different plans offered in the insurance market, and to advise their prospective clients, based on the needs and long-term affordability of each individual client’s position. (a) As part of due diligence, clients should be informed of the key differences in premiums, benefits, exclusions, deductibles, coinsurance and other co-payment features between the various IPs offered by their insurer, versus MediShield Life. There are different benefits provided in the IP policy, and plans can be “as charged” or “non-as-charged” (i.e. they have sub-limits imposed). Information provided should be easy to understand, unambiguous and clearly explained to help clients make an informed decision. (b) Clients should consider if they would wish to stay in a private or subsidised ward in a public hospital, or in a private hospital, and if they wish to choose their own doctors. To aid them in this decision, clients should be informed of how a given plan’s coverage and their copayment might differ according to their choice of ward, hospital, and doctor. Where possible, clients should be reminded of these considerations prior to seeking any treatment, including the need to confirm the panel status of their medical provider if they prefer to access panel benefits. Subsidised treatments in public hospitals (B2/C wards) currently make up around two-thirds of all admissions from Singapore residents. For the vast majority of these treatments, MediShield Life provides sufficient coverage, meaning that having an IP plan would not provide any additional payout. As such, clients may not need to purchase an IP if they intend to stay in Class B2/C wards. (c) Premiums for IPs are higher than for MediShield Life. Premiums for health insurance usually increase with age and are subject to change. IP premiums may thus increase significantly as an insured person gets older. The private insurance component of the IP premiums could be up to 5 times the MediShield Life premiums for those in their late-70s or early-80s this year12. Clients should consider if they can afford their IP premiums in the long term by referring to www.go.gov.sg/mohcompare- ip which contains a comparison of indicative lifetime premiums and benefits across different IPs. (d) Clients who pay for, or are insured under IPs are not eligible for APS. If clients are currently receiving APS to pay their MediShield Life and/or CareShield Life premiums, they will stop receiving APS if they choose to be insured under an IP. In addition, if a client chooses to be insured under an IP, the person paying for the client’s IP will stop receiving APS, if he or she is currently receiving APS. (e) The additional private insurance component of IP premiums is payable by MediSave only up to the Additional Withdrawal Limit (AWL), which is often exceeded at older ages. The policyholders may have to pay a substantial portion of their IP premiums in cash, especially at the older ages. (f) Unlike MediShield Life which provides coverage for pre-existing medical conditions (with Additional Premiums for the first 10 years), the coverage for the private insurance component of the IP may be declined or imposed with exclusions for pre-existing conditions. The private insurance component may also have premium loading, arising from pre-existing conditions. Clients should be advised to disclose their medical history truthfully and completely, to avoid unexpected policy exclusions subsequently. Once their policy has been approved, clients should be made to understand their full list of policy exclusions from their insurer or agent, and be advised of their free-look period where they can review their purchase and obtain a full refund of premiums if they choose to cancel their policy. Where possible, clients should be reminded again of their exclusions prior to any treatment, procedure or admission, including both general exclusions and specific medical exclusions of pre-existing conditions.
paragraphs.textJP: The figure below illustrates the components of Integrated Shield Plans (IPs) as outlined above. 11 https://www.MOH（保健省）.gov.sg/cost-financing/healthcare-schemes-subsidies/medishield-life/comparison-ofintegrated- shield-plans With the different IPs available, it is necessary for the sellers of such plans to understand the different plans offered in the insurance market, and to advise their prospective clients, based on the needs and long-term affordability of each individual client’s position. (a) As part of due diligence, clients should be informed of the key differences in 保険料s, 給付s, 免責事由s, 免責金額s, coinsurance and other 自己負担 features between the various IPs offered by their 保険者, versus メディシールド・ライフ. There are different 給付s provided in the IP policy, and plans can be “実費払い” or “non-as-charged” (i.e. they have 支払限度額（サブリミット）s imposed). Information provided should be easy to understand, unambiguous and clearly explained to help clients make an informed decision. (b) Clients should consider if they would wish to stay in a private or subsidised ward in a public hospital, or in a private hospital, and if they wish to choose their own doctors. To aid them in this decision, clients should be informed of how a given plan’s 補償 and their copayment might differ according to their choice of ward, hospital, and doctor. Where possible, clients should be reminded of these considerations prior to seeking any treatment, including the need to confirm the panel status of their medical provider if they prefer to access panel 給付s. Subsidised treatments in public hospitals (B2/C wards) currently make up around two-thirds of all admissions from Singapore residents. For the vast majority of these treatments, メディシールド・ライフ provides sufficient 補償, meaning that having an IP plan would not provide any additional payout. As such, clients may not need to pur地域医療支援スキームe an IP if they intend to stay in Class B2/C wards. (c) 保険料s for IPs are higher than for メディシールド・ライフ. 保険料s for health insurance usually increase with age and are subject to change. IP 保険料s may thus increase significantly as an 被保険者 person gets older. The private insurance component of the IP 保険料s could be up to 5 times the メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s for those in their late-70s or early-80s this year12. Clients should consider if they can afford their IP 保険料s in the long term by referring to www.go.gov.sg/MOH（保健省）compare- ip which contains a comparison of indicative lifetime 保険料s and 給付s across different IPs. (d) Clients who pay for, or are 被保険者 under IPs are not eligible for APS. If clients are currently receiving APS to pay their メディシールド・ライフ and/or ケアシールド・ライフ 保険料s, they will stop receiving APS if they choose to be 被保険者 under an IP. In addition, if a client chooses to be 被保険者 under an IP, the person paying for the client’s IP will stop receiving APS, if he or she is currently receiving APS. (e) The additional private insurance component of IP 保険料s is payable by メディセーブ only up to the 追加引出限度額, which is often exceeded at older ages. The 保険契約者s may have to pay a substantial portion of their IP 保険料s in cash, especially at the older ages. (f) Unlike メディシールド・ライフ which provides 補償 for pre-existing medical conditions (with Additional 保険料s for the first 10 years), the 補償 for the private insurance component of the IP may be declined or imposed with 免責事由s for 既存疾病s. The private insurance component may also have 保険料 loading, arising from 既存疾病s. Clients should be advised to disclose their medical history truthfully and completely, to avoid unexpected policy 免責事由s subsequently. Once their policy has been approved, clients should be made to understand their full list of policy 免責事由s from their 保険者 or agent, and be advised of their クーリングオフ期間 where they can review their pur地域医療支援スキームe and obtain a full refund of 保険料s if they choose to cancel their policy. Where possible, clients should be reminded again of their 免責事由s prior to any treatment, procedure or admission, including both general 免責事由s and specific medical 免責事由s of 既存疾病s.

paragraphs.text: Under the integrated structure of IPs, if a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident purchases an IP, he will enjoy the benefits of MediShield Life within his IP. However, he will be serviced by his private insurer. In other words, he will continue to pay one premium and needs to make only one claim with just his private insurer. His private insurer will then arrange with the CPF Board on the amount claimable under the MediShield Life component of the plan. The insured will not need to liaise with both parties (i.e. his insurer and the CPF Board). 12 Accurate as of August 2024
paragraphs.textJP: Under the integrated structure of IPs, if a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident pur地域医療支援スキームes an IP, he will enjoy the 給付s of メディシールド・ライフ within his IP. However, he will be serviced by his private 保険者. In other words, he will continue to pay one 保険料 and needs to make only one 保険金請求 with just his private 保険者. His private 保険者 will then arrange with the CPF（中央積立基金） Board on the amount 保険金請求able under the メディシールド・ライフ component of the plan. The 被保険者 will not need to liaise with both parties (i.e. his 保険者 and the CPF（中央積立基金） Board). 12 Accurate as of August 2024

paragraphs.text: As mentioned above, a person insured with an IP already benefits from the MediShield Life component within his IP. He will have coverage for preexisting conditions and be assured of lifelong coverage under the MediShield Life component of his IP. This is so, even if the condition is excluded from the additional private insurance coverage by the IP insurer.
paragraphs.textJP: As mentioned above, a person 被保険者 with an IP already 給付s from the メディシールド・ライフ component within his IP. He will have 補償 for preexisting conditions and be assured of lifelong 補償 under the メディシールド・ライフ component of his IP. This is so, even if the condition is excluded from the additional private insurance 補償 by the IP 保険者.

paragraphs.text: The IP premiums paid to private insurers also comprise two parts, namely the premium for the MediShield Life component, and the premiums for the additional private insurance component. A person insured under an IP will also be able to receive the applicable MediShield Life subsidies (i.e. Premium Subsidies for lower- to middle-income; Pioneer and Merdeka Generation Subsidies; Transitional Subsidies) if he meets the eligibility criteria, but will not be eligible for Additional Premium Support. Those who cannot afford to pay for IP premiums should remain on MediShield Life.
paragraphs.textJP: The IP 保険料s paid to private 保険者s also comprise two parts, namely the 保険料 for the メディシールド・ライフ component, and the 保険料s for the additional private insurance component. A person 被保険者 under an IP will also be able to receive the applicable メディシールド・ライフ subsidies (i.e. 保険料 Subsidies for lower- to middle-income; Pioneer and ムルデカ世代 Subsidies; Transitional Subsidies) if he meets the eligibility criteria, but will not be eligible for Additional 保険料 Support. Those who cannot afford to pay for IP 保険料s should remain on メディシールド・ライフ.

paragraphs.text: When deciding on the level of premiums for the additional private insurance component, private insurers will also need to factor in claims experience and medical inflation along with the benefits offered. Clients concerned with the affordability of IP premiums should first consider more affordable insurance options if it is within their means, such as IPs with a lower ward entitlement. They could also consider whether MediShield Life (which provides sufficient coverage for the vast majority of subsidised treatments (e.g., B2/C wards) has adequately met their needs.
paragraphs.textJP: When deciding on the level of 保険料s for the additional private insurance component, private 保険者s will also need to factor in 保険金請求s experience and 医療インフレ along with the 給付s offered. Clients concerned with the affordability of IP 保険料s should first consider more affordable insurance options if it is within their means, such as IPs with a lower ward entitlement. They could also consider whether メディシールド・ライフ (which provides sufficient 補償 for the vast majority of subsidised treatments (e.g., B2/C wards) has adequately met their needs.

paragraphs.text: The main differences between the MediShield Life Scheme and an IP are described in Table 9.3 below. Table 9.3: Differences Between MediShield Life & IP MediShield Life Scheme Integrated Shield Plan (IP) (a) It is administered by the CPF Board. (a) It is administered by private insurers, as approved by MOH. (b) Its coverage is sized for stays in Class B2/C wards of public hospitals. (b) It provides for enhanced coverage beyond MediShield Life, with various plan types available for stays in private hospitals and/or Class A/B1 wards of public hospitals. (c) It covers all pre-existing conditions. (c) Coverage for the private insurance component of the IP may be declined or imposed with exclusions and/or premium loading, arising from pre-existing conditions. (d) There is no minimum or maximum age limit. (d) It may have a minimum or maximum entry age limit. (e) There are sub-limits applicable. (e) It may have sub-limits, although most do not have sub-limits. (f) MediShield Life premiums are fully payable by MediSave. (f) The MediShield Life component of the IP is fully payable by MediSave, while the private insurance component of the IP is payable by MediSave only up to Additional Withdrawal Limit (AWL), and the remainder has to be paid in cash. (g) All Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents are automatically covered for life. No application is required. (g) Application to private insurers is required.
paragraphs.textJP: The main differences between the メディシールド・ライフ Scheme and an IP are described in Table 9.3 below. Table 9.3: Differences Between メディシールド・ライフ & IP メディシールド・ライフ Scheme 統合シールドプラン (a) It is administered by the CPF（中央積立基金） Board. (a) It is administered by private 保険者s, as approved by MOH（保健省）. (b) Its 補償 is sized for stays in Class B2/C wards of public hospitals. (b) It provides for enhanced 補償 beyond メディシールド・ライフ, with various plan types available for stays in private hospitals and/or Class A/B1 wards of public hospitals. (c) It covers all 既存疾病s. (c) 補償 for the private insurance component of the IP may be declined or imposed with 免責事由s and/or 保険料 loading, arising from 既存疾病s. (d) There is no minimum or maximum age limit. (d) It may have a minimum or maximum entry age limit. (e) There are 支払限度額（サブリミット）s applicable. (e) It may have 支払限度額（サブリミット）s, although most do not have 支払限度額（サブリミット）s. (f) メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s are fully payable by メディセーブ. (f) The メディシールド・ライフ component of the IP is fully payable by メディセーブ, while the private insurance component of the IP is payable by メディセーブ only up to 追加引出限度額, and the remainder has to be paid in cash. (g) All Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents are automatically covered for life. No application is required. (g) Application to private 保険者s is required.

paragraphs.text: The Government has worked with IP insurers to develop the Standard IP, a “no frills” product targeted at covering large Class B1 ward hospital bills and selected outpatient treatments.
paragraphs.textJP: The Government has worked with IP 保険者s to develop the スタンダードIP, a “no frills” product targeted at covering large Class B1 ward hospital bills and selected 外来 treatments.

paragraphs.text: From 1 May 2016, all IP insurers were required to sell the Standard IP. The benefits of the Standard IP are regulated by MOH and are identical across all IP insurers. The Standard IP has claim limits sized to fully cover nine out of ten Class B1 bills. Like other IPs, it also has co-insurance and deductible components.
paragraphs.textJP: From 1 May 2016, all IP 保険者s were required to sell the スタンダードIP. The 給付s of the スタンダードIP are regulated by MOH（保健省） and are identical across all IP 保険者s. The スタンダードIP has 保険金請求 limits sized to fully cover nine out of ten Class B1 bills. Like other IPs, it also has 共同保険 and 免責金額 components.

paragraphs.text: As a private plan that provides coverage beyond MediShield Life, the Standard IP is managed by insurers. Like all other IPs, premiums and underwriting decisions for the plan are determined by each insurer, based on their own commercial considerations and risk assessment frameworks. Therefore, premiums vary, depending on each insurer’s approach.
paragraphs.textJP: As a private plan that provides 補償 beyond メディシールド・ライフ, the スタンダードIP is managed by 保険者s. Like all other IPs, 保険料s and 引受審査 decisions for the plan are determined by each 保険者, based on their own commercial considerations and risk assessment frameworks. Therefore, 保険料s vary, depending on each 保険者’s approach.

paragraphs.text: Nonetheless, the Standard IP is an affordable option for Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents who want additional coverage beyond MediShield Life and may find other higher coverage IPs too expensive. It is also a viable option for those who want to switch from their Class A and Private Hospital IPs to a more affordable plan.
paragraphs.textJP: Nonetheless, the スタンダードIP is an affordable option for Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents who want additional 補償 beyond メディシールド・ライフ and may find other higher 補償 IPs too expensive. It is also a viable option for those who want to switch from their Class A and Private Hospital IPs to a more affordable plan.

paragraphs.text: MediSave can be used to pay premiums for IPs, either in full or in part, depending on the age group and the IP plan type. The MediShield Life component of the IP premiums is fully payable by MediSave. This takes into account any Additional Premiums (for those with serious pre-existing conditions) and MediShield Life subsidies (where applicable). From 1 November 2015, MediSave use for the additional private insurance component of the IP premiums is subject to the new Additional Withdrawal Limit (AWL). The AWL is set per insured person per policy year, as stated below: (a) Age Next Birthday (ANB) 40 years and below: S$300 (b) ANB 41 to 70 years: S$600 (c) ANB 71 years and above: S$900
paragraphs.textJP: メディセーブ can be used to pay 保険料s for IPs, either in full or in part, depending on the age group and the IP plan type. The メディシールド・ライフ component of the IP 保険料s is fully payable by メディセーブ. This takes into account any Additional 保険料s (for those with serious 既存疾病s) and メディシールド・ライフ subsidies (where applicable). From 1 November 2015, メディセーブ use for the additional private insurance component of the IP 保険料s is subject to the new 追加引出限度額. The AWL is set per 被保険者 person per policy year, as stated below: (a) Age Next Birthday (ANB) 40 years and below: S$300 (b) ANB 41 to 70 years: S$600 (c) ANB 71 years and above: S$900

paragraphs.text: IP insurers also sell rider plans, which pay for the co-insurance and deductible portion of the IP, subject to minimum 5% co-payment. These riders are not approved for MediSave use, and insurers are not allowed to market riders as part of IPs. Sellers of such riders should highlight the distinction between IPs and riders to policyholders and share that riders can be paid using only cash. Importantly, they should remind policyholders that rider premiums rise significantly with age. Sellers should highlight that only about half of patients with IP plus rider protection end up using subsidised public healthcare when hospitalised or receiving day surgery, despite MediShield Life being generally sufficient for such settings. Instead of paying for a rider, the deductible and coinsurance portion incurred in the event of hospitalisation can be saved for and paid via MediSave or cash.
paragraphs.textJP: IP 保険者s also sell 特約 plans, which pay for the 共同保険 and 免責金額 portion of the IP, subject to minimum 5% 自己負担. These 特約s are not approved for メディセーブ use, and 保険者s are not allowed to market 特約s as part of IPs. Sellers of such 特約s should highlight the distinction between IPs and 特約s to 保険契約者s and share that 特約s can be paid using only cash. Importantly, they should remind 保険契約者s that 特約 保険料s rise significantly with age. Sellers should highlight that only about half of patients with IP plus 特約 protection end up using subsidised public healthcare when hospitalised or receiving day surgery, despite メディシールド・ライフ being generally sufficient for such settings. Instead of paying for a 特約, the 免責金額 and coinsurance portion incurred in the event of 入院 can be saved for and paid via メディセーブ or cash.

paragraphs.text: On 8 March 2018, MOH introduced requirements for new IP riders to have: (a) a co-payment of 5% or more, to encourage prudent use and appropriate charging of healthcare services, and (b) a co-payment cap for panel/pre-authorised treatment to protect policyholders against the unlikely event of large bills. Insurers can set the cap at S$3,000 or more per policy year.
paragraphs.textJP: On 8 March 2018, MOH（保健省） introduced requirements for new IP 特約s to have: (a) a 自己負担 of 5% or more, to encourage prudent use and appropriate charging of healthcare services, and (b) a 自己負担 cap for panel/pre-authorised treatment to protect 保険契約者s against the unlikely event of large bills. 保険者s can set the cap at S$3,000 or more per policy year.

paragraphs.text: These requirements apply to new policyholders who purchase riders on and after 8 March 2018.
paragraphs.textJP: These requirements apply to new 保険契約者s who pur地域医療支援スキームe 特約s on and after 8 March 2018.

subsections.label: D1
subsections.title: The Principle Underlying the Need For Co-Payments13

paragraphs.text: Co-payment is an important principle in the design of healthcare insurance. It encourages policyholders and their doctors, to consider the necessity of the medical treatment and its cost, so that they can make an informed decision on the appropriate healthcare services. This encourages prudence and keeps healthcare cost, and health insurance premiums, affordable and sustainable in the long term.
paragraphs.textJP: 自己負担 is an important principle in the design of healthcare insurance. It encourages 保険契約者s and their doctors, to consider the necessity of the medical treatment and its cost, so that they can make an informed decision on the appropriate healthcare services. This encourages prudence and keeps healthcare cost, and health insurance 保険料s, affordable and sustainable in the long term.

paragraphs.text: This principle applies regardless of hospital category. Full riders that covered the entire co-payment under the IPs were not in line with this principle. Such riders allowed policyholders to avoid co-payment regardless of their bill size, and had contributed to over-consumption, over-servicing and over-charging.
paragraphs.textJP: This principle applies regardless of hospital category. Full 特約s that covered the entire 自己負担 under the IPs were not in line with this principle. Such 特約s allowed 保険契約者s to avoid 自己負担 regardless of their bill size, and had contributed to over-consumption, over-servicing and over-charging.

paragraphs.text: This was one of the reasons why MOH had announced the requirement for a minimum 5% co-payment for new riders across all settings in March 13 Partially extracted and adapted from https://www.moh.gov.sg/news-highlights/details/co-payment-inintegrated- shield-plans-for-specific-hospital-categories-and-breakdown-of-average-claims-made-throughfull- riders-of-integrated-shield-plans. 2018. This applied for new riders sold from 1 April 2019. Additionally, riders sold after the announcement, between 8 March 2018 and 31 March 2019, had to transition to these new co-payment riders by 1 April 2021.
paragraphs.textJP: This was one of the reasons why MOH（保健省） had announced the requirement for a minimum 5% 自己負担 for new 特約s across all settings in March 13 Partially extracted and adapted from https://www.MOH（保健省）.gov.sg/news-highlights/details/自己負担-inintegrated- shield-plans-for-specific-hospital-categories-and-breakdown-of-average-保険金請求s-made-throughfull- 特約s-of-integrated-shield-plans. 2018. This applied for new 特約s sold from 1 April 2019. Additionally, 特約s sold after the announcement, between 8 March 2018 and 31 March 2019, had to transition to these new 自己負担 特約s by 1 April 2021.

paragraphs.text: While this co-payment requirement was not mandated for riders purchased before 8 March 2018, some insurers announced that they will also be including a co-payment component to these riders. This is allowed under their contractual terms with their policyholders. MOH has encouraged the insurers, in considering these changes, to ensure that their policyholders’ interests are safeguarded even as insurers seek to ensure the sustainability of their portfolios. As an additional safeguard, insurers are required to clearly explain these changes to their policyholders at least 30 days before they take effect.
paragraphs.textJP: While this 自己負担 requirement was not mandated for 特約s pur地域医療支援スキームed before 8 March 2018, some 保険者s announced that they will also be including a 自己負担 component to these 特約s. This is allowed under their contractual terms with their 保険契約者s. MOH（保健省） has encouraged the 保険者s, in considering these changes, to ensure that their 保険契約者s’ interests are safeguarded even as 保険者s seek to ensure the sustainability of their portfolios. As an additional safeguard, 保険者s are required to clearly explain these changes to their 保険契約者s at least 30 days before they take effect.

paragraphs.text: To provide their policyholders more assurance after these changes, some insurers had also introduced co-payment limits for treatments that are provided by their panel doctors, or for pre-authorised treatments. However, even if policyholders do not seek treatment with a panel doctor or are unable to seek pre-authorisation prior to treatment, they may still claim under their IP and rider, though additional benefits such as the co-payment limit may not apply. Policyholders can also continue to tap on MediSave to pay the copayment amount under their riders, subject to the MediSave withdrawal limits.
paragraphs.textJP: To provide their 保険契約者s more assurance after these changes, some 保険者s had also introduced 自己負担 limits for treatments that are provided by their panel doctors, or for pre-authorised treatments. However, even if 保険契約者s do not seek treatment with a panel doctor or are unable to seek 事前承認 prior to treatment, they may still 保険金請求 under their IP and 特約, though additional 給付s such as the 自己負担 limit may not apply. 保険契約者s can also continue to tap on メディセーブ to pay the copayment amount under their 特約s, subject to the メディセーブ withdrawal limits.

paragraphs.text: An IP policyholder who cannot afford, or does not wish to continue paying the premium for his IP, can switch his plan to a lower coverage plan within the same insurer at any point in time, without additional underwriting involved. He can also terminate his IP at any point in time, and still be covered by MediShield Life which provides basic health insurance, regardless of any preexisting conditions.
paragraphs.textJP: An IP 保険契約者 who cannot afford, or does not wish to continue paying the 保険料 for his IP, can switch his plan to a lower 補償 plan within the same 保険者 at any point in time, without additional 引受審査 involved. He can also terminate his IP at any point in time, and still be covered by メディシールド・ライフ which provides basic health insurance, regardless of any preexisting conditions.

paragraphs.text: In the event that the person with an existing IP switches to a new IP with another insurer, his original IP with the previous insurer will be automatically terminated. Moreover, under the new IP with the new insurer, he will undergo underwriting again. As such, he may lose coverage for his existing medical conditions covered by his original plan with his existing insurer and may not be able to re-apply for his original coverage subsequently.
paragraphs.textJP: In the event that the person with an existing IP switches to a new IP with another 保険者, his original IP with the previous 保険者 will be automatically terminated. Moreover, under the new IP with the new 保険者, he will undergo 引受審査 again. As such, he may lose 補償 for his existing medical conditions covered by his original plan with his existing 保険者 and may not be able to re-apply for his original 補償 subsequently.

paragraphs.text: Any policyholder who switches his IP from one insurer to another will have the option to go back to the previous insurer within 30 days from the date of notification of termination given to the previous insurer. No re-underwriting is allowed by the previous insurer, and it would be as if the first IP had never been terminated.
paragraphs.textJP: Any 保険契約者 who switches his IP from one 保険者 to another will have the option to go back to the previous 保険者 within 30 days from the date of notification of termination given to the previous 保険者. No re-引受審査 is allowed by the previous 保険者, and it would be as if the first IP had never been terminated.

paragraphs.text: If a claim is received by the new insurer and the claim incurred date falls within 30 days from the commencement date of the new policy and the new insurer is not able to admit the claim for any reason, the new insurer has the option to request the policyholder to reinstate his policy with the previous insurer. The previous insurer will take the claim incurred date (or claim event date) to be the date of request to reinstate the policy. Once reinstated, the claim will be assessed by the previous insurer, and the claim will be paid by the previous insurer if the liability under the previous insurer’s policy is established.
paragraphs.textJP: If a 保険金請求 is received by the new 保険者 and the 保険金請求 incurred date falls within 30 days from the commencement date of the new policy and the new 保険者 is not able to admit the 保険金請求 for any reason, the new 保険者 has the option to request the 保険契約者 to reinstate his policy with the previous 保険者. The previous 保険者 will take the 保険金請求 incurred date (or 保険金請求 event date) to be the date of request to reinstate the policy. Once reinstated, the 保険金請求 will be assessed by the previous 保険者, and the 保険金請求 will be paid by the previous 保険者 if the liability under the previous 保険者’s policy is established.

paragraphs.text: With effect from the launch of MediShield Life, MOH allows IP insurers to riskload insured members with pre-existing conditions for the private insurance component of the IPs. If offered, this could help to reduce uncertainty of coverage owing to exclusions. It also affords insured members who prefer to pay higher private insurance premiums the peace of mind, knowing that they are also covered for their pre-existing conditions by the private insurance component of their IPs. More details on this will be shared by private insurers if and when they decide to offer risk-loading to their insured members.
paragraphs.textJP: With effect from the launch of メディシールド・ライフ, MOH（保健省） allows IP 保険者s to riskload 被保険者 members with 既存疾病s for the private insurance component of the IPs. If offered, this could help to reduce uncertainty of 補償 owing to 免責事由s. It also affords 被保険者 members who prefer to pay higher private insurance 保険料s the peace of mind, knowing that they are also covered for their 既存疾病s by the private insurance component of their IPs. More details on this will be shared by private 保険者s if and when they decide to offer risk-loading to their 被保険者 members.

paragraphs.text: The LIA has issued a guidance paper on Pre-Authorisation Process for IPs. Preauthorisation is a service where the IP insurer gives prior approval to the hospitalisation and/or medical treatment and associated costs before the actual surgery or admission. It is designed for elective treatments in the private healthcare sector, for patients (policyholders) seeking specialist care by private doctors in private hospitals.
paragraphs.textJP: The LIA has issued a guidance paper on 事前承認 Process for IPs. Preauthorisation is a service where the IP 保険者 gives prior approval to the 入院 and/or medical treatment and associated costs before the actual surgery or admission. It is designed for elective treatments in the private healthcare sector, for patients (保険契約者s) seeking specialist care by private doctors in private hospitals.

paragraphs.text: The benefits of Pre-authorisation are: (a) Insurers can assess the medical necessity and cost of treatment to ensure it is within the terms and conditions of the policy’s coverage; (b) Patients will have a peace of mind in knowing that their procedure is within their insurance coverage; and (c) Healthcare providers have better clarity on the type of procedures covered by insurance to better advise their patients prior to the actual procedure. Policyholders are thus encouraged to seek pre-authorisation for planned procedures as far as possible if the option is provided by the insurer.
paragraphs.textJP: The 給付s of 事前承認 are: (a) 保険者s can assess the medical necessity and cost of treatment to ensure it is within the terms and conditions of the policy’s 補償; (b) Patients will have a peace of mind in knowing that their procedure is within their insurance 補償; and (c) Healthcare providers have better clarity on the type of procedures covered by insurance to better advise their patients prior to the actual procedure. 保険契約者s are thus encouraged to seek 事前承認 for planned procedures as far as possible if the option is provided by the 保険者.

paragraphs.text: All IP insurers offering pre-authorisation services to its IP policyholders have to use the standard LIA Pre-Authorisation Form. (See Appendix 9C)
paragraphs.textJP: All IP 保険者s offering 事前承認 services to its IP 保険契約者s have to use the standard LIA 事前承認 Form. (See Appendix 9C)

paragraphs.text: IP insurers will directly settle pre-authorised bills with the hospital, day surgery centre or clinic.
paragraphs.textJP: IP 保険者s will directly settle pre-authorised bills with the hospital, day surgery centre or clinic.

paragraphs.text: MediSave is a mandatory national health savings scheme in Singapore under the Central Provident Fund (CPF) umbrella. Between 8% and 10.5% of each CPF member’s monthly salary (depending on his age) is set aside in his personal MediSave Account (MA). This amount consists of contributions from him and his employer.14 In the case of self-employed persons, they must make compulsory contributions to their MediSave Accounts after receiving a Notice of Computation (NOC) of CPF contributions from IRAS. The MediSave contribution is between 4% and 10.5% of yearly income and is computed based on the self-employed person’s age and Net Trade Income (NTI). The Government also provides MediSave top-ups for Singaporeans, especially lower-income workers and elderly persons.
paragraphs.textJP: メディセーブ is a mandatory national health savings scheme in Singapore under the Central Provident Fund (CPF（中央積立基金）) umbrella. Between 8% and 10.5% of each CPF（中央積立基金） member’s monthly salary (depending on his age) is set aside in his personal メディセーブ Account (MA). This amount consists of contributions from him and his employer.14 In the case of self-employed persons, they must make compulsory contributions to their メディセーブ Accounts after receiving a Notice of Computation (NOC) of CPF（中央積立基金） contributions from IRAS. The メディセーブ contribution is between 4% and 10.5% of yearly income and is computed based on the self-employed person’s age and Net Trade Income (NTI). The Government also provides メディセーブ top-ups for Singaporeans, especially lower-income workers and elderly persons.

paragraphs.text: Amounts in the MA earn an attractive annual interest income, with additional interest accruing to older CPF members to protect their savings against inflation. Since its introduction on 1 April 1984, MediSave has helped many Singaporeans save for their healthcare needs. Saving for future healthcare expenses is important, since the need for healthcare increases significantly as one grows older.
paragraphs.textJP: Amounts in the MA earn an attractive annual interest income, with additional interest accruing to older CPF（中央積立基金） members to protect their savings against inflation. Since its introduction on 1 April 1984, メディセーブ has helped many Singaporeans save for their healthcare needs. Saving for future healthcare expenses is important, since the need for healthcare increases significantly as one grows older.

paragraphs.text: Starting from 1 January 2022, the maximum amount members can top up to MediSave will be the difference between their Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) and their current MediSave balances. Members were previously only able to contribute the lower of the CPF Annual Limit and the difference between the BHS and their current MediSave balance. With the simplification, there is no longer a need to consider the CPF Annual Limit, which is typically made known only at the end of each year.
paragraphs.textJP: Starting from 1 January 2022, the maximum amount members can top up to メディセーブ will be the difference between their Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) and their current メディセーブ balances. Members were previously only able to contribute the lower of the CPF（中央積立基金） Annual Limit and the difference between the BHS and their current メディセーブ balance. With the simplification, there is no longer a need to consider the CPF（中央積立基金） Annual Limit, which is typically made known only at the end of each year.

paragraphs.text: Furthermore, with effect from 1 January 2022, members can enjoy annual tax relief of: (a) up to S$8,000 (previously S$7,000) when the member tops up his/her own Special/Retirement Account and/or MA; and (b) an additional tax relief of up to S$8,000 (previously S$7,000) when the member tops up his/her loved ones’ Special/Retirement Account and/or MA. Note that the changes related to top-ups to MA do not apply to self-employed persons.
paragraphs.textJP: Furthermore, with effect from 1 January 2022, members can enjoy annual tax relief of: (a) up to S$8,000 (previously S$7,000) when the member tops up his/her own Special/Retirement Account and/or MA; and (b) an additional tax relief of up to S$8,000 (previously S$7,000) when the member tops up his/her loved ones’ Special/Retirement Account and/or MA. Note that the changes related to top-ups to MA do not apply to self-employed persons.

paragraphs.text: With the MediSave Grant for Newborns, each Singaporean baby is now a CPF member at birth. The grant ensures that newborns have enough to pay for their MeidiShield Life premiums from birth till age 21.
paragraphs.textJP: With the メディセーブ Grant for Newborns, each Singaporean baby is now a CPF（中央積立基金） member at birth. The grant ensures that newborns have enough to pay for their MeidiShield Life 保険料s from birth till age 21.

paragraphs.text: Contributions to the MA are subject to a maximum amount, known as the Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS). The BHS is designed to be enough for a CPF member’s basic, subsidised healthcare needs in his old age.
paragraphs.textJP: Contributions to the MA are subject to a maximum amount, known as the Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS). The BHS is designed to be enough for a CPF（中央積立基金） member’s basic, subsidised healthcare needs in his old age.

paragraphs.text: Amounts above the BHS will flow to the CPF member’s Special or Retirement Accounts to boost his monthly payouts. If the CPF member has already met his Full Retirement Sum, the amounts will flow to his Ordinary Account, and can be withdrawn as cash from the age of 55 years. 14These rates apply up to the monthly CPF Ordinary Wage Ceiling, which is S$6,800 for 2024, and Additional Wage ceiling which is [S$102,000 – Total Ordinary Wage subject to CPF for the year].
paragraphs.textJP: Amounts above the BHS will flow to the CPF（中央積立基金） member’s Special or Retirement Accounts to boost his monthly payouts. If the CPF（中央積立基金） member has already met his Full Retirement Sum, the amounts will flow to his Ordinary Account, and can be withdrawn as cash from the age of 55 years. 14These rates apply up to the monthly CPF（中央積立基金） Ordinary Wage Ceiling, which is S$6,800 for 2024, and Additional Wage ceiling which is [S$102,000 – Total Ordinary Wage subject to CPF（中央積立基金） for the year].

paragraphs.text: As life expectancy and healthcare costs rise, successive cohorts of CPF members will need more MediSave savings for their healthcare expenses in their old age. Therefore, the BHS is adjusted yearly in January, to keep pace with the growth in MediSave use by the elderly.
paragraphs.textJP: As life expectancy and healthcare costs rise, successive cohorts of CPF（中央積立基金） members will need more メディセーブ savings for their healthcare expenses in their old age. Therefore, the BHS is adjusted yearly in January, to keep pace with the growth in メディセーブ use by the elderly.

paragraphs.text: The yearly BHS adjustment will apply only to CPF members aged 65 years and below in that year. When a CPF member reaches the age of 65 years, the BHS for him will be fixed at the prevailing BHS, and the amount will not change for the rest of his life.15
paragraphs.textJP: The yearly BHS adjustment will apply only to CPF（中央積立基金） members aged 65 years and below in that year. When a CPF（中央積立基金） member reaches the age of 65 years, the BHS for him will be fixed at the prevailing BHS, and the amount will not change for the rest of his life.15
