paragraphs.text: Refer to the following link at the CPF Website for the current BHS: https://www.cpf.gov.sg/member/faq/healthcare-financing/basic-healthcaresum/ what-is-the-basic-healthcare-sum
paragraphs.textJP: Refer to the following link at the CPF（中央積立基金） Website for the current BHS: https://www.CPF（中央積立基金）.gov.sg/member/faq/healthcare-financing/basic-healthcaresum/ what-is-the-basic-healthcare-sum

paragraphs.text: MediSave can be used to pay for: (a) the CPF member’s healthcare expenses at accredited institutions, including both public and private sectors; (b) healthcare expenses of the CPF member’s approved dependants, namely his spouse, children, parents, siblings, and grandparents. If paying for a sibling or grandparent, the patient must be a Singapore Citizen or Singapore Permanent Resident (SPR); and (c) Health insurance such as MediShield Life, ElderShield, CareShield Life and Integrated Shield Plans (IPs).
paragraphs.textJP: メディセーブ can be used to pay for: (a) the CPF（中央積立基金） member’s healthcare expenses at accredited institutions, including both public and private sectors; (b) healthcare expenses of the CPF（中央積立基金） member’s approved dependants, namely his spouse, children, parents, siblings, and grandparents. If paying for a sibling or grandparent, the patient must be a Singapore Citizen or Singapore Permanent Resident (SPR); and (c) Health insurance such as メディシールド・ライフ, エルダーシールド, ケアシールド・ライフ and Integrated Shield Plans (IPs).

paragraphs.text: Let us now look at these uses of MediSave in greater detail.
paragraphs.textJP: Let us now look at these uses of メディセーブ in greater detail.

paragraphs.text: MediSave can be used to pay for selected outpatient services, including chronic disease treatments and preventative care at GPs, polyclinics and Specialist Outpatient Clinics (SOCs). (a) Outpatient Treatment For Approved Chronic Diseases Under The Chronic Disease Management Programme (i) MediSave can be used to pay for outpatient treatment for 23 approved chronic conditions under the Chronic Disease Management Programme (CDMP). The full list of approved chronic conditions under the CDMP is available on the MOH Website at the following link: https://www.moh.gov.sg/policies-and-legislation/chronic-diseasemanagement- programme-(cdmp) 15 For example, all cohorts aged 65 years and above in 2016 will have the same BHS of S$49,800 for the rest of their lives. (ii) These are chronic conditions which can:  result in serious complications like heart disease, kidney failure and leg amputations, when not well managed; and  have high cost of treating the condition over the long term if poorly controlled. (iii) The aim of allowing MediSave use under the CDMP is to:  lower long-term healthcare costs; and  improve care for patients with chronic conditions, resulting in better health outcomes. (iv) A CPF member can use up to S$500 for non-complex chronic conditions, or S$700 for complex chronic conditions per patient per year, under the MediSave500/700 scheme. (b) Outpatient Treatment for Vaccinations MediSave500/700 can also be used to pay for vaccinations under the National Childhood Immunisation Schedule (NCIS) and National Adult Immunisation Schedule (NAIS). For the list of vaccinations which are payable by MediSave, please visit the CPF website at https://www.cpf.gov.sg/member/healthcare-financing/using-yourmedisave- savings/using-medisave-for-outpatient-treatments (c) Outpatient Treatment for Health Screenings The MediSave500/700 scheme can be used to pay for approved health screenings. MediSave-eligible health screenings include:  Mammogram screenings (for women aged 50 and above); and  Selected screening tests for newborns. (d) Flexi-MediSave for the Elderly Under the Flexi-MediSave scheme, patients aged 60 and above can use up to S$300 per patient per year from their own or their spouse’s MediSave Account (if the spouse is also aged 60 and above) for outpatient medical treatment, as well as approved vaccinations and screenings. Flexi-MediSave can be used at:  SOCs in the public hospitals and national specialty centres;  Polyclinics; and  Participating GP clinics. Flexi-MediSave can be used together with other outpatient MediSave limits, such as the MediSave500/700 limit.
paragraphs.textJP: メディセーブ can be used to pay for selected 外来 services, including chronic disease treatments and preventative care at GPs, polyclinics and Specialist 外来 Clinics (SOCs). (a) 外来 Treatment For Approved Chronic Diseases Under The Chronic Disease Management Programme (i) メディセーブ can be used to pay for 外来 treatment for 23 approved chronic conditions under the Chronic Disease Management Programme (慢性疾患管理プログラム). The full list of approved chronic conditions under the 慢性疾患管理プログラム is available on the MOH（保健省） Website at the following link: https://www.MOH（保健省）.gov.sg/policies-and-legislation/chronic-diseasemanagement- programme-(慢性疾患管理プログラム) 15 For example, all cohorts aged 65 years and above in 2016 will have the same BHS of S$49,800 for the rest of their lives. (ii) These are chronic conditions which can:  result in serious complications like heart disease, kidney failure and leg amputations, when not well managed; and  have high cost of treating the condition over the long term if poorly controlled. (iii) The aim of allowing メディセーブ use under the 慢性疾患管理プログラム is to:  lower long-term healthcare costs; and  improve care for patients with chronic conditions, resulting in better health outcomes. (iv) A CPF（中央積立基金） member can use up to S$500 for non-complex chronic conditions, or S$700 for complex chronic conditions per patient per year, under the メディセーブ500/700 scheme. (b) 外来 Treatment for Vaccinations メディセーブ500/700 can also be used to pay for vaccinations under the National Childhood Immunisation Schedule (NCIS) and National Adult Immunisation Schedule (NAIS). For the list of vaccinations which are payable by メディセーブ, please visit the CPF（中央積立基金） website at https://www.CPF（中央積立基金）.gov.sg/member/healthcare-financing/using-yourメディセーブ- savings/using-メディセーブ-for-外来-treatments (c) 外来 Treatment for Health Screenings The メディセーブ500/700 scheme can be used to pay for approved health screenings. メディセーブ-eligible health screenings include:  Mammogram screenings (for women aged 50 and above); and  Selected screening tests for newborns. (d) フレキシ・メディセーブ for the Elderly Under the フレキシ・メディセーブ scheme, patients aged 60 and above can use up to S$300 per patient per year from their own or their spouse’s メディセーブ Account (if the spouse is also aged 60 and above) for 外来 medical treatment, as well as approved vaccinations and screenings. フレキシ・メディセーブ can be used at:  SOCs in the public hospitals and national specialty centres;  Polyclinics; and  Participating GP clinics. フレキシ・メディセーブ can be used together with other 外来 メディセーブ limits, such as the メディセーブ500/700 limit.

paragraphs.text: There are several types of hospitalisation and day surgery expenses that can be paid with MediSave.
paragraphs.textJP: There are several types of 入院 and day surgery expenses that can be paid with メディセーブ.

paragraphs.text: The inpatient daily hospital limit applies if the patient is admitted to the hospital for at least 8 hours, while the day surgery limit applies if a patient undergoes a surgical operation listed under the Table of Surgical Procedures (TOSP) and is admitted and discharged within the same day. Visit the website: https://www.cpf.gov.sg/member/healthcare-financing/using-your-medisavesavings/ using-medisave-for-hospitalisation to view the TOSP for the full list of MediSave-claimable surgeries and their corresponding withdrawal limits.
paragraphs.textJP: The 入院患者 daily hospital limit applies if the patient is admitted to the hospital for at least 8 hours, while the day surgery limit applies if a patient undergoes a surgical operation listed under the Table of Surgical Procedures (TOSP) and is admitted and discharged within the same day. Visit the website: https://www.CPF（中央積立基金）.gov.sg/member/healthcare-financing/using-your-メディセーブsavings/ using-メディセーブ-for-入院 to view the TOSP for the full list of メディセーブ-保険金請求able surgeries and their corresponding withdrawal limits.

paragraphs.text: Let us look at some of these items: (a) Inpatient Episodes The CPF member can use up to S$550 per day for the first two days and S$400 per day thereafter under the daily hospital limit, which includes daily ward charges, daily treatment fees, investigations, and medicines. MediSave can also be used to pay for surgical procedures required during the inpatient stay. The withdrawal limit depends on the complexity of the surgery, and ranges from S$250 to S$7,550 (refer to the MOH and the CPF Board websites for details). For a CPF member who passes away during his hospitalisation, the patient’s own MediSave can be used to pay for the last inpatient hospital bill in full, without being subject to existing MediSave withdrawal limits. This is because the need to save for future healthcare needs is no longer relevant following his death. (b) Inpatient Psychiatric Episodes The CPF member can use up to S$150 per day can be used for hospital charges, subject to a maximum of S$5,000 a year. (c) Day Surgery Episodes The CPF member can use up to S$300 per day for hospital charges, which include daily ward charges, daily treatment fees, investigations, and medicines. MediSave can also be used to pay for the day surgical procedure. The withdrawal limit depends on the complexity of the surgery, and ranges from S$250 to S$7,550 (refer to the MOH and the CPF Board websites for details). (d) Assisted Conception Procedures MediSave may be used for Assisted Conception Procedures regardless of whether treatment is received in inpatient or outpatient setting. Only the MediSave accounts of the patient or her spouse may be used.
paragraphs.textJP: Let us look at some of these items: (a) 入院患者 Episodes The CPF（中央積立基金） member can use up to S$550 per day for the first two days and S$400 per day thereafter under the daily hospital limit, which includes daily ward charges, daily treatment fees, investigations, and medicines. メディセーブ can also be used to pay for surgical procedures required during the 入院患者 stay. The withdrawal limit depends on the complexity of the surgery, and ranges from S$250 to S$7,550 (refer to the MOH（保健省） and the CPF（中央積立基金） Board websites for details). For a CPF（中央積立基金） member who passes away during his 入院, the patient’s own メディセーブ can be used to pay for the last 入院患者 hospital bill in full, without being subject to existing メディセーブ withdrawal limits. This is because the need to save for future healthcare needs is no longer relevant following his death. (b) 入院患者 Psychiatric Episodes The CPF（中央積立基金） member can use up to S$150 per day can be used for hospital charges, subject to a maximum of S$5,000 a year. (c) Day Surgery Episodes The CPF（中央積立基金） member can use up to S$300 per day for hospital charges, which include daily ward charges, daily treatment fees, investigations, and medicines. メディセーブ can also be used to pay for the day surgical procedure. The withdrawal limit depends on the complexity of the surgery, and ranges from S$250 to S$7,550 (refer to the MOH（保健省） and the CPF（中央積立基金） Board websites for details). (d) Assisted Conception Procedures メディセーブ may be used for Assisted Conception Procedures regardless of whether treatment is received in 入院患者 or 外来 setting. Only the メディセーブ accounts of the patient or her spouse may be used.

paragraphs.text: (a) Rehabilitation MediSave can be used to cover the costs of rehabilitative care, if the patient is:  An inpatient at an approved community hospital (for S$250 per day, up to S$5,000 per annum)  An inpatient at an approved convalescent hospital (for S$50 per day, up to S$3,000 per annum)  An inpatient at an approved day hospital (for S$150 per day, up to S$3,000 per annum)  An outpatient at an approved day rehabilitation centre (for S$25 per rehab service per day, up to S$1,500 per annum) (b) Palliative Care MediSave can be used to pay for palliative care, subject to the following withdrawal limits:  Approved inpatient hospice palliative care services (up to S$250 per day for general palliative care, and up to S$350 per day for specialised palliative care)  Home palliative care and day hospice (up to S$2,500 per lifetime). If the patient has terminal cancer, end-stage organ failure or advanced dementia, there will not be any withdrawal limit if the bill is paid using the patient’s own MediSave. (c) Disability Care Under MediSave Care, Singapore Residents aged 30 and above, with severe disability, will be able to withdraw up to a total of S$200 per month in cash from their own or their spouse’s MediSave account for their long-term care needs. The withdrawal quantum depends on the MediSave balance at the point of monthly withdrawal (see Appendix 9A).
paragraphs.textJP: (a) Rehabilitation メディセーブ can be used to cover the costs of rehabilitative care, if the patient is:  An 入院患者 at an approved community hospital (for S$250 per day, up to S$5,000 per annum)  An 入院患者 at an approved convalescent hospital (for S$50 per day, up to S$3,000 per annum)  An 入院患者 at an approved day hospital (for S$150 per day, up to S$3,000 per annum)  An 外来 at an approved day rehabilitation centre (for S$25 per rehab service per day, up to S$1,500 per annum) (b) 緩和ケア メディセーブ can be used to pay for 緩和ケア, subject to the following withdrawal limits:  Approved 入院患者 hospice 緩和ケア services (up to S$250 per day for general 緩和ケア, and up to S$350 per day for specialised 緩和ケア)  Home 緩和ケア and day hospice (up to S$2,500 per lifetime). If the patient has terminal cancer, end-stage organ failure or advanced dementia, there will not be any withdrawal limit if the bill is paid using the patient’s own メディセーブ. (c) Disability Care Under メディセーブ Care, Singapore Residents aged 30 and above, with 重度障害, will be able to withdraw up to a total of S$200 per month in cash from their own or their spouse’s メディセーブ account for their long-term care needs. The withdrawal quantum depends on the メディセーブ balance at the point of monthly withdrawal (see Appendix 9A).

paragraphs.text: Besides using MediSave to pay for medical expenses, a CPF member can use his MediSave to pay for his own health insurance premiums, or those of his approved dependants. MediShield Life and ElderShield or CareShield Life premiums can be paid fully with MediSave whereas Integrated Shield Plans (IPs) and ElderShield or CareShield Life supplement premiums are subject to withdrawal limits which are periodically reviewed by MOH.
paragraphs.textJP: Besides using メディセーブ to pay for medical expenses, a CPF（中央積立基金） member can use his メディセーブ to pay for his own health insurance 保険料s, or those of his approved dependants. メディシールド・ライフ and エルダーシールド or ケアシールド・ライフ 保険料s can be paid fully with メディセーブ whereas Integrated Shield Plans (IPs) and エルダーシールド or ケアシールド・ライフ supplement 保険料s are subject to withdrawal limits which are periodically reviewed by MOH（保健省）.

paragraphs.text: Table 9.1 summarises the uses of the MediSave Account. Table 9.1: Uses of MediSave Account Uses of Medisave Items that can be paid using MediSave Outpatient Treatments Selected outpatient treatments such as:  Approved chronic conditions under the CDMP  Approved vaccinations  Approved health screenings Inpatient Treatments Inpatient care such as:  Inpatient episodes  Inpatient psychiatric episodes  Day surgery episodes  Assisted Conception Procedures Long-Term Care Long-Term Care includes:  Rehabilitative care  Palliative care  Disability care Insurance Premiums Premium payments of:  MediShield Life  ElderShield/CareShield Life which can be fully paid with MediSave  Integrated Shield Plans and ElderShield/CareShield Life supplements which are subject to withdrawal limits periodically reviewed by MOH
paragraphs.textJP: Table 9.1 summarises the uses of the メディセーブ Account. Table 9.1: Uses of メディセーブ Account Uses of メディセーブ Items that can be paid using メディセーブ 外来 Treatments Selected 外来 treatments such as:  Approved chronic conditions under the 慢性疾患管理プログラム  Approved vaccinations  Approved health screenings 入院患者 Treatments 入院患者 care such as:  入院患者 episodes  入院患者 psychiatric episodes  Day surgery episodes  Assisted Conception Procedures Long-Term Care Long-Term Care includes:  Rehabilitative care  緩和ケア  Disability care Insurance 保険料s 保険料 payments of:  メディシールド・ライフ  エルダーシールド/ケアシールド・ライフ which can be fully paid with メディセーブ  Integrated Shield Plans and エルダーシールド/ケアシールド・ライフ supplements which are subject to withdrawal limits periodically reviewed by MOH（保健省）

subsections.label: C
subsections.title: How Much MediSave Can Be Used?

paragraphs.text: MediSave use is subject to withdrawal limits, which are generally sufficient for subsidised treatments in public healthcare institutions. This ensures that there continues to be sufficient savings for future healthcare needs. Those seeking treatments in Class B1 and Class A wards of public hospitals or the private sector will usually have some out-of-pocket cash payments. The types of limits include: (i) Withdrawal Limit – the maximum amount that the CPF member can withdraw from his/her MediSave Account for his or her medical expenses. For the different categories and corresponding withdrawal limits, please refer to Appendix 9A. (ii) Additional Withdrawal Limit (AWL) – the maximum amount of MediSave that can be used to pay for additional private insurance premiums. The excess premiums above AWLs are payable in cash. Please refer to Appendix 9A for the AWL for Integrated Shield Plans (IP) and ElderShield/CareShield Life supplements.
paragraphs.textJP: メディセーブ use is subject to withdrawal limits, which are generally sufficient for subsidised treatments in public healthcare institutions. This ensures that there continues to be sufficient savings for future healthcare needs. Those seeking treatments in Class B1 and Class A wards of public hospitals or the private sector will usually have some out-of-pocket cash payments. The types of limits include: (i) Withdrawal Limit – the maximum amount that the CPF（中央積立基金） member can withdraw from his/her メディセーブ Account for his or her medical expenses. For the different categories and corresponding withdrawal limits, please refer to Appendix 9A. (ii) 追加引出限度額 – the maximum amount of メディセーブ that can be used to pay for additional private insurance 保険料s. The excess 保険料s above AWLs are payable in cash. Please refer to Appendix 9A for the AWL for Integrated Shield Plans (IP) and エルダーシールド/ケアシールド・ライフ supplements.

paragraphs.text: Payments via MediSave are allowed in all public healthcare institutions, as well as approved private healthcare institutions. For an updated list of medical institutions participating in MediSave, refer to the CPF Board Website.
paragraphs.textJP: Payments via メディセーブ are allowed in all public healthcare institutions, as well as approved private healthcare institutions. For an updated list of medical institutions participating in メディセーブ, refer to the CPF（中央積立基金） Board Website.

paragraphs.text: All CPF members can use their MediSave to pay for their approved dependents’ medical expenses; and there may be more than one payer per bill, e.g. two children can pay for their mother’s hospitalisation bill. Each CPF member should specify to the institution the amount of MediSave that he would like to use to pay for the hospitalisation bill.
paragraphs.textJP: All CPF（中央積立基金） members can use their メディセーブ to pay for their approved dependents’ medical expenses; and there may be more than one payer per bill, e.g. two children can pay for their mother’s 入院 bill. Each CPF（中央積立基金） member should specify to the institution the amount of メディセーブ that he would like to use to pay for the 入院 bill.

paragraphs.text: If the approved dependants’ MediSave are not sufficient to pay for the hospitalisation bill, then the patient’s family members can make an appeal to the hospital to have the bill settled by non-immediate family members via MediSave, e.g. children-in-law. However, this option is available only if the patient meets certain conditions, such as having stayed in the Class B2 or Class C ward of a public hospital.
paragraphs.textJP: If the approved dependants’ メディセーブ are not sufficient to pay for the 入院 bill, then the patient’s family members can make an appeal to the hospital to have the bill settled by non-immediate family members via メディセーブ, e.g. children-in-law. However, this option is available only if the patient meets certain conditions, such as having stayed in the Class B2 or Class C ward of a public hospital.

paragraphs.text: If a patient was hospitalised just before death and had authorised the use of his MediSave to pay for his medical bill, his MediSave balance would be used in full, without being subject to the existing MediSave withdrawal limits, to pay for his last inpatient medical bill.
paragraphs.textJP: If a patient was hospitalised just before death and had authorised the use of his メディセーブ to pay for his medical bill, his メディセーブ balance would be used in full, without being subject to the existing メディセーブ withdrawal limits, to pay for his last 入院患者 medical bill.

paragraphs.text: If the patient had not authorised the use of his MediSave before his demise, his immediate family member (spouse, parent, or child who is aged 21 years and above), or donee or deputy who are not lacking mental capacity can also do so for the last inpatient medical bill, provided that it has not been paid out to the patient’s nominated beneficiaries. In the event that the patient does not have any immediate family member, or donee or deputy to authorise the use of the patient’s MediSave, a relative who has been taking care of the patient may also write to MOH through the hospital to seek approval for the relative to authorise the withdrawal of patient’s MediSave, to pay for the last inpatient medical bill.
paragraphs.textJP: If the patient had not authorised the use of his メディセーブ before his demise, his immediate family member (spouse, parent, or child who is aged 21 years and above), or donee or deputy who are not lacking mental capacity can also do so for the last 入院患者 medical bill, provided that it has not been paid out to the patient’s nominated beneficiaries. In the event that the patient does not have any immediate family member, or donee or deputy to authorise the use of the patient’s メディセーブ, a relative who has been taking care of the patient may also write to MOH（保健省） through the hospital to seek approval for the relative to authorise the withdrawal of patient’s メディセーブ, to pay for the last 入院患者 medical bill.

paragraphs.text: The remaining MediSave balance, after the payment of the last medical bill, would be distributed to the nominated beneficiaries of the patient’s CPF account if a nomination was made before his death. If there was no nomination made, the balance in the deceased patient’s MA would be distributed by the Public Trustee to his family members under the intestacy laws for non-Muslims, or the Muslim inheritance law for Muslims.
paragraphs.textJP: The remaining メディセーブ balance, after the payment of the last medical bill, would be distributed to the nominated beneficiaries of the patient’s CPF（中央積立基金） account if a nomination was made before his death. If there was no nomination made, the balance in the deceased patient’s MA would be distributed by the Public Trustee to his family members under the intestacy laws for non-Muslims, or the Muslim inheritance law for Muslims.

paragraphs.text: Any patient who wishes to claim from his MediShield Life, IP, and/or MediSave will need to complete the Medical Claims Authorisation Form to: (a) allow the hospital to claim from his insurer; and (b) instruct the CPF Board to deduct from his MediSave to pay for his hospital bill, while the insurer settles the claim, which may take some time.
paragraphs.textJP: Any patient who wishes to 保険金請求 from his メディシールド・ライフ, IP, and/or メディセーブ will need to complete the Medical 保険金請求s Authorisation Form to: (a) allow the hospital to 保険金請求 from his 保険者; and (b) instruct the CPF（中央積立基金） Board to deduct from his メディセーブ to pay for his hospital bill, while the 保険者 settles the 保険金請求, which may take some time.

paragraphs.text: The patient may be required to pay a deposit in cash upon his hospital admission, as his MediSave may not cover the full hospital charges. This is usually the case for non-subsidised ward classes.
paragraphs.textJP: The patient may be required to pay a deposit in cash upon his hospital admission, as his メディセーブ may not cover the full hospital charges. This is usually the case for non-subsidised ward classes.

paragraphs.text: Prior to a treatment episode, IP policyholders can approach the medical institution to request for a Letter of Guarantee (LOG) from his insurer. The LOG is a service provided by IP insurers for policyholders, to waive the upfront cash required by the medical institution, up to the eligible LOG amount.
paragraphs.textJP: Prior to a treatment episode, IP 保険契約者s can approach the medical institution to request for a 保証状 from his 保険者. The LOG is a service provided by IP 保険者s for 保険契約者s, to waive the upfront cash required by the medical institution, up to the eligible LOG amount.

paragraphs.text: Insurers may not offer LOGs for all treatment settings (e.g., inpatient / day surgery / outpatient; public / private medical institution). Policyholders should check with their insurer to find out if they can obtain a LOG for their particular treatment setting.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者s may not offer LOGs for all treatment settings (e.g., 入院患者 / day surgery / 外来; public / private medical institution). 保険契約者s should check with their 保険者 to find out if they can obtain a LOG for their particular treatment setting.

paragraphs.text: Depending on the insurer, the LOG amount will either be: (i) computed based on the estimated IP payout in respect of the plan’s benefits, subject to the respective caps imposed by the insurer(s), or (ii) a flat amount for all eligible policyholders and treatment settings. The medical institution may fully or partially waive the deposit in such a case. A policyholder may not get the LOG if he does not meet the eligibility criteria, for example, if the reason for hospitalization is within his excluded conditions. It is important that the policyholder understands that a LOG may not mean that the insurer will pay for the specified amount. To obtain assurance that their bill will be covered, the policyholder may wish to apply for pre-authorisation instead, if available.
paragraphs.textJP: Depending on the 保険者, the LOG amount will either be: (i) computed based on the estimated IP payout in respect of the plan’s 給付s, subject to the respective caps imposed by the 保険者(s), or (ii) a flat amount for all eligible 保険契約者s and treatment settings. The medical institution may fully or partially waive the deposit in such a case. A 保険契約者 may not get the LOG if he does not meet the eligibility criteria, for example, if the reason for hospitalization is within his excluded conditions. It is important that the 保険契約者 understands that a LOG may not mean that the 保険者 will pay for the specified amount. To obtain assurance that their bill will be covered, the 保険契約者 may wish to apply for 事前承認 instead, if available.

paragraphs.text: For any IP policyholder who wants to claim from MediShield Life, IP and/or MediSave, the hospital will submit the claim on his behalf through the MediClaim online system after he has been discharged.
paragraphs.textJP: For any IP 保険契約者 who wants to 保険金請求 from メディシールド・ライフ, IP and/or メディセーブ, the hospital will submit the 保険金請求 on his behalf through the Medi保険金請求 online system after he has been discharged.

paragraphs.text: For MediShield Life claims, the CPF Board will calculate how much MediShield Life will pay and settle the amount directly with the hospital.
paragraphs.textJP: For メディシールド・ライフ 保険金請求s, the CPF（中央積立基金） Board will calculate how much メディシールド・ライフ will pay and settle the amount directly with the hospital.

paragraphs.text: For IP claims, the hospital will submit the claim electronically to the insurers directly. For payouts, the IP insurer will calculate how much the IP will pay based on the IP benefits, and the CPF Board will independently calculate how much MediShield Life will pay based on the MediShield Life benefits. The eventual payout will be the higher of the two amounts. For example, if the payout computed based on the full IP benefits is S$2,000, and the payout based on MediShield Life benefits is S$500, the policyholder will receive S$2,000, which comprises S$500 from the MediShield Life payout, and S$1,500 from the IP’s additional coverage payout. In the case where the payout based on MediShield Life benefits is higher than the IP benefits, the eventual payout will be based on the MediShield Life benefits.
paragraphs.textJP: For IP 保険金請求s, the hospital will submit the 保険金請求 electronically to the 保険者s directly. For payouts, the IP 保険者 will calculate how much the IP will pay based on the IP 給付s, and the CPF（中央積立基金） Board will independently calculate how much メディシールド・ライフ will pay based on the メディシールド・ライフ 給付s. The eventual payout will be the higher of the two amounts. For example, if the payout computed based on the full IP 給付s is S$2,000, and the payout based on メディシールド・ライフ 給付s is S$500, the 保険契約者 will receive S$2,000, which comprises S$500 from the メディシールド・ライフ payout, and S$1,500 from the IP’s additional 補償 payout. In the case where the payout based on メディシールド・ライフ 給付s is higher than the IP 給付s, the eventual payout will be based on the メディシールド・ライフ 給付s.

paragraphs.text: The patient can make use of his MediSave to pay for part of his bill, up to the prevailing MediSave withdrawal limits. The hospital will have to submit the claim electronically. Once the claim is approved, the CPF Board will make payment to the hospital from the patient’s MediSave. Any outstanding amount not covered by MediShield Life/IP, MediSave, or other private insurance (e.g. Group Hospital & Surgical Insurance policy effected by the patient’s employer) will have to be settled by the patient in cash.
paragraphs.textJP: The patient can make use of his メディセーブ to pay for part of his bill, up to the prevailing メディセーブ withdrawal limits. The hospital will have to submit the 保険金請求 electronically. Once the 保険金請求 is approved, the CPF（中央積立基金） Board will make payment to the hospital from the patient’s メディセーブ. Any outstanding amount not covered by メディシールド・ライフ/IP, メディセーブ, or other private insurance (e.g. Group Hospital & Surgical Insurance policy effected by the patient’s employer) will have to be settled by the patient in cash.

subsections.label: C
subsections.title: Claiming From Non-MediSave payable Private Insurance

paragraphs.text: For someone who is covered by his employer’s medical benefits or other private insurance plans, he will need to show his insurance card (provided by some insurers for certain Medical Expense Insurance plans) to the hospital admission staff. The hospital may fully or partially waive the deposit in such a case.
paragraphs.textJP: For someone who is covered by his employer’s medical 給付s or other private insurance plans, he will need to show his insurance card (provided by some 保険者s for certain 医療費保険 plans) to the hospital admission staff. The hospital may fully or partially waive the deposit in such a case.

paragraphs.text: Some hospitals will send the hospitalisation bill directly to the patient’s non- MediSave payable private insurer or employer. In other cases, the patient will have to personally submit the bill to the insurer. If the bill is more than the reimbursement from the insurer or the employer, he can choose to claim the remainder from MediShield Life/his IP and/or his MediSave, up to prevailing limits and subject to co-payment, where applicable.
paragraphs.textJP: Some hospitals will send the 入院 bill directly to the patient’s non- メディセーブ payable private 保険者 or employer. In other cases, the patient will have to personally submit the bill to the 保険者. If the bill is more than the 実費精算・償還払い from the 保険者 or the employer, he can choose to 保険金請求 the remainder from メディシールド・ライフ/his IP and/or his メディセーブ, up to prevailing limits and subject to 自己負担, where applicable.

paragraphs.text: For members who are covered under multiple schemes, the Ministry of Health has set out the following claims protocol to avoid duplicative payouts: (a) Employer, private insurance, other third party pays first (b) Followed by MediShield Life / IP (c) Then MediSave (d) Then Cash
paragraphs.textJP: For members who are covered under multiple schemes, the Ministry of Health has set out the following 保険金請求s protocol to avoid duplicative payouts: (a) Employer, private insurance, other third party pays first (b) Followed by メディシールド・ライフ / IP (c) Then メディセーブ (d) Then Cash

paragraphs.text: Patients should inform their medical institutions of all the parties that they intend to make claims from, as early as possible. If the patient has filed a claim against MediShield Life / IP first and subsequently with a third-party payer, the patient or the third-party payer must reimburse the relevant amount to MediShield Life / IP, in line with the claims protocol above. Other Healthcare Financing Schemes Set Up By The Singapore Government LEARNING OUTCOMES After studying this chapter, you should be able to:  describe how エルダーシールド and エルダーシールド Supplements work  explain the shift from エルダーシールド to ケアシールド・ライフ  describe the enhanced 給付s under ケアシールド・ライフ  describe other healthcare financing schemes set up by the Singapore Government: - パイオニア世代 Package - ムルデカ世代 Package - Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (傷害・健康保険販売事業体) - エルダーファンド - メディファンド

paragraphs.text: ElderShield is a long-term care insurance scheme launched in September 2002. It provides basic financial protection to those who need long-term care, especially during old age.
paragraphs.textJP: エルダーシールド is a long-term care insurance scheme launched in September 2002. It provides basic financial protection to those who need long-term care, especially during old age.

paragraphs.text: Unlike MediShield Life, which is compulsory for all, ElderShield is an opt-out scheme. That means that all Singapore Citizens (SCs) and Singapore Permanent Residents (SPRs) can choose to opt-out of the insurance scheme if they wish. For new cohorts born in 1980 and after, they would be enrolled into CareShield Life instead of ElderShield on 1 Oct 2020 or when they turn 30, whichever is later. Those born in 1979 or earlier have the option to join CareShield Life if they do not have severe disability.
paragraphs.textJP: Unlike メディシールド・ライフ, which is compulsory for all, エルダーシールド is an opt-out scheme. That means that all Singapore Citizens (SCs) and Singapore Permanent Residents (SPRs) can choose to opt-out of the insurance scheme if they wish. For new cohorts born in 1980 and after, they would be enrolled into ケアシールド・ライフ instead of エルダーシールド on 1 Oct 2020 or when they turn 30, whichever is later. Those born in 1979 or earlier have the option to join ケアシールド・ライフ if they do not have 重度障害.

paragraphs.text: Premiums can be paid fully through MediSave. There are two ElderShield schemes, namely ElderShield300 and ElderShield400. ElderShield300 was launched on 30 September 2002 offering a cash payout of S$300 per month for a maximum of 60 months. ElderShield400 was introduced in 2007 offering an improved coverage with cash payout of S$400 per month for a maximum of 72 months. SCs and SPRs who joined ElderShield after 30 September 2007 will be on the ElderShield400 scheme.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険料s can be paid fully through メディセーブ. There are two エルダーシールド schemes, namely エルダーシールド300 and エルダーシールド400. エルダーシールド300 was launched on 30 September 2002 offering a cash payout of S$300 per month for a maximum of 60 months. エルダーシールド400 was introduced in 2007 offering an improved 補償 with cash payout of S$400 per month for a maximum of 72 months. SCs and SPRs who joined エルダーシールド after 30 September 2007 will be on the エルダーシールド400 scheme.

paragraphs.text: Before 1 November 2021, the Government had appointed three private insurers, Aviva Ltd (now known as Singlife), Great Eastern Life Assurance Co Ltd and Income Insurance Limited, to offer ElderShield. Any CPF member who is a SC or SPR and who attained the age of 40 years was automatically covered under the scheme if he/she did not opt out. SCs or SPRs who opted out of ElderShield and thereafter wished to opt back in would need to undergo medical underwriting.
paragraphs.textJP: Before 1 November 2021, the Government had appointed three private 保険者s, Aviva Ltd (now known as Singlife), Great Eastern Life Assurance Co Ltd and Income Insurance Limited, to offer エルダーシールド. Any CPF（中央積立基金） member who is a SC or SPR and who attained the age of 40 years was automatically covered under the scheme if he/she did not opt out. SCs or SPRs who opted out of エルダーシールド and thereafter wished to opt back in would need to undergo medical 引受審査.

paragraphs.text: The Government had taken over the ElderShield administration from 1 November 2021, and ElderShield had become a closed scheme from 1 August 2021 onwards. New cohorts born in 1980 or later were covered under CareShield Life from 1 Oct 2020 (more details in Section 7 of this Chapter), but SCs and SPRs born in 1979 or earlier could still opt in to the ElderShield scheme up till 1 Aug 2021 if they were below 65. Those born between 1970 and 1979, who were ElderShield400 policyholders, and did not have severe disability, were also automatically enrolled into CareShield Life and could choose to opt out by 31 Dec 2023. Existing ElderShield policyholders can continue to remain covered under the scheme with no changes to their coverage and benefits.
paragraphs.textJP: The Government had taken over the エルダーシールド administration from 1 November 2021, and エルダーシールド had become a closed scheme from 1 August 2021 onwards. New cohorts born in 1980 or later were covered under ケアシールド・ライフ from 1 Oct 2020 (more details in Section 7 of this Chapter), but SCs and SPRs born in 1979 or earlier could still opt in to the エルダーシールド scheme up till 1 Aug 2021 if they were below 65. Those born between 1970 and 1979, who were エルダーシールド400 保険契約者s, and did not have 重度障害, were also automatically enrolled into ケアシールド・ライフ and could choose to opt out by 31 Dec 2023. Existing エルダーシールド 保険契約者s can continue to remain covered under the scheme with no changes to their 補償 and 給付s.

paragraphs.text: It should be noted that existing ElderShield insureds who wish to obtain higher severe disability insurance coverage can purchase Supplements. Supplement plans complement the current ElderShield scheme, by offering additional benefits such as: (a) increasing the monthly payout; (b) extending the payout period; and/or (c) increasing disability coverage.
paragraphs.textJP: It should be noted that existing エルダーシールド 被保険者s who wish to obtain higher 重度障害 insurance 補償 can pur地域医療支援スキームe Supplements. Supplement plans complement the current エルダーシールド scheme, by offering additional 給付s such as: (a) increasing the monthly payout; (b) extending the payout period; and/or (c) increasing disability 補償.

paragraphs.text: To purchase and maintain a Supplement, an insured must have an ElderShield policy. Premiums for Supplement can be paid using MediSave (up to a limit of S$600 per insured per calendar year) and/or by cash. As ElderShield and Supplements are structured as stand-alone policies, claims can be made under both policies if the insured fulfils the claim eligibility criteria. Supplement plans are currently (as of August 2024) only offered by the three approved insurers who were administering ElderShield prior to 1 November 2021.
paragraphs.textJP: To pur地域医療支援スキームe and maintain a Supplement, an 被保険者 must have an エルダーシールド policy. 保険料s for Supplement can be paid using メディセーブ (up to a limit of S$600 per 被保険者 per calendar year) and/or by cash. As エルダーシールド and Supplements are structured as stand-alone policies, 保険金請求s can be made under both policies if the 被保険者 fulfils the 保険金請求 eligibility criteria. Supplement plans are currently (as of August 2024) only offered by the three approved 保険者s who were administering エルダーシールド prior to 1 November 2021.

subsections.label: A
subsections.title: Eligibility Criteria For Payment Of ElderShield Benefits

paragraphs.text: There is a waiting period of 90 days from the policy commencement date. The benefit will not be payable if the insured becomes disabled in the first 90 days of coverage. Instead, the insurer will terminate the policy and refund all the premiums paid. However, the waiting period does not apply if the disability is due solely to an accident. The waiting period is waived for policyholders who were auto enrolled.
paragraphs.textJP: There is a 待機期間 of 90 days from the policy commencement date. The 給付 will not be payable if the 被保険者 becomes disabled in the first 90 days of 補償. Instead, the 保険者 will terminate the policy and refund all the 保険料s paid. However, the 待機期間 does not apply if the disability is due solely to an accident. The 待機期間 is waived for 保険契約者s who were auto enrolled.

subsections.label: A2
subsections.title: Unable To Perform The Specified Number Of Activities Of Daily Living As

paragraphs.text: Defined
paragraphs.textJP: Defined

paragraphs.text: The ElderShield scheme will pay a monthly benefit of either S$300 up to 60 months, or S$400 up to 72 months, depending on the type of the scheme in which the CPF member is covered. It pays the benefits if the insured is unable to perform at least three of the six Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) as illustrated in the diagram below:
paragraphs.textJP: The エルダーシールド scheme will pay a monthly 給付 of either S$300 up to 60 months, or S$400 up to 72 months, depending on the type of the scheme in which the CPF（中央積立基金） member is covered. It pays the 給付s if the 被保険者 is unable to perform at least three of the six 日常生活動作 as illustrated in the diagram below:

paragraphs.text: The ADLs are defined as below: (a) Washing – the ability to wash in the bath or shower (including getting into and out of the bath or shower) or wash by other means. (b) Dressing – the ability to put on, take off, secure and unfasten all garments and, as appropriate, any braces, artificial limbs or other surgical and medical appliances. (c) Feeding – the ability to feed oneself after the food has been prepared and made available. (d) Toileting – the ability to use the lavatory or manage bowel and bladder function through the use of protective undergarments (e.g. diapers) or surgical appliances (e.g. urinary catheters) if appropriate. Washing Dressing Feeding Mobility Transferring Toileting Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) (e) Mobility – the ability to move indoors from room to room on level surfaces. (f) Transferring – the ability to move from a bed to an upright chair or wheelchair, and vice versa.
paragraphs.textJP: The ADLs are defined as below: (a) Washing – the ability to wash in the bath or shower (including getting into and out of the bath or shower) or wash by other means. (b) Dressing – the ability to put on, take off, secure and unfasten all garments and, as appropriate, any braces, artificial limbs or other surgical and medical appliances. (c) Feeding – the ability to feed oneself after the food has been prepared and made available. (d) Toileting – the ability to use the lavatory or manage bowel and bladder function through the use of protective undergarments (e.g. diapers) or surgical appliances (e.g. urinary catheters) if appropriate. Washing Dressing Feeding Mobility Transferring Toileting 日常生活動作 (e) Mobility – the ability to move indoors from room to room on level surfaces. (f) Transferring – the ability to move from a bed to an upright chair or wheelchair, and vice versa.

paragraphs.text: There is a deferment period of 90 days starting from the claim date, i.e. the date that the claim application is received by the AIC. If the insured is still disabled after this period, he will then be eligible for the benefit, provided that he satisfies the other conditions as specified in the policy. If he recovers from the disability within the period, then no benefits are payable.
paragraphs.textJP: There is a deferment period of 90 days starting from the 保険金請求 date, i.e. the date that the 保険金請求 application is received by the AIC. If the 被保険者 is still disabled after this period, he will then be eligible for the 給付, provided that he satisfies the other conditions as specified in the policy. If he recovers from the disability within the period, then no 給付s are payable.

paragraphs.text: However, the deferment period will not apply if the insured suffers a relapse from the same cause within 180 days of recovery, provided that he has been disabled for at least 90 days during the first disability.
paragraphs.textJP: However, the deferment period will not apply if the 被保険者 suffers a relapse from the same cause within 180 days of recovery, provided that he has been disabled for at least 90 days during the first disability.

subsections.label: B
subsections.title: How To Make A Claim Under ElderShield?

paragraphs.text: To make a claim, the insured has to make an appointment with an MOHaccredited severe disability assessor to have a disability assessment done. The assessor will then complete the assessment form and submit it to the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC). The list of accredited assessors can be found on the AIC’s website.
paragraphs.textJP: To make a 保険金請求, the 被保険者 has to make an appointment with an MOH（保健省）accredited 重度障害 assessor to have a disability assessment done. The assessor will then complete the assessment form and submit it to the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC). The list of accredited assessors can be found on the AIC’s website.

paragraphs.text: Next, the insured needs to complete a claim form. In the event that he is not able to complete it on his own, his immediate family member or caregiver may do so on his behalf.
paragraphs.textJP: Next, the 被保険者 needs to complete a 保険金請求 form. In the event that he is not able to complete it on his own, his immediate family member or caregiver may do so on his behalf.

paragraphs.text: It will cost S$100 for an assessment at the assessor’s clinic, and S$250 if the insured requires the assessor to make a house call. The fees for the initial or subsequent assessments will be reimbursed to the insured by AIC if the outcome of the assessment is severe disability.
paragraphs.textJP: It will cost S$100 for an assessment at the assessor’s clinic, and S$250 if the 被保険者 requires the assessor to make a house call. The fees for the initial or subsequent assessments will be reimbursed to the 被保険者 by AIC if the outcome of the assessment is 重度障害.

paragraphs.text: ElderShield benefits are payable on a monthly basis. The premium under the policy will be waived during the benefit payout period. If an insured recovers from his disability, the monthly cash payout will stop and the premium payment will re-commence. However, if he becomes disabled again some years later, he can still make a claim under his policy, as long as he has not claimed more than 60 months or 72 months in total, depending on the ElderShield plan under which the insured is covered. The cash payout under this scheme is not tied to institutional care, and can be used to pay for any expenses, such as home nursing services, nursing home stay, day rehabilitation or medical bills, etc.
paragraphs.textJP: エルダーシールド 給付s are payable on a monthly basis. The 保険料 under the policy will be waived during the 給付 payout period. If an 被保険者 recovers from his disability, the monthly cash payout will stop and the 保険料 payment will re-commence. However, if he becomes disabled again some years later, he can still make a 保険金請求 under his policy, as long as he has not 保険金請求ed more than 60 months or 72 months in total, depending on the エルダーシールド plan under which the 被保険者 is covered. The cash payout under this scheme is not tied to institutional care, and can be used to pay for any expenses, such as home nursing services, nursing home stay, day rehabilitation or medical bills, etc.

paragraphs.text: ElderShield premiums are pre-funded. This enables the policy to acquire a reserve. The accumulated reserve enables the insured to enjoy reduced benefits (i.e. the policy will be converted into a paid-up policy) if he decides to stop paying his premiums after the policy has been in force for a number of years.
paragraphs.textJP: エルダーシールド 保険料s are pre-funded. This enables the policy to acquire a reserve. The accumulated reserve enables the 被保険者 to enjoy reduced 給付s (i.e. the policy will be converted into a paid-up policy) if he decides to stop paying his 保険料s after the policy has been in force for a number of years.

paragraphs.text: For example, an insured who joins ElderShield at the age of 40 years will be eligible for a reduced benefit of around S$100 per month if he decides to stop paying his premiums 11 years after the policy has been in force.
paragraphs.textJP: For example, an 被保険者 who joins エルダーシールド at the age of 40 years will be eligible for a reduced 給付 of around S$100 per month if he decides to stop paying his 保険料s 11 years after the policy has been in force.

paragraphs.text: The other key features of the ElderShield scheme are described below: (a) It is guaranteed renewability on an annual basis. (b) It provides worldwide coverage. (c) It has minimum (i.e. 40 years old) and maximum entry age (i.e. 64 years old). (d) A grace period of 75 days is allowed for payment of the overdue premiums. (e) Reinstatement of the policy is allowed within 180 days from the expiry of the grace period, subject to evidence of insurability at the insured’s expense and payment of the overdue premiums with interest. (f) The insurer has the right to appoint an assessor to examine the insured periodically. (g) Where the insured is overseas at the time of a claim, the insurer has the right to commute the benefit payments to a single payment reflecting the present value of future benefit payments, or withhold the claim payment if it is unable to assess the claim after having made reasonable attempts to do so. (h) There is no cash surrender value. (i) There is a free-look period of 60 days from the policy commencement date during which the insured may cancel his policy and receive a full refund of the premiums paid.
paragraphs.textJP: The other key features of the エルダーシールド scheme are described below: (a) It is guaranteed renewability on an annual basis. (b) It provides worldwide 補償. (c) It has minimum (i.e. 40 years old) and maximum entry age (i.e. 64 years old). (d) A 猶予期間 of 75 days is allowed for payment of the overdue 保険料s. (e) 復活 of the policy is allowed within 180 days from the expiry of the 猶予期間, subject to evidence of insurability at the 被保険者’s expense and payment of the overdue 保険料s with interest. (f) The 保険者 has the right to appoint an assessor to examine the 被保険者 periodically. (g) Where the 被保険者 is overseas at the time of a 保険金請求, the 保険者 has the right to commute the 給付 payments to a single payment reflecting the present value of future 給付 payments, or withhold the 保険金請求 payment if it is unable to assess the 保険金請求 after having made reasonable attempts to do so. (h) There is no cash surrender value. (i) There is a クーリングオフ期間 of 60 days from the policy commencement date during which the 被保険者 may cancel his policy and receive a full refund of the 保険料s paid.

paragraphs.text: The ElderShield policy does not cover any disability arising directly or indirectly, wholly or partly, from any one of the following occurrences: (a) self-inflicted injury, suicide or attempted suicide; (b) alcoholism or drug addiction; or (c) war, whether declared or undeclared.
paragraphs.textJP: The エルダーシールド policy does not cover any disability arising directly or indirectly, wholly or partly, from any one of the following occurrences: (a) 自傷行為, suicide or attempted suicide; (b) alcoholism or drug addiction; or (c) war, whether declared or undeclared.

paragraphs.text: CPF members who are suffering from pre-existing disabilities, will not be covered under ElderShield.
paragraphs.textJP: CPF（中央積立基金） members who are suffering from pre-existing disabilities, will not be covered under エルダーシールド.

paragraphs.text: The cover under the ElderShield policy will be terminated upon the occurrence of any one of the following events: (a) expiry of the grace period if the ElderShield policy is not eligible for the non-forfeiture benefit (i.e. it has been in force for too short a period for it to acquire sufficient reserves to be converted into a paid-up policy); (b) death of the insured; (c) date in which the last benefit payment has been received; (d) no longer an SC/SPR from 1 Apr 2024 (following the closure of CPF foreigner accounts); or (e) date in which the written notice from the insured to cancel the policy is received by the insurer (if the policy does not have sufficient reserves, it will not be converted into a paid-up policy).
paragraphs.textJP: The cover under the エルダーシールド policy will be terminated upon the occurrence of any one of the following events: (a) expiry of the 猶予期間 if the エルダーシールド policy is not eligible for the non-forfeiture 給付 (i.e. it has been in force for too short a period for it to acquire sufficient reserves to be converted into a paid-up policy); (b) death of the 被保険者; (c) date in which the last 給付 payment has been received; (d) no longer an SC/SPR from 1 Apr 2024 (following the closure of CPF（中央積立基金） foreigner accounts); or (e) date in which the written notice from the 被保険者 to cancel the policy is received by the 保険者 (if the policy does not have sufficient reserves, it will not be converted into a paid-up policy).

paragraphs.text: From 1 November 2021, the Government has taken over the administration of ElderShield from private insurers (Singlife, Great Eastern Life Assurance Co Ltd, and Income Insurance Limited). This allows ElderShield policyholders to upgrade to CareShield Life more smoothly, and benefit from improvements to the claims assessment process that is being implemented for CareShield Life.
paragraphs.textJP: From 1 November 2021, the Government has taken over the administration of エルダーシールド from private 保険者s (Singlife, Great Eastern Life Assurance Co Ltd, and Income Insurance Limited). This allows エルダーシールド 保険契約者s to upgrade to ケアシールド・ライフ more smoothly, and 給付 from improvements to the 保険金請求s assessment process that is being implemented for ケアシールド・ライフ.

paragraphs.text: ElderShield policyholders who did not upgrade to CareShield Life remain covered by their existing ElderShield policies. The Government administers the ElderShield scheme on a not-for-profit basis. In the event that the actual claims experience turns out better than expected, there will continue to be premium rebates for ElderShield policyholders.
paragraphs.textJP: エルダーシールド 保険契約者s who did not upgrade to ケアシールド・ライフ remain covered by their existing エルダーシールド policies. The Government administers the エルダーシールド scheme on a not-for-profit basis. In the event that the actual 保険金請求s experience turns out better than expected, there will continue to be 保険料 rebates for エルダーシールド 保険契約者s.

paragraphs.text: Holders of ElderShield Supplements were not affected. They continue to be served by their existing Supplement insurers.
paragraphs.textJP: Holders of エルダーシールド Supplements were not affected. They continue to be served by their existing Supplement 保険者s.

paragraphs.text: CareShield Life was introduced in 2020 and is administered by the Government. As Singapore’s population ages and more people need longterm care, the role of risk-pooling via insurance was strengthened to help Singaporeans better prepare for their long-term care needs.
paragraphs.textJP: ケアシールド・ライフ was introduced in 2020 and is administered by the Government. As Singapore’s population ages and more people need longterm care, the role of risk-pooling via insurance was strengthened to help Singaporeans better prepare for their long-term care needs.

paragraphs.text: CareShield Life provides worldwide coverage with payouts as long as one has severe disability. Singaporeans will continue to be covered for life once they have completed paying all their premiums, which will happen in the year they reach the age of 67 or for 10 years, whichever is longer. Regardless of the place of residence, Singaporeans will remain covered, and be able to make a claim and receive payouts, if they have severe disability and continue to pay their premiums.
paragraphs.textJP: ケアシールド・ライフ provides worldwide 補償 with payouts as long as one has 重度障害. Singaporeans will continue to be covered for life once they have completed paying all their 保険料s, which will happen in the year they reach the age of 67 or for 10 years, whichever is longer. Regardless of the place of residence, Singaporeans will remain covered, and be able to make a 保険金請求 and receive payouts, if they have 重度障害 and continue to pay their 保険料s.

paragraphs.text: In order to make a claim, the insured needs to be assessed by a MOHaccredited severe disability assessor as being unable to perform at least three out of the six Activities of Daily Living (ADLs).
paragraphs.textJP: In order to make a 保険金請求, the 被保険者 needs to be assessed by a MOH（保健省）accredited 重度障害 assessor as being unable to perform at least three out of the six 日常生活動作.

paragraphs.text: CareShield Life is administered by the Government on a not-for-profit basis, and offers: (a) better protection and higher payouts that increase over time, so that claimants can better afford their basic long-term care needs; and (b) protection for life.
paragraphs.textJP: ケアシールド・ライフ is administered by the Government on a not-for-profit basis, and offers: (a) better protection and higher payouts that increase over time, so that 保険金請求ants can better afford their basic long-term care needs; and (b) protection for life.

paragraphs.text: To ensure that no one will lose CareShield Life coverage due to genuine inability to pay premiums, the Government provides the following premium subsidies and support: (a) Means-tested premium subsidies of up to 30% of base premiums of CareShield Life, to help lower- to middle-income households; (b) Participation incentives of up to S$2,500 for Singapore Citizens, if they join CareShield Life by 31 December 2023, to encourage those born in 1979 or earlier to join CareShield Life; (c) Additional participation incentives of S$1,500 for Merdeka and Pioneer Generation citizens; (d) Participation incentives of up to S$1,875 for Singapore Citizens if they join and are covered under CareShield Life between 1 January 2024 and 31 December 2024. Additional participation incentives of S$1,125 are provided for Merdeka and Pioneer Generation citizens; and (e) Additional Premium Support for Singaporeans who are unable to pay their CareShield Life premiums even after premium subsidies, so that no one will lose coverage under CareShield Life due to genuine inability to pay premiums.
paragraphs.textJP: To ensure that no one will lose ケアシールド・ライフ 補償 due to genuine inability to pay 保険料s, the Government provides the following 保険料 subsidies and support: (a) Means-tested 保険料 subsidies of up to 30% of base 保険料s of ケアシールド・ライフ, to help lower- to middle-income households; (b) Participation incentives of up to S$2,500 for Singapore Citizens, if they join ケアシールド・ライフ by 31 December 2023, to encourage those born in 1979 or earlier to join ケアシールド・ライフ; (c) Additional participation incentives of S$1,500 for Merdeka and パイオニア世代 citizens; (d) Participation incentives of up to S$1,875 for Singapore Citizens if they join and are covered under ケアシールド・ライフ between 1 January 2024 and 31 December 2024. Additional participation incentives of S$1,125 are provided for Merdeka and パイオニア世代 citizens; and (e) Additional 保険料 Support for Singaporeans who are unable to pay their ケアシールド・ライフ 保険料s even after 保険料 subsidies, so that no one will lose 補償 under ケアシールド・ライフ due to genuine inability to pay 保険料s.

paragraphs.text: ElderShield became a closed scheme from 1 August 2021. However, existing ElderShield300 and ElderShield400 policyholders can continue participating in the ElderShield scheme with no change to their benefits and coverage, unless they choose to join CareShield Life.
paragraphs.textJP: エルダーシールド became a closed scheme from 1 August 2021. However, existing エルダーシールド300 and エルダーシールド400 保険契約者s can continue participating in the エルダーシールド scheme with no change to their 給付s and 補償, unless they choose to join ケアシールド・ライフ.

paragraphs.text: It is important to note that any CareShield Life policyholder who wishes to obtain higher long-term care insurance coverage can also purchase Supplements. Supplements are plans which complement one’s basic CareShield Life plan by offering additional benefits, such as higher monthly payout amounts, which enhances the coverage.
paragraphs.textJP: It is important to note that any ケアシールド・ライフ 保険契約者 who wishes to obtain higher long-term care insurance 補償 can also pur地域医療支援スキームe Supplements. Supplements are plans which complement one’s basic ケアシールド・ライフ plan by offering additional 給付s, such as higher monthly payout amounts, which enhances the 補償.

paragraphs.text: An insured must have a CareShield Life or ElderShield policy in order to purchase a Supplement. Premiums for Supplements can be paid using MediSave (up to a limit of S$600 per insured per calendar year) and/or by cash. As CareShield Life and Supplements are structured as stand-alone policies, claims can be made under both policies if the insured fulfils the claim eligibility criteria. The Supplement plans are currently (as of August 2024) only offered by the three insurers (Great Eastern, Income Insurance Limited, Singlife) who were previously administering ElderShield.
paragraphs.textJP: An 被保険者 must have a ケアシールド・ライフ or エルダーシールド policy in order to pur地域医療支援スキームe a Supplement. 保険料s for Supplements can be paid using メディセーブ (up to a limit of S$600 per 被保険者 per calendar year) and/or by cash. As ケアシールド・ライフ and Supplements are structured as stand-alone policies, 保険金請求s can be made under both policies if the 被保険者 fulfils the 保険金請求 eligibility criteria. The Supplement plans are currently (as of August 2024) only offered by the three 保険者s (Great Eastern, Income Insurance Limited, Singlife) who were previously administering エルダーシールド.

paragraphs.text: For SCs and SPRs born in 1980 or later, CareShield Life is universal. There is no need to apply for CareShield Life. They are automatically covered on their 30th birthday or when the scheme was launched in October 2020, whichever is later, even if they have pre-existing disabilities or medical conditions.
paragraphs.textJP: For SCs and SPRs born in 1980 or later, ケアシールド・ライフ is universal. There is no need to apply for ケアシールド・ライフ. They are automatically covered on their 30th birthday or when the scheme was launched in October 2020, whichever is later, even if they have pre-existing disabilities or medical conditions.

paragraphs.text: SCs and SPRs born in 1979 or before, joining CareShield Life is optional. They can choose to apply to join CareShield Life from 6 November 2021 onwards as long as they do not have any severe disability. However, they can still join the scheme if they have pre-existing conditions (e.g. diabetes, hypertension). There is no age limit to join CareShield Life. However, once they join CareShield Life, they will not be able to leave the scheme after the 60-day free look period expires. The following are worth noting: (a) To increase the convenience of joining, SCs and SPRs born between 1970 and 1979, and who are insured under ElderShield400, had been auto-enrolled into CareShield Life from 1 December 2021. They have up to 31 December 2023 to opt-out of the scheme. If they opt-out, they will be reinstated onto ElderShield400. (b) Unlike for existing SCs and SPRs born in 1979 or before, it is mandatory for new SCs and SPRs to join CareShield Life, as long as they do not have any pre-existing severe disability.
paragraphs.textJP: SCs and SPRs born in 1979 or before, joining ケアシールド・ライフ is optional. They can choose to apply to join ケアシールド・ライフ from 6 November 2021 onwards as long as they do not have any 重度障害. However, they can still join the scheme if they have 既存疾病s (e.g. diabetes, hypertension). There is no age limit to join ケアシールド・ライフ. However, once they join ケアシールド・ライフ, they will not be able to leave the scheme after the 60-day クーリングオフ期間 expires. The following are worth noting: (a) To increase the convenience of joining, SCs and SPRs born between 1970 and 1979, and who are 被保険者 under エルダーシールド400, had been auto-enrolled into ケアシールド・ライフ from 1 December 2021. They have up to 31 December 2023 to opt-out of the scheme. If they opt-out, they will be reinstated onto エルダーシールド400. (b) Unlike for existing SCs and SPRs born in 1979 or before, it is mandatory for new SCs and SPRs to join ケアシールド・ライフ, as long as they do not have any pre-existing 重度障害.

paragraphs.text: CareShield Life features higher payouts that increase over time with no cap on payout duration, to provide better protection against the uncertainty of longterm care costs in the event of severe disability. The following points are worth noting: (a) Lifetime cash payouts are given for as long as the policyholder has severe disability. (b) Payouts increase annually until age 67 or when a successful claim is made, whichever is earlier, with payouts starting at S$600 per month in 2020.
paragraphs.textJP: ケアシールド・ライフ features higher payouts that increase over time with no cap on payout duration, to provide better protection against the uncertainty of longterm care costs in the event of 重度障害. The following points are worth noting: (a) Lifetime cash payouts are given for as long as the 保険契約者 has 重度障害. (b) Payouts increase annually until age 67 or when a successful 保険金請求 is made, whichever is earlier, with payouts starting at S$600 per month in 2020.

paragraphs.text: For more details on the estimated monthly payouts applicable, please refer to https://www.careshieldlife.gov.sg/careshield-life/benefits.html
paragraphs.textJP: For more details on the estimated monthly payouts applicable, please refer to https://www.careshieldlife.gov.sg/careshield-life/給付s.html

paragraphs.text: The CareShield Life scheme makes use of pre-funded premiums, where the insured CPF members pay more than the cost of their health risk while they are younger, so as to to cover for the higher risk in their later years. Insured CPF members will pay premiums until the year they reach the age of 67 or for 10 years, whichever is longer, but will remain insured for life, even after completion of the premium payments. Premiums are not guaranteed and are subject to review. The premiums are fully payable by MediSave.
paragraphs.textJP: The ケアシールド・ライフ scheme makes use of pre-funded 保険料s, where the 被保険者 CPF（中央積立基金） members pay more than the cost of their health risk while they are younger, so as to to cover for the higher risk in their later years. 被保険者 CPF（中央積立基金） members will pay 保険料s until the year they reach the age of 67 or for 10 years, whichever is longer, but will remain 被保険者 for life, even after completion of the 保険料 payments. 保険料s are not guaranteed and are subject to review. The 保険料s are fully payable by メディセーブ.

paragraphs.text: For SCs born in 1980 or later, premiums are paid from the age of enrolment until age 67. Premiums will increase over time, to support payout increases. For the first five years of CareShield Life’s implementation, payouts and premiums will both increase by 2% per year. Beyond 2025, an independent CareShield Life Council will advise the Government on premium and payout adjustments based on an actuarially sound adjustment framework.
paragraphs.textJP: For SCs born in 1980 or later, 保険料s are paid from the age of enrolment until age 67. 保険料s will increase over time, to support payout increases. For the first five years of ケアシールド・ライフ’s implementation, payouts and 保険料s will both increase by 2% per year. Beyond 2025, an independent ケアシールド・ライフ Council will advise the Government on 保険料 and payout adjustments based on an actuarially sound adjustment framework.

paragraphs.text: SCs born in 1979 or earlier (optional cohorts) will pay a base premium. The base premium is generally paid from the age of enrolment until age 67, but Singapore residents who join at age 59 or older will be able to spread their catch-up premiums over 10 years, beyond age 67. Other points in this regard are as follows: (a) Base premiums will increase over time to support payout increases, until the year policyholders turn 67. Premiums and payouts do not increase after the year policyholders turn 67. For the first five years of CareShield Life’s implementation, payouts and base premiums for policyholders age 67 and below will both increase by 2% per year. (b) ElderShield 400 policyholders who have been consistently on an ElderShield policy (i.e. never opted out, or upgraded from ElderShield 300 to ElderShield 400 in 2007 when they had the chance to) will only pay the base premium each year, if they join CareShield Life in 2021. (c) Other groups, including existing ElderShield 300 policyholders, existing ElderShield 400 policyholders who upgraded to CareShield Life later than 2021 as well as those not insured under ElderShield, will need to pay an additional catch-up component (which differs for each group), as they would not have paid as much premiums as those in their same cohort who were consistently insured under the ElderShield 400 scheme and upgraded to CareShield Life in 2021. The catch-up component will be paid over 10 years and will remain flat. The diagram below further illustrates the points highlighted above. Note: Optional Cohorts who join CareShield Life at age of 59 or older will end their premium payment after age 67 as they will have a 10-year premium payment term. Diagram not drawn to scale Illustration of Base Premium and Catch-up Component Payable by Singaporeans from Optional Cohorts who join CareShield Life
paragraphs.textJP: SCs born in 1979 or earlier (optional cohorts) will pay a base 保険料. The base 保険料 is generally paid from the age of enrolment until age 67, but Singapore residents who join at age 59 or older will be able to spread their catch-up 保険料s over 10 years, beyond age 67. Other points in this regard are as follows: (a) Base 保険料s will increase over time to support payout increases, until the year 保険契約者s turn 67. 保険料s and payouts do not increase after the year 保険契約者s turn 67. For the first five years of ケアシールド・ライフ’s implementation, payouts and base 保険料s for 保険契約者s age 67 and below will both increase by 2% per year. (b) エルダーシールド 400 保険契約者s who have been consistently on an エルダーシールド policy (i.e. never opted out, or upgraded from エルダーシールド 300 to エルダーシールド 400 in 2007 when they had the chance to) will only pay the base 保険料 each year, if they join ケアシールド・ライフ in 2021. (c) Other groups, including existing エルダーシールド 300 保険契約者s, existing エルダーシールド 400 保険契約者s who upgraded to ケアシールド・ライフ later than 2021 as well as those not 被保険者 under エルダーシールド, will need to pay an additional catch-up component (which differs for each group), as they would not have paid as much 保険料s as those in their same cohort who were consistently 被保険者 under the エルダーシールド 400 scheme and upgraded to ケアシールド・ライフ in 2021. The catch-up component will be paid over 10 years and will remain flat. The diagram below further illustrates the points highlighted above. Note: Optional Cohorts who join ケアシールド・ライフ at age of 59 or older will end their 保険料 payment after age 67 as they will have a 10-year 保険料 payment term. Diagram not drawn to scale Illustration of Base 保険料 and Catch-up Component Payable by Singaporeans from Optional Cohorts who join ケアシールド・ライフ

paragraphs.text: SCs may obtain estimates of the premiums and subsidies through https://www.careshieldlife.gov.sg/careshield-life/premiums-and-premiumsupport/ future-cohort--1980-or-later.html
paragraphs.textJP: SCs may obtain estimates of the 保険料s and subsidies through https://www.careshieldlife.gov.sg/careshield-life/保険料s-and-保険料support/ future-cohort--1980-or-later.html

paragraphs.text: CareShield Life premiums are fully payable by MediSave. Subsidies and support are available to make sure the premiums remain affordable, so the insured does not need to worry about losing coverage if he/she has a genuine inability to pay premiums.
paragraphs.textJP: ケアシールド・ライフ 保険料s are fully payable by メディセーブ. Subsidies and support are available to make sure the 保険料s remain affordable, so the 被保険者 does not need to worry about losing 補償 if he/she has a genuine inability to pay 保険料s.

paragraphs.text: Pioneer and Merdeka Generation seniors who join CareShield Life by 31 Dec 2023, will receive additional participation incentives of S$1,500, spread over 10 years (i.e. total participation incentives of S$4,000 for MG and PG members).
paragraphs.textJP: Pioneer and ムルデカ世代 seniors who join ケアシールド・ライフ by 31 Dec 2023, will receive additional participation incentives of S$1,500, spread over 10 years (i.e. total participation incentives of S$4,000 for MG and PG members).

paragraphs.text: For those who join and are covered under CareShield Life between 1 January 2024 to 31 December 2024, Pioneer and Merdeka Generation seniors will receive additional participation incentives of S$1,125. The total participation incentive of S$3,000 will be spread over 10 years.
paragraphs.textJP: For those who join and are covered under ケアシールド・ライフ between 1 January 2024 to 31 December 2024, Pioneer and ムルデカ世代 seniors will receive additional participation incentives of S$1,125. The total participation incentive of S$3,000 will be spread over 10 years.

paragraphs.text: Pioneer and Merdeka Generation seniors are classified as follows: (a) Pioneer Generation seniors refer to living Singapore Citizens born on or before 31 December 1949 and obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1986. (b) Merdeka Generation seniors refer to living Singapore Citizens who were born from 1 January 1950 to 31 December 1959 and had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996; or (c) those who were born on or before 31 December 1949, had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996 and do not receive the Pioneer Generation Package.
paragraphs.textJP: Pioneer and ムルデカ世代 seniors are classified as follows: (a) パイオニア世代 seniors refer to living Singapore Citizens born on or before 31 December 1949 and obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1986. (b) ムルデカ世代 seniors refer to living Singapore Citizens who were born from 1 January 1950 to 31 December 1959 and had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996; or (c) those who were born on or before 31 December 1949, had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996 and do not receive the パイオニア世代 Package.

subsections.label: E
subsections.title: Eligibility Criteria for Payment of CareShield Life Benefits

paragraphs.text: The eligibility criteria for CareShield Life benefits is similar to that of ElderShield.
paragraphs.textJP: The eligibility criteria for ケアシールド・ライフ 給付s is similar to that of エルダーシールド.

subsections.label: E1
subsections.title: Unable To Perform The Specified Number Of Activities Of Daily Living As

paragraphs.text: Defined
paragraphs.textJP: Defined

paragraphs.text: CareShield Life will pay a monthly benefit of at least S$600 for as long as the insured has severe disability. It pays the benefits if the insured is unable to perform at least three of the six Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) as defined.
paragraphs.textJP: ケアシールド・ライフ will pay a monthly 給付 of at least S$600 for as long as the 被保険者 has 重度障害. It pays the 給付s if the 被保険者 is unable to perform at least three of the six 日常生活動作 as defined.

paragraphs.text: There may be a deferment period of 90 days starting from the claim date, i.e. the date that the claim application is received by the AIC. If the insured is still disabled after this period, he will then be eligible for the benefit, provided that he satisfies the other conditions as specified in the policy.
paragraphs.textJP: There may be a deferment period of 90 days starting from the 保険金請求 date, i.e. the date that the 保険金請求 application is received by the AIC. If the 被保険者 is still disabled after this period, he will then be eligible for the 給付, provided that he satisfies the other conditions as specified in the policy.

subsections.label: F
subsections.title: How to Make a Claim Under CareShield Life?

paragraphs.text: To make a claim, the insured has to make an appointment with an MOHaccredited severe disability assessor to have a disability assessment done. The assessor will then complete the assessment and submit it to AIC directly. The list of accredited assessors can be found on the Agency of Integrated Care (AIC)’s website.
paragraphs.textJP: To make a 保険金請求, the 被保険者 has to make an appointment with an MOH（保健省）accredited 重度障害 assessor to have a disability assessment done. The assessor will then complete the assessment and submit it to AIC directly. The list of accredited assessors can be found on the Agency of Integrated Care (AIC)’s website.

paragraphs.text: Next, the insured needs to submit a claim application to AIC. In the event that he is not able to complete it on his own e.g. if he lacks mental capacity, his donee/deputy/immediate family member or caregiver may do so on his behalf.
paragraphs.textJP: Next, the 被保険者 needs to submit a 保険金請求 application to AIC. In the event that he is not able to complete it on his own e.g. if he lacks mental capacity, his donee/deputy/immediate family member or caregiver may do so on his behalf.

paragraphs.text: The fees for the first CareShield Life disability assessment are waived regardless of whether the claim is eventually assessed to be payable. Thereafter, the fees for subsequent assessments will only be reimbursed to the insured by AIC if the claim is assessed to be payable. More details on the applicable assessment fees can be found on AIC’s website.
paragraphs.textJP: The fees for the first ケアシールド・ライフ disability assessment are waived regardless of whether the 保険金請求 is eventually assessed to be payable. Thereafter, the fees for subsequent assessments will only be reimbursed to the 被保険者 by AIC if the 保険金請求 is assessed to be payable. More details on the applicable assessment fees can be found on AIC’s website.

subsections.label: G
subsections.title: Other Key Features of CareShield Life

paragraphs.text: The other key features of the CareShield Life scheme are stated below: (a) It is guaranteed renewable on an annual basis. (b) It provides worldwide coverage. (c) It has a minimum entry age of 30 years old, but no maximum entry age. (d) The CareShield Life policy will be terminated if the insured is no longer a SC or SPR. (e) There is no cash surrender value or death benefit.
paragraphs.textJP: The other key features of the ケアシールド・ライフ scheme are stated below: (a) It is 更新保証 on an annual basis. (b) It provides worldwide 補償. (c) It has a minimum entry age of 30 years old, but no maximum entry age. (d) The ケアシールド・ライフ policy will be terminated if the 被保険者 is no longer a SC or SPR. (e) There is no cash surrender value or death 給付.

sections.title: Other Healthcare Financing Schemes Set Up By The Singapore

paragraphs.text: GOVERNMENT
paragraphs.textJP: GOVERNMENT

paragraphs.text: Besides the Government subsidies, MediSave and insurance schemes as discussed above, the Government has also put in place a number of other schemes, to help Pioneers and needy Singaporeans.
