The schemes are as follows: (a) Pioneer Generation Package; (b) Merdeka Generation Package; (c) Majulah Package (d) Interim Disability Assistance Programme For The Elderly (IDAPE); (e) ElderFund; and (f) Medifund.
paragraphs.textJP: Besides the Government subsidies, メディセーブ and insurance schemes as discussed above, the Government has also put in place a number of other schemes, to help Pioneers and needy Singaporeans. The schemes are as follows: (a) パイオニア世代 Package; (b) ムルデカ世代 Package; (c) マジュラパッケージ (d) Interim Disability Assistance Programme For The Elderly (傷害・健康保険販売事業体); (e) エルダーファンド; and (f) メディファンド.

paragraphs.text: The Government introduced the Pioneer Generation Package in 2014 to honour and thank the Pioneer Generations for their significant contributions in the early years of nation-building.
paragraphs.textJP: The Government introduced the パイオニア世代 Package in 2014 to honour and thank the パイオニア世代s for their significant contributions in the early years of nation-building.

paragraphs.text: The “Pioneer Generation” refers to living Singaporeans who meet two criteria, namely: (a) aged 16 years and above in 1965 (born on or before 31 December 1949, which also means that they were aged 65 years and above in 2014); and (b) obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1986.
paragraphs.textJP: The “パイオニア世代” refers to living Singaporeans who meet two criteria, namely: (a) aged 16 years and above in 1965 (born on or before 31 December 1949, which also means that they were aged 65 years and above in 2014); and (b) obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1986.

paragraphs.text: The package provides the benefits (as described below) which the Pioneer Generation will enjoy for life.
paragraphs.textJP: The package provides the 給付s (as described below) which the パイオニア世代 will enjoy for life.

paragraphs.text: (a) Additional 50% off remaining bill for subsidised services and medications at polyclinics and Specialist Outpatient Clinics. (b) Special subsidies at participating GP and dental clinics under CHAS. (c) Cash of S$1,200 a year for those with moderate to severe functional disabilities under the Pioneer Generation Disability Assistance Scheme.
paragraphs.textJP: (a) Additional 50% off remaining bill for subsidised services and medications at polyclinics and Specialist 外来 Clinics. (b) Special subsidies at participating GP and dental clinics under 地域医療支援スキーム. (c) Cash of S$1,200 a year for those with moderate to severe functional disabilities under the パイオニア世代 Disability Assistance Scheme.

paragraphs.text: Pioneers will receive special Pioneer Generation Subsidies for their MediShield Life premiums.
paragraphs.textJP: Pioneers will receive special パイオニア世代 Subsidies for their メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s.

paragraphs.text: All Pioneers will receive annual MediSave top-ups for life, which range from S$250-S$900 depending on birth cohort. These top-ups can be used to further offset premiums for MediShield Life, ElderShield, and CareShield Life. Older Pioneers with serious pre-existing conditions will receive additional MediSave top-ups of S$50-S$200 from 2021-2025, to help with their higher MediShield Life premiums.
paragraphs.textJP: All Pioneers will receive annual メディセーブ top-ups for life, which range from S$250-S$900 depending on birth cohort. These top-ups can be used to further offset 保険料s for メディシールド・ライフ, エルダーシールド, and ケアシールド・ライフ. Older Pioneers with serious 既存疾病s will receive additional メディセーブ top-ups of S$50-S$200 from 2021-2025, to help with their higher メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s.

paragraphs.text: This scheme is for Pioneers who permanently need at least moderate assistance in a minimum of three of these ADLs:  Eating;  Bathing;  Dressing;  Transferring;  Toileting; or  Walking or moving around.
paragraphs.textJP: This scheme is for Pioneers who permanently need at least moderate assistance in a minimum of three of these ADLs:  Eating;  Bathing;  Dressing;  Transferring;  Toileting; or  Walking or moving around.

paragraphs.text: Lifelong cash assistance of S$100 each month is provided to help Pioneers with their care expenses.
paragraphs.textJP: Lifelong cash assistance of S$100 each month is provided to help Pioneers with their care expenses.

paragraphs.text: Pioneers will receive CareShield Life Additional Participation Incentives of S$2,500 if they join CareShield Life by 31 December 2023. Do refer to earlier section of this Chapter for more information on this.
paragraphs.textJP: Pioneers will receive ケアシールド・ライフ Additional Participation Incentives of S$2,500 if they join ケアシールド・ライフ by 31 December 2023. Do refer to earlier section of this Chapter for more information on this.

paragraphs.text: The Government introduced the Merdeka Generation Package (MGP) in 2019 to honour and thank our Merdeka Generation (MG) for their contributions. The MGP supports the aspirations of MG seniors in their silver years by helping them to stay active and healthy, and providing them better peace of mind over future healthcare costs.
paragraphs.textJP: The Government introduced the ムルデカ世代 Package (MGP) in 2019 to honour and thank our ムルデカ世代 (MG) for their contributions. The MGP supports the aspirations of MG seniors in their silver years by helping them to stay active and healthy, and providing them better peace of mind over future healthcare costs.

paragraphs.text: The “Merdeka Generation” refers to living Singaporeans who: (a) were born from 1 January 1950 to 31 December 1959, and (b) had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996.
paragraphs.textJP: The “ムルデカ世代” refers to living Singaporeans who: (a) were born from 1 January 1950 to 31 December 1959, and (b) had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996.

paragraphs.text: The MGP is also extended to living Singaporeans who: (a) were born on or before 31 December 1949, (b) had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996, and (c) do not receive the Pioneer Generation Package.
paragraphs.textJP: The MGP is also extended to living Singaporeans who: (a) were born on or before 31 December 1949, (b) had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996, and (c) do not receive the パイオニア世代 Package.

paragraphs.text: The package comprises the following benefits:
paragraphs.textJP: The package comprises the following 給付s:

paragraphs.text: MG seniors receive a one-time S$100 top-up to their PAssion Silver cards. They can use this to pay for use on active ageing programmes, public transport, entries to swimming pools and other purchases at all EZ-Link merchants.
paragraphs.textJP: MG seniors receive a one-time S$100 top-up to their PAssion Silver cards. They can use this to pay for use on active ageing programmes, public transport, entries to swimming pools and other pur地域医療支援スキームes at all EZ-Link merchants.

paragraphs.text: MG seniors receive a MediSave top-up of S$200 per year for five years, from 2019 to 2023. This will help them save more for their healthcare needs. This is on top of the GST Voucher – MediSave top-ups that eligible seniors aged 65 and above receive every year.
paragraphs.textJP: MG seniors receive a メディセーブ top-up of S$200 per year for five years, from 2019 to 2023. This will help them save more for their healthcare needs. This is on top of the GST Voucher – メディセーブ top-ups that eligible seniors aged 65 and above receive every year.

paragraphs.text: (a) Additional 25% off remaining bill for subsidised services and medications at polyclinics and Specialist Outpatient Clinics. (b) Special subsidies at participating GP and dental clinics under CHAS.
paragraphs.textJP: (a) Additional 25% off remaining bill for subsidised services and medications at polyclinics and Specialist 外来 Clinics. (b) Special subsidies at participating GP and dental clinics under 地域医療支援スキーム.

paragraphs.text: MG seniors receive additional Merdeka Generation Subsidies for their MediShield Life premiums, which is on top of other premium subsidies that the seniors may receive.
paragraphs.textJP: MG seniors receive additional ムルデカ世代 Subsidies for their メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s, which is on top of other 保険料 subsidies that the seniors may receive.

paragraphs.text: MG seniors will receive CareShield Life Additional Participation Incentives of S$2,500 if they join CareShield Life by 31 December 2023. Do refer to earlier section of this Chapter for more information on this.
paragraphs.textJP: MG seniors will receive ケアシールド・ライフ Additional Participation Incentives of S$2,500 if they join ケアシールド・ライフ by 31 December 2023. Do refer to earlier section of this Chapter for more information on this.

paragraphs.text: The Government introduced the Majulah Package in 2023 to strengthen support for retirement adequacy for “Young Seniors”, who are born in 1960 to 1973.
paragraphs.textJP: The Government introduced the マジュラパッケージ in 2023 to strengthen support for retirement adequacy for “Young Seniors”, who are born in 1960 to 1973.

paragraphs.text: The package is extended to all Singapore Citizens born in 1973 or earlier, including the Pioneer and Merdeka Generation.
paragraphs.textJP: The package is extended to all Singapore Citizens born in 1973 or earlier, including the Pioneer and ムルデカ世代.

paragraphs.text: The package comprises three components:
paragraphs.textJP: The package comprises three components:

paragraphs.text: Working seniors, who own no more than one property and live in a property with an annual value of S$25,000 or below, will receive an annual bonus of up to S$1,000 in their Special Account or Retirement Account, depending on age, to build up their CPF retirement savings while they work. The first payment will be made in March 2025.
paragraphs.textJP: Working seniors, who own no more than one property and live in a property with an annual value of S$25,000 or below, will receive an annual bonus of up to S$1,000 in their Special Account or Retirement Account, depending on age, to build up their CPF（中央積立基金） retirement savings while they work. The first payment will be made in March 2025.

paragraphs.text: Seniors with retirement savings below the Basic Retirement Sum for 2023 of S$99,400 will receive a one-time bonus of up to S$1,500 in their Special Account or Retirement Account (depending on age). The bonus will be paid in December 2024. Only seniors who own no more than one property and live in a property with an annual value of S$25,000 or below would be eligible.
paragraphs.textJP: Seniors with retirement savings below the Basic Retirement Sum for 2023 of S$99,400 will receive a one-time bonus of up to S$1,500 in their Special Account or Retirement Account (depending on age). The bonus will be paid in December 2024. Only seniors who own no more than one property and live in a property with an annual value of S$25,000 or below would be eligible.

paragraphs.text: All Singapore Citizens who are born on 31 December 1973 or earlier will receive a one-time bonus of up to S$1,500 in their MediSave Account. The bonus will be paid in December 2024.
paragraphs.textJP: All Singapore Citizens who are born on 31 December 1973 or earlier will receive a one-time bonus of up to S$1,500 in their メディセーブ Account. The bonus will be paid in December 2024.

subsections.label: D
subsections.title: Interim Disability Assistance Programme for The Elderly (IDAPE)

paragraphs.text: IDAPE was launched by the Government to take care of Singaporeans who were not eligible to join the ElderShield scheme, when it was launched in September 2002, either because they exceeded the maximum entry age or they had a pre-existing disability. It is aimed at helping this group of people to cope with their medical expenses, in the event that they develop severe disability. Individuals covered under the scheme need not pay any premiums.
paragraphs.textJP: 傷害・健康保険販売事業体 was launched by the Government to take care of Singaporeans who were not eligible to join the エルダーシールド scheme, when it was launched in September 2002, either because they exceeded the maximum entry age or they had a pre-existing disability. It is aimed at helping this group of people to cope with their medical expenses, in the event that they develop 重度障害. Individuals covered under the scheme need not pay any 保険料s.

paragraphs.text: A Singaporean will qualify for IDAPE if: (a) He is unable to perform three or more of the 6 Activities of Daily Living (ADL) - washing, feeding, dressing, toileting, mobility and transferring. (b) He is a Singapore citizen born before 30 September 1932 OR born between 1 October 1932 and 30 September 1962 (both dates inclusive) but with pre-existing disabilities as at 30 September 2002. (c) His per capita household monthly income is S$2,800 or less OR he has no household income and is living in a residence with Annual Value of S$13,000 and below.
paragraphs.textJP: A Singaporean will qualify for 傷害・健康保険販売事業体 if: (a) He is unable to perform three or more of the 6 Activities of Daily Living (ADL) - washing, feeding, dressing, toileting, mobility and transferring. (b) He is a Singapore citizen born before 30 September 1932 OR born between 1 October 1932 and 30 September 1962 (both dates inclusive) but with pre-existing disabilities as at 30 September 2002. (c) His per capita household monthly income is S$2,800 or less OR he has no household income and is living in a residence with Annual Value of S$13,000 and below.

paragraphs.text: The payment under the scheme is dependent on the individual’s per capita household income as shown in Table 9.4. Table 9.4: IDAPE Payout Per Capita Household Income* IDAPE Payout (With Effect From 1 July 2013) (a) S$0 to S$2,000 (a) S$250 per month for up to 72 months (b) S$2,001 to S$2,800 (b) S$150 per month for up to 72 months *An IDAPE applicant from a household with no income will qualify for the S$250 monthly payout if the annual value of his place of residence is S$13,000 or lower. Source: MOH Website
paragraphs.textJP: The payment under the scheme is dependent on the individual’s per capita household income as shown in Table 9.4. Table 9.4: 傷害・健康保険販売事業体 Payout Per Capita Household Income* 傷害・健康保険販売事業体 Payout (With Effect From 1 July 2013) (a) S$0 to S$2,000 (a) S$250 per month for up to 72 months (b) S$2,001 to S$2,800 (b) S$150 per month for up to 72 months *An 傷害・健康保険販売事業体 applicant from a household with no income will qualify for the S$250 monthly payout if the annual value of his place of residence is S$13,000 or lower. Source: MOH（保健省） Website

paragraphs.text: IDAPE is currently administered by the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC). Other than the fact that the payout is lower than the ElderShield scheme, the definition of disability and the claim procedures are the same as those for ElderShield with the following exceptions. (a) those making claims under IDAPE will be subject to a means testing administered by the Ministry of Health Holdings (MOHH); and (b) the recipient of the payout will need to pay S$100 for an assessment at the assessor’s clinic, and S$250 if the recipient requires the assessor to make a house call. The assessment fees will be reimbursed to the recipient, by AIC, upon successful application.
paragraphs.textJP: 傷害・健康保険販売事業体 is currently administered by the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC). Other than the fact that the payout is lower than the エルダーシールド scheme, the definition of disability and the 保険金請求 procedures are the same as those for エルダーシールド with the following exceptions. (a) those making 保険金請求s under 傷害・健康保険販売事業体 will be subject to a 資力審査 administered by the Ministry of Health Holdings (MOH（保健省）H); and (b) the recipient of the payout will need to pay S$100 for an assessment at the assessor’s clinic, and S$250 if the recipient requires the assessor to make a house call. The assessment fees will be reimbursed to the recipient, by AIC, upon successful application.

paragraphs.text: As you can see, the payouts under ElderShield and IDAPE are basic and limited to 72 months. Those on Eldershield who wish to have higher payouts or longer payout periods, and able to afford the premiums for the extra coverage, can consider purchasing additional Long-Term Care Insurance coverage. Existing and prospective CareShield Life clients can also purchase additional Long-Term Care Insurance coverage, if they wish to have higher payouts.
paragraphs.textJP: As you can see, the payouts under エルダーシールド and 傷害・健康保険販売事業体 are basic and limited to 72 months. Those on エルダーシールド who wish to have higher payouts or longer payout periods, and able to afford the 保険料s for the extra 補償, can consider pur地域医療支援スキームing additional Long-Term Care Insurance 補償. Existing and prospective ケアシールド・ライフ clients can also pur地域医療支援スキームe additional Long-Term Care Insurance 補償, if they wish to have higher payouts.

paragraphs.text: ElderFund is a discretionary assistance scheme launched on 31 January 2020. It is targeted at lower-income Singapore Citizens aged 30 and older, with severe disability, low Medisave balances, inadequate personal savings and are not able to benefit from CareShield Life, ElderShield, and the Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (IDAPE). The goal is to meet their longterm care needs. Eligible Singapore Citizens can receive up to S$250 cash per month for as long as they remain eligible, and meet the terms and conditions needed for receipt of payment.
paragraphs.textJP: エルダーファンド is a discretionary assistance scheme launched on 31 January 2020. It is targeted at lower-income Singapore Citizens aged 30 and older, with 重度障害, low メディセーブ balances, inadequate personal savings and are not able to 給付 from ケアシールド・ライフ, エルダーシールド, and the Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (傷害・健康保険販売事業体). The goal is to meet their longterm care needs. Eligible Singapore Citizens can receive up to S$250 cash per month for as long as they remain eligible, and meet the terms and conditions needed for receipt of payment.

paragraphs.text: MediFund is an endowment fund set up by the Government. It provides a safety net for needy Singaporeans who face difficulties paying for their remaining healthcare bills after Government subsidies, insurance and MediSave.
paragraphs.textJP: メディファンド is an endowment fund set up by the Government. It provides a safety net for needy Singaporeans who face difficulties paying for their remaining healthcare bills after Government subsidies, insurance and メディセーブ.

paragraphs.text: MediFund Silver and MediFund Junior are carved out from MediFund to provide more targeted assistance for the needy elderly and children respectively.
paragraphs.textJP: メディファンド Silver and メディファンド Junior are carved out from メディファンド to provide more targeted assistance for the needy elderly and children respectively.

paragraphs.text: As an endowment fund, interest income generated from the capital sum are allocated to MediFund-approved healthcare institutions, to assist with the healthcare bill payments of needy patients. Every MediFund-approved institution has an independent MediFund Committee to consider and approve applications, and decide on the appropriate quantum of assistance.
paragraphs.textJP: As an endowment fund, interest income generated from the 基本保険金額 are allocated to メディファンド-approved healthcare institutions, to assist with the healthcare bill payments of needy patients. Every メディファンド-approved institution has an independent メディファンド Committee to consider and approve applications, and decide on the appropriate quantum of assistance.

paragraphs.text: To apply for MediFund assistance, one must: (a) be a Singapore citizen; (b) be a subsidised patient; (c) have received or require treatment from a MediFund-approved institution; and (d) have difficulties paying for his/her remaining healthcare bills after tapping on Government subsidies and other available means, including MediShield Life and MediSave.
paragraphs.textJP: To apply for メディファンド assistance, one must: (a) be a Singapore citizen; (b) be a subsidised patient; (c) have received or require treatment from a メディファンド-approved institution; and (d) have difficulties paying for his/her remaining healthcare bills after tapping on Government subsidies and other available means, including メディシールド・ライフ and メディセーブ.

paragraphs.text:
The patient’s application will be assessed holistically by the medical social workers and the MediFund Committees, taking into consideration his and his family’s financial, health and social circumstances. HEALTHCARE FINANCING PART I & II CHAPTER IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Singapore government subsidies
▪ Government provides funding to public healthcare institutions (e.g. public hospitals, national specialty centres and polyclinics), as well as selected private clinics and voluntary welfare organisations (VWOs).
▪ Means-test is used to target subsidies, so that needy patients can receive more help; lower-income patients receive more subsidies than higher-income patients. Singapore's Healthcare Financing Philosophy Through a mixed financing system (see figure below), Singapore has secured good healthcare outcomes for its population. For example, life expectancy at birth has increased from 80.3 in 2006 to 83.93 in 2022, and infant mortality rate improved from 2.6 per thousand live births to 1.8 per thousand live births as of 2020. The Government has done so with a current national health expenditure of about 4.4% of the country’s Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in 2019, a percentage which is low among developed countries (although this is expected to grow with an ageing population over the years). Main components of Singapore’s Healthcare Financing System MediSave • National healthcare savings scheme in Singapore under the Central Provident Board (CPF) umbrella.
▪ Contributions to the Medisave Account (MA) are subject to a maximum amount, known as the Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS). The BHS is designed to be enough for a CPF member’s basic, subsidised healthcare needs in his old age. Hence, amounts in the MA up to the BHS cannot be withdrawn as cash.
▪ Amounts above the BHS will flow to the CPF member’s Special or Retirement Accounts to boost his monthly payouts. If the CPF member has already met his Full Retirement Sum, the amounts will flow to his Ordinary Account, and can be withdrawn as cash from the age of 55 years.
▪ Can be used to pay for the CPF member’s medical expenses at all accredited institutions, in both the public and private sector.
▪ Can also be used to pay for medical expenses of the CPF member’s approved dependents, namely his spouse, children, parents, siblings, and grandparents. If paying for a sibling or grandparent, the patient must be a Singapore Citizen or Singapore Permanent Resident (SPR). IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Community Health Assist Scheme (CHAS) The Community Health Assist Scheme (CHAS) enables eligible Singapore Citizens to receive subsidies for medical and/or dental care at participating General Practitioner (GP) and dental clinics. With effect from Nov 2019, this scheme has been expanded to include all Singapore Citizens regardless of their income levels. The colour of the CHAS card indicates the subsidy tier that cardholders are entitled to, namely the CHAS Blue, Orange or Green tier. All Pioneer Generation (PG) and Merdeka Generation (MG) seniors also receive special subsidies at CHAS clinics. The amount that cardholders pay will be based on the fees charged by the clinic, less the CHAS subsidies. The fees will vary according to each patient’s condition and clinic’s charges. Uses of MediSave Account Uses of Medisave Items that can be paid using MediSave Outpatient Treatments Selected outpatient treatments such as:
▪ Approved chronic conditions under the chronic disease management programme
▪ Vaccinations
▪ Flexi-MediSave for the elderly
▪ Health screenings Inpatient Treatments Inpatient care which includes:
▪ Inpatient episodes
▪ Inpatient psychiatric episodes
▪ Day surgery
▪ Assisted Conception Procedures Long-Term Care Long-Term Care treatments which include:
▪ Rehabilitative care
▪ Palliative care
▪ Disability care Insurance Premiums Premium payments of:
▪ MediShield Life
▪ ElderShield/CareShield Life which can be fully paid with MediSave
▪ Integrated Shield Plans and ElderShield/CareShield Life supplements which are subject to withdrawal limits periodically reviewed by MOH How much Medisave can be used The use of MediSave is subject to:
▪ Withdrawal Limit.
▪ Additional Withdrawal Limit (AWL). Payments via MediSave are allowed in all public healthcare institutions, as well as approved private hospitals and medical institutions IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Distribution Of MediSave Upon Demise
▪ If a patient was hospitalised just before death and had authorised the use of his MediSave to pay for his medical bill, his MediSave balance would be used in full, without being subject to the existing MediSave withdrawal limits, to pay for his last inpatient medical bill.
▪ If the patient had not authorised the use of his MediSave before his demise, his immediate family member (spouse, parent, or child who is aged 21 years and above), or donee or deputy can also do so for the last inpatient medical bill, provided that it has not been paid out to the patient’s nominated beneficiaries.
▪ In the event that the patient does not have any immediate family member, or donee or deputy to authorise the use of the patient’s MediSave, a relative who has been taking care of the patient may also write to MOH through the hospital to seek approval for the relative to authorise the withdrawal of patient’s MediSave, to pay for the last inpatient medical bill.
▪ Remaining MediSave balance, after the payment of the last medical bill, would be distributed to the nominated beneficiaries of the patient’s CPF account if a nomination was made before his death. If there was no nomination made, the balance in the deceased patient’s MA would be distributed by the Public Trustee to his family members under the intestacy laws for non-Muslims, or the Muslim inheritance law for Muslims. MediShield Life • Basic healthcare insurance scheme.
▪ Covers all Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents against large medical bills for life, regardless of age or health conditions.
▪ The claim limits are sized to cover subsidised bills incurred for hospitalisation in class B2/C wards, day surgery, and selected costly outpatient treatments (e.g., dialysis and chemotherapy) in the public hospitals.
▪ However, MediShield Life benefits are designed based on Class B2/C bills, so as to keep premiums affordable. Those who would like additional coverage in Class A/B1 wards or private hospitals may consider buying Integrated Shield Plans (IP).
▪ No need to apply for MediShield Life. All SCs and PRs are automatically covered under MediShield Life from 1 Nov 2015. New SCs are covered from birth or from the day they attain citizenship. PRs are covered from the day they attain permanent residency.
▪ MediShield Life premiums may be fully paid from MediSave.
▪ Please refer to the Study Text for a list of treatment items, procedures, conditions, activities which are not covered by MediShield Life and cannot be claimed. MediShield Life Premiums • Actuarially priced based on the health risks and expected healthcare utilisation of each age group so that each age group’s payouts are broadly supported by their own premiums.
▪ Those with pre-existing medical conditions can enjoy coverage for their conditions. Only those with serious pre-existing medical conditions listed in the MOH website need to pay a nominal Additional Premium of 30% for the first 10 years, in addition to the standard MediShield Life premiums.
▪ One can pay premiums from own MediSave, or family members may pay one’s premium using their MediSave. Parents may also tap on the MediSave Grant for newborns to pay for their child’s MediShield Life premiums. How Are MediShield Life Claim Payouts Computed?
▪ Pays on a reimbursement basis, subject to the claim limits imposed on the covered medical expenses, as well as deductible, co-insurance and pro-ration factors.
▪ The deductible is the fixed amount payable by the insured each policy year (the year following his policy renewal month), before the MediShield Life payout starts. The deductible is payable only once every policy year.
▪ Co-insurance is the patient’s share of the claimable amount which the insured will have to pay, along with the deductible. Please refer to the Study Text for example of how the reimbursement under MediShield Life is arrived at. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Integrated Shield Plans Integrated Shield Plans (IPs) are MediSave-approved hospitalisation insurance plans made up of two parts.
▪ MediShield Life component administered by the CPF Board.
▪ An additional private insurance coverage component administered by approved private insurers, typically to cover Class A/B1 wards of public hospitals or private hospitals. Following the implementation of MediShield Life on 1 November 2015, MediShield Life automatically replaced the MediShield component of IPs. Thus, those who have IPs are already covered by MediShield Life, and there is no duplication of coverage between IPs and MediShield Life. Consideration on Integrated Shield Plans (IP)
▪ With the different IPs available, it is necessary for the sellers of such plans to understand the different plans offered in the insurance market, and to advise their prospective clients, based on the needs and long-term affordability of each individual client’s position.
▪ As part of due diligence, clients should be informed of the key differences in premiums, benefits, exclusions, deductibles, co-insurance and other copayment features between the various IPs offered by their insurer, versus MediShield Life. There are different benefits provided in the IP policy, and plans can be “as charged” or “non -as-charged” (i.e. they have sub-limits imposed). Information provided should be easy to understand, unambiguous and clearly explained to help clients make an informed decision.
▪ Clients should consider if they would wish to stay in a private or subsidised ward in a public hospital, or in a private hospital, and if they wish to choose their own doctors.
▪ Clients should consider if they can afford their IP premiums in the long term as the policyholders may have to pay a substantial portion of their IP premiums in cash, especially at the older ages.
▪ Unlike MediShield Life which provides coverage for pre-existing medical conditions (with Additional Premiums for the first 10 years), the coverage for the private insurance component of the IP may be declined or imposed with exclusions for pre-existing conditions. The private insurance component may also have premium loading, arising from pre-existing conditions. How MediSave can be used to meet a CPF member’s healthcare needs and buy approved Medical Expense Insurance? MediSave can be used to pay premiums for IPs, either in full or in part, depending on the age group and the IP plan type. From 1 November 2015, MediSave use for the additional private insurance component of the IP premiums is subject to the new Additional Withdrawal Limit (AWL). The AWL is set per insured person per policy year, as stated below:
▪ Age Next Birthday (ANB) 40 years and below: S$300
▪ ANB 41 to 70 years: S$600
▪ ANB 71 years and above: S$900 How MediShield Life premiums are kept affordable?
▪ Premium Subsidies for lower- to middle-income.
▪ Pioneer and Merdeka Generation Subsidies.
▪ COVID-19 Subsidies.
▪ Additional premium support. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS How MediShield Life interacts with Integrated Shield Plans? MediShield Life Scheme Integrated Shield Plan (IP) It is administered by the CPF Board It is administered by private insurers, as approved by MOH. Its coverage is sized for stays in Class B2/C wards of public hospitals. It provides for enhanced coverage beyond MediShield Life, with various plan types available for stays in private hospitals and/ or Class A/B1 wards of public hospitals. It covers all pre-existing conditions. Coverage for the private insurance component of the IP may be declined or imposed with exclusions and/or premium loading, arising from pre-existing conditions. There is no minimum or maximum age limit. It may have a minimum or maximum entry age limit. There are sub-limits applicable. It may have sub-limits, although most do not have sub-limits. MediShield Life premiums are fully payable by MediSave. MediShield Life component of the IP is fully payable by MediSave, while private insurance component of the IP is payable by MediSave up to Additional Withdrawal Limit, and the remainder is payable by cash. All Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents are automatically covered for life. No application is required. Application to private insurers is required. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Standard IP • “No frills” product targeted at covering large Class B1 ward hospital bills and selected outpatient treatments.
▪ Affordable option for Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents who want additional coverage beyond MediShield Life and may find other higher coverage IPs too expensive. Riders • Pay for the co-insurance and deductible portion of the IP, and may pay cash payouts upon hospitalisation. Need for Co-Payments • Co-payment is an important principle in the design of healthcare insurance. It encourages policyholders and their doctors, to consider the necessity of the medical treatment and its cost, so that they can make an informed decision on the appropriate healthcare services. This encourages prudence and keeps healthcare cost, and health insurance premiums, affordable and sustainable in the long term. Terminating or Switching IP/Switching Insurer
▪ An IP policyholder who cannot afford, or does not wish to continue paying the premium for his IP, can switch his plan to a lower coverage plan within the same insurer at any point in time, without additional underwriting involved.
▪ He can also terminate his IP at any point in time, and still be covered by MediShield Life which provides basic health insurance, regardless of any preexisting conditions.
▪ If the person with an existing IP switches to a new IP with another insurer, Original IP with the previous insurer will be automatically terminated.
▪ Option to go back to the previous insurer within 30 days from the date of notification of termination from the previous insurer. Risk-Loading by private insurers
▪ MOH allows IP insurers to risk-load insured members with pre-existing conditions for the private insurance component of the IPs. Benefits in having Pre- Authorisation for IP
▪ Insurers can assess the medical necessity and cost of treatment to ensure it is within the terms and conditions of the policy’s coverage.
▪ Patients will have a peace of mind in knowing that their procedure is within their insurance coverage; and
▪ Healthcare providers have better clarity on the type of procedures covered by insurance to better advise their patients prior to the actual procedure. Policyholders are thus encouraged to seek pre-authorisation for planned procedures as far as possible if the option is provided by the insurer. Claims process of MediShield Life, Integrated Shield Plans, other Medical Expense Insurance and MediSave Any insured CPF member who wishes to claim from his MediShield Life, IP and/or MediSave will need to complete the Medical Claims Authorisation Form to:
▪ Allow the hospital to claim from his insurer; and
▪ Instruct the CPF Board to deduct from his MA to pay for his hospital bill, while the insurer settles the claim. For any insured CPF member who wants to claim from MediShield Life, IP and/or MediSave, the hospital will submit the claim on his behalf through the MediClaim online system after he has been discharged. If there is no pre-authorisation, the insured CPF member under an IP can approach the hospital to trigger a request for a Letter of Guarantee (LOG) from his insurer upon hospital admission. It is important that the insured CPF member understands that a LOG may not mean that the insurer will pay for the specified amount. To obtain assurance that their bill will be covered, the insured CPF member may wish to apply for pre-authorisation instead, if available. For someone who is covered by his employer medical benefits or other private insurance plans, he will need to show his insurance card (provided by some insurers for certain Medical Expense Insurance plans) to the hospital admission staff. The hospital may fully or partially waive the deposit in such a case.
▪ Some hospitals will send the hospitalisation bill directly to the CPF member’s non-IP private insurer or employer. In other cases, the insured CPF member will have to submit the bill himself to the insurer. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Withdrawal Limits for Inpatient, Outpatient, Long-Term Care and Insurance Premiums Please refer to the Study Text for the full list. MediShield Life Benefits Please refer to the Study Text for the full list. Claiming from MediShield Life/IP & MediSave Any patient who wishes to claim from his MediShield Life, IP, and/or MediSave will need to complete the Medical Claims Authorisation Form to:
▪ allow the hospital to claim from his insurer; and
▪ instruct the CPF Board to deduct from his MediSave to pay for his hospital bill, while the insurer settles the claim, which may take some time. Letter of Guarantee (LOG) Prior to a treatment episode, IP policyholders can approach the medical institution to request for a LOG from his insurer. The LOG is a service provided by IP insurers for policyholders, to waive the upfront cash required by the medical institution, up to the eligible LOG amount. ElderShield & ElderShield Supplements ElderShield is a long-term care insurance scheme launched in September 2002. It provides basic financial protection to those who need long-term care, especially during old age. ElderShield Supplement plans complement the current ElderShield scheme, by offering additional benefits at different pricing levels which increase the insured’s coverage through:
▪ increasing the monthly payout;
▪ extending the payout period; and/or
▪ increasing disability coverage How ElderShield and ElderShield Supplements work? To purchase and maintain an ElderShield Supplement, an insured must have an ElderShield policy. Premiums for ElderShield Supplement can be paid using MediSave (up to a limit of S$600 per insured per calendar year) and/or by cash. As ElderShield and ElderShield Supplements are structured as stand-alone policies, claims can be made under both policies if the insured fulfils the claim eligibility criteria. Supplement plans are currently (as of August 2024) only offered by the three approved insurers who were administering ElderShield prior to 1 November 2021. Eligibility Criteria For Payment Of ElderShield benefits
▪ Meets the waiting period requirement.
▪ Unable to perform the specified number of activities of daily living
▪ Meets the deferment period requirement (If he recovers from the disability within the period, then no benefits are payable).
▪ Please also note on making a claim under ElderShield and how the benefits are paid. Features of ElderShield • Non-forfeiture feature.
▪ Guaranteed renewability on an annual basis.
▪ Worldwide coverage.
▪ Minimum (i.e. 40 years old) and maximum entry age (i.e. 64 years old).
▪ Grace period of 75 days is allowed for payment of the overdue premiums.
▪ Reinstatement of the policy is allowed within 180 days from the expiry of the grace period, subject to evidence of insurability at the insured’s expense and payment of the overdue premiums with interest.
▪ Insurer has the right to appoint an assessor to examine the insured periodically.
▪ Where the insured is overseas at the time of a claim, the insurer has the right to commute the benefit payments to a single payment reflecting the present value of future benefit payments, or withhold the claim payment if it is unable to assess the claim after having made reasonable attempts to do so.
▪ No cash surrender value.
▪ Free-look period of 60 days from the policy commencement date during which the insured may cancel his policy, and receive a full refund of the premiums paid. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Exclusions of ElderShield • Self-inflicted injury, suicide or attempted suicide;
▪ Alcoholism or drug addiction; or
▪ War, whether declared or undeclared.
▪ CPF members who are suffering from pre-existing disabilities, will not be covered under ElderShield. Termination of cover for ElderShield
▪ Expiry of the grace period if the ElderShield policy is not eligible for the nonforfeiture benefit (i.e. it has been in force for too short a period for it to acquire sufficient reserves to be converted into a paid-up policy);
▪ Death of the insured;
▪ Date in which the last benefit payment has been received;
▪ No longer an SC/SPR from 1 Apr 2024 (following the closure of CPF foreigner accounts); or
▪ Date in which the written notice from the insured to cancel the policy is received by the insurer (if the policy does not have sufficient reserves, it will not be converted into a paid-up policy). ElderShield to CareShield Life
▪ From 1 November 2021, the Government has taken over the administration of ElderShield from private insurers (Singlife, Great Eastern Life Assurance Co Ltd, and Income Insurance Limited). This allows ElderShield policyholders to upgrade to CareShield Life more smoothly, and benefit from improvements to the claims assessment process that is being implemented for CareShield Life.
▪ ElderShield policyholders who did not upgrade to CareShield Life remain covered by their existing ElderShield policies. The Government administers the ElderShield scheme on a not-for-profit basis. In the event that the actual claims experience turns out better than expected, there will continue to be premium rebates for ElderShield policyholders.
▪ Holders of ElderShield Supplements were not affected. They continue to be served by their existing ElderShield Supplement insurers. CareShield Life & CareShield Life Supplements CareShield Life was introduced in 2020, as an enhancement of ElderShield and is administered by the Government on a not-for-profit basis, and offers;
▪ Better protection and higher payouts that increase over time, so that claimants can better afford their basic long-term care needs; and
▪ Protection for life. Any CareShield Life policyholder who wishes to obtain higher long-term care insurance coverage can also purchase Supplements. Supplements are plans which complement one’s basic CareShield Life plan by offering additional benefits, such as higher monthly payout amounts, which enhances the coverage. CareShield Life premium subsidies and support To ensure that no one will lose CareShield Life coverage due to genuine inability to pay, the Government provides the following premium subsidies and support:
▪ Means-tested premium subsidies of up to 30% of premiums of CareShield Life, to help lower- to middle-income households.
▪ Participation incentives of up to $2,500 for Singapore Citizens, if they join CareShield Life by 31 December 2023, to encourage those born in 1979 or earlier to join CareShield Life.
▪ Additional participation incentives of S$1,500 for Merdeka and Pioneer Generation citizens;
▪ Participation incentives of up to S$1,875 for Singapore Citizens if they join and are covered under CareShield Life between 1 January 2024 and 31 December 2024. Additional participation incentives of S$1,125 are provided for Merdeka and Pioneer Generation citizens; and
▪ Additional Premium Support for Singaporeans who are unable to pay their CareShield Life premiums even after premium subsidies, so that no one will lose coverage under CareShield Life due to genuine inability to pay premiums. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Enhanced benefits under CareShield Life CareShield Life features higher payouts that increase over time with no cap on payout duration, to provide better protection against the uncertainty of long-term care costs in the event of severe disability.
▪ Lifetime cash payouts for as long as the policyholder is severely disabled.
▪ Payouts increase until age 67 or when a claim is made, whichever is earlier, with payouts starting at S$600 per month in 2020. CareShield Life Premiums • The CareShield Life scheme makes use of pre-funded premiums i.e. the insured CPF members pay more than the cost of their health risk, while they are younger, to cover for the higher risk in their later years.
▪ For Singapore Residents born 1980 or later (future cohorts), premiums are paid from the age of enrolment until age 67. Premiums will increase over time, to support payout increases.
▪ Singapore Residents born in 1979 or earlier (existing cohorts) will pay a base premium.
▪ Fully payable by MediSave. Eligibility Criteria for Payment of CareShield Life Benefits Similar to that of ElderShield. Other key features of CareShield Life
▪ Guaranteed renewable on an annual basis.
▪ Worldwide coverage.
▪ Minimum entry age of 30 years old, but no maximum entry age.
▪ CareShield Life policy will be terminated if the insured is no longer a Singapore Citizen or SPR.
▪ No cash surrender value. Other healthcare financing schemes set up by the Singapore government
▪ Pioneer Generation Package;
▪ Merdeka Generation Package;
▪ Majulah Package;
▪ Interim Disability Assistance Programme For The Elderly (IDAPE);
▪ ElderFund and
▪ Medifund. Interim Disability Assistance Programme For The Elderly (IDAPE)
▪ IDAPE was launched by the Government to take care of Singaporeans who were not eligible to join the ElderShield scheme, when it was launched in September 2002, either because they exceeded the maximum entry age or they had a preexisting disability. Singaporean will qualify for IDAPE if:
▪ He is unable to perform three or more of the 6 Activities of Daily Living (ADL) - washing, feeding, dressing, toileting, mobility and transferring.
▪ He is a Singapore citizen born before 30 September 1932 OR born between 1 October 1932 and 30 September 1962 (both dates inclusive) but with preexisting disabilities as at 30 September 2002.
▪ His per capita household monthly income is $2,800 or less OR he has no household income and is living in a residence with Annual Value of $13,000 and below. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Pioneer Generation Package The “Pioneer Generation” refers to living Singaporeans who meet two criteria, namely: a. aged 16 years and above in 1965 (born on or before 31 December 1949, which also means that they were aged 65 years and above in 2014); and b. obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1986. The package provides the benefits (as described below) which the Pioneer Generation will enjoy for life: 1. Outpatient Care 2. MediShield Life 3. MediSave Top-Ups 4. Pioneer Generation Disability Assistance Scheme 5. CareShield Life Merdeka Generation Package The “Merdeka Generation” refers to living Singaporeans who: a. were born from 1 January 1950 to 31 December 1959, and b. had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996. The MGP is also extended to living Singaporeans who: a. were born on or before 31 December 1949, b. had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996, and c. do not receive the Pioneer Generation Package. The package provides the benefits (as described below) which the Pioneer Generation will enjoy for life: 1. Passion Silver Card Top-Ups 2. MediSave Top-Ups 3. Outpatient Care 4. MediShield Life 5. CareShield Life IDAPE Payout Per Capita Household Income* IDAPE Payout (With Effect From 1 July 2013) (a) S$0 to S$2,000 (a) S$250 per month for up to 72 months (b) S$2,001 to S$2,800 (b) S$150 per month for up to 72 months *An IDAPE applicant from a household with no income will qualify for the S$250 monthly payout if the annual value of his place of residence is S$13,000 or lower. ElderFund ElderFund is a discretionary assistance scheme launched on 31 January 2020. It is targeted at severely disabled lower-income Singapore Citizens aged 30 and older, who are not able to benefit from CareShield Life, ElderShield, and the Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (IDAPE), have low MediSave balances and have inadequate personal savings to meet their long-term care needs. MediFund MediFund is an endowment fund set up by the Government. It provides a safety net for needy Singaporeans who face difficulties paying for their remaining healthcare bills after Government subsidies, insurance and MediSave.

The patient’s application will be assessed holistically by the medical social workers and the メディファンド Committees, taking into consideration his and his family’s financial, health and social circumstances. HEALTHCARE FINANCING PART I & II CHAPTER IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Singapore government subsidies
▪ Government provides funding to public healthcare institutions (e.g. public hospitals, national specialty centres and polyclinics), as well as selected private clinics and voluntary welfare organisations (VWOs).
▪ Means-test is used to target subsidies, so that needy patients can receive more help; lower-income patients receive more subsidies than higher-income patients. Singapore's Healthcare Financing Philosophy Through a mixed financing system (see figure below), Singapore has secured good healthcare outcomes for its population. For example, life expectancy at birth has increased from 80.3 in 2006 to 83.93 in 2022, and infant mortality rate improved from 2.6 per thousand live births to 1.8 per thousand live births as of 2020. The Government has done so with a current national health expenditure of about 4.4% of the country’s Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in 2019, a percentage which is low among developed countries (although this is expected to grow with an ageing population over the years). Main components of Singapore’s Healthcare Financing System メディセーブ • National healthcare savings scheme in Singapore under the Central Provident Board (CPF（中央積立基金）) umbrella.
▪ Contributions to the メディセーブ Account (MA) are subject to a maximum amount, known as the Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS). The BHS is designed to be enough for a CPF（中央積立基金） member’s basic, subsidised healthcare needs in his old age. Hence, amounts in the MA up to the BHS cannot be withdrawn as cash.
▪ Amounts above the BHS will flow to the CPF（中央積立基金） member’s Special or Retirement Accounts to boost his monthly payouts. If the CPF（中央積立基金） member has already met his Full Retirement Sum, the amounts will flow to his Ordinary Account, and can be withdrawn as cash from the age of 55 years.
▪ Can be used to pay for the CPF（中央積立基金） member’s medical expenses at all accredited institutions, in both the public and private sector.
▪ Can also be used to pay for medical expenses of the CPF（中央積立基金） member’s approved dependents, namely his spouse, children, parents, siblings, and grandparents. If paying for a sibling or grandparent, the patient must be a Singapore Citizen or Singapore Permanent Resident (SPR). IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Community Health Assist Scheme (地域医療支援スキーム) The Community Health Assist Scheme (地域医療支援スキーム) enables eligible Singapore Citizens to receive subsidies for medical and/or dental care at participating General Practitioner (GP) and dental clinics. With effect from Nov 2019, this scheme has been expanded to include all Singapore Citizens regardless of their income levels. The colour of the 地域医療支援スキーム card indicates the subsidy tier that cardholders are entitled to, namely the 地域医療支援スキーム Blue, Orange or Green tier. All パイオニア世代 (PG) and ムルデカ世代 (MG) seniors also receive special subsidies at 地域医療支援スキーム clinics. The amount that cardholders pay will be based on the fees charged by the clinic, less the 地域医療支援スキーム subsidies. The fees will vary according to each patient’s condition and clinic’s charges. Uses of メディセーブ Account Uses of メディセーブ Items that can be paid using メディセーブ 外来 Treatments Selected 外来 treatments such as:
▪ Approved chronic conditions under the chronic disease management programme
▪ Vaccinations
▪ フレキシ・メディセーブ for the elderly
▪ Health screenings 入院患者 Treatments 入院患者 care which includes:
▪ 入院患者 episodes
▪ 入院患者 psychiatric episodes
▪ Day surgery
▪ Assisted Conception Procedures Long-Term Care Long-Term Care treatments which include:
▪ Rehabilitative care
▪ 緩和ケア
▪ Disability care Insurance 保険料s 保険料 payments of:
▪ メディシールド・ライフ
▪ エルダーシールド/ケアシールド・ライフ which can be fully paid with メディセーブ
▪ Integrated Shield Plans and エルダーシールド/ケアシールド・ライフ supplements which are subject to withdrawal limits periodically reviewed by MOH（保健省） How much メディセーブ can be used The use of メディセーブ is subject to:
▪ Withdrawal Limit.
▪ 追加引出限度額. Payments via メディセーブ are allowed in all public healthcare institutions, as well as approved private hospitals and medical institutions IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Distribution Of メディセーブ Upon Demise
▪ If a patient was hospitalised just before death and had authorised the use of his メディセーブ to pay for his medical bill, his メディセーブ balance would be used in full, without being subject to the existing メディセーブ withdrawal limits, to pay for his last 入院患者 medical bill.
▪ If the patient had not authorised the use of his メディセーブ before his demise, his immediate family member (spouse, parent, or child who is aged 21 years and above), or donee or deputy can also do so for the last 入院患者 medical bill, provided that it has not been paid out to the patient’s nominated beneficiaries.
▪ In the event that the patient does not have any immediate family member, or donee or deputy to authorise the use of the patient’s メディセーブ, a relative who has been taking care of the patient may also write to MOH（保健省） through the hospital to seek approval for the relative to authorise the withdrawal of patient’s メディセーブ, to pay for the last 入院患者 medical bill.
▪ Remaining メディセーブ balance, after the payment of the last medical bill, would be distributed to the nominated beneficiaries of the patient’s CPF（中央積立基金） account if a nomination was made before his death. If there was no nomination made, the balance in the deceased patient’s MA would be distributed by the Public Trustee to his family members under the intestacy laws for non-Muslims, or the Muslim inheritance law for Muslims. メディシールド・ライフ • Basic healthcare insurance scheme.
▪ Covers all Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents against large medical bills for life, regardless of age or health conditions.
▪ The 保険金請求 limits are sized to cover subsidised bills incurred for 入院 in class B2/C wards, day surgery, and selected costly 外来 treatments (e.g., dialysis and chemotherapy) in the public hospitals.
▪ However, メディシールド・ライフ 給付s are designed based on Class B2/C bills, so as to keep 保険料s affordable. Those who would like additional 補償 in Class A/B1 wards or private hospitals may consider buying Integrated Shield Plans (IP).
▪ No need to apply for メディシールド・ライフ. All SCs and PRs are automatically covered under メディシールド・ライフ from 1 Nov 2015. New SCs are covered from birth or from the day they attain citizenship. PRs are covered from the day they attain permanent residency.
▪ メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s may be fully paid from メディセーブ.
▪ Please refer to the Study Text for a list of treatment items, procedures, conditions, activities which are not covered by メディシールド・ライフ and cannot be 保険金請求ed. メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s • Actuarially priced based on the health risks and expected healthcare utilisation of each age group so that each age group’s payouts are broadly supported by their own 保険料s.
▪ Those with pre-existing medical conditions can enjoy 補償 for their conditions. Only those with serious pre-existing medical conditions listed in the MOH（保健省） website need to pay a nominal Additional 保険料 of 30% for the first 10 years, in addition to the standard メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s.
▪ One can pay 保険料s from own メディセーブ, or family members may pay one’s 保険料 using their メディセーブ. Parents may also tap on the メディセーブ Grant for newborns to pay for their child’s メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s. How Are メディシールド・ライフ 保険金請求 Payouts Computed?
▪ Pays on a 実費精算・償還払い basis, subject to the 保険金請求 limits imposed on the covered medical expenses, as well as 免責金額, 共同保険 and 按分係数s.
▪ The 免責金額 is the fixed amount payable by the 被保険者 each policy year (the year following his policy renewal month), before the メディシールド・ライフ payout starts. The 免責金額 is payable only once every policy year.
▪ 共同保険 is the patient’s share of the 保険金請求able amount which the 被保険者 will have to pay, along with the 免責金額. Please refer to the Study Text for example of how the 実費精算・償還払い under メディシールド・ライフ is arrived at. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Integrated Shield Plans Integrated Shield Plans (IPs) are メディセーブ-approved 入院 insurance plans made up of two parts.
▪ メディシールド・ライフ component administered by the CPF（中央積立基金） Board.
▪ An additional private insurance 補償 component administered by approved private 保険者s, typically to cover Class A/B1 wards of public hospitals or private hospitals. Following the implementation of メディシールド・ライフ on 1 November 2015, メディシールド・ライフ automatically replaced the MediShield component of IPs. Thus, those who have IPs are already covered by メディシールド・ライフ, and there is no duplication of 補償 between IPs and メディシールド・ライフ. Consideration on Integrated Shield Plans (IP)
▪ With the different IPs available, it is necessary for the sellers of such plans to understand the different plans offered in the insurance market, and to advise their prospective clients, based on the needs and long-term affordability of each individual client’s position.
▪ As part of due diligence, clients should be informed of the key differences in 保険料s, 給付s, 免責事由s, 免責金額s, 共同保険 and other copayment features between the various IPs offered by their 保険者, versus メディシールド・ライフ. There are different 給付s provided in the IP policy, and plans can be “実費払い” or “non -as-charged” (i.e. they have 支払限度額（サブリミット）s imposed). Information provided should be easy to understand, unambiguous and clearly explained to help clients make an informed decision.
▪ Clients should consider if they would wish to stay in a private or subsidised ward in a public hospital, or in a private hospital, and if they wish to choose their own doctors.
▪ Clients should consider if they can afford their IP 保険料s in the long term as the 保険契約者s may have to pay a substantial portion of their IP 保険料s in cash, especially at the older ages.
▪ Unlike メディシールド・ライフ which provides 補償 for pre-existing medical conditions (with Additional 保険料s for the first 10 years), the 補償 for the private insurance component of the IP may be declined or imposed with 免責事由s for 既存疾病s. The private insurance component may also have 保険料 loading, arising from 既存疾病s. How メディセーブ can be used to meet a CPF（中央積立基金） member’s healthcare needs and buy approved 医療費保険? メディセーブ can be used to pay 保険料s for IPs, either in full or in part, depending on the age group and the IP plan type. From 1 November 2015, メディセーブ use for the additional private insurance component of the IP 保険料s is subject to the new 追加引出限度額. The AWL is set per 被保険者 person per policy year, as stated below:
▪ Age Next Birthday (ANB) 40 years and below: S$300
▪ ANB 41 to 70 years: S$600
▪ ANB 71 years and above: S$900 How メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s are kept affordable?
▪ 保険料 Subsidies for lower- to middle-income.
▪ Pioneer and ムルデカ世代 Subsidies.
▪ COVID-19 Subsidies.
▪ Additional 保険料 support. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS How メディシールド・ライフ interacts with Integrated Shield Plans? メディシールド・ライフ Scheme 統合シールドプラン It is administered by the CPF（中央積立基金） Board It is administered by private 保険者s, as approved by MOH（保健省）. Its 補償 is sized for stays in Class B2/C wards of public hospitals. It provides for enhanced 補償 beyond メディシールド・ライフ, with various plan types available for stays in private hospitals and/ or Class A/B1 wards of public hospitals. It covers all 既存疾病s. 補償 for the private insurance component of the IP may be declined or imposed with 免責事由s and/or 保険料 loading, arising from 既存疾病s. There is no minimum or maximum age limit. It may have a minimum or maximum entry age limit. There are 支払限度額（サブリミット）s applicable. It may have 支払限度額（サブリミット）s, although most do not have 支払限度額（サブリミット）s. メディシールド・ライフ 保険料s are fully payable by メディセーブ. メディシールド・ライフ component of the IP is fully payable by メディセーブ, while private insurance component of the IP is payable by メディセーブ up to Additional Withdrawal Limit, and the remainder is payable by cash. All Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents are automatically covered for life. No application is required. Application to private 保険者s is required. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS スタンダードIP • “No frills” product targeted at covering large Class B1 ward hospital bills and selected 外来 treatments.
▪ Affordable option for Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents who want additional 補償 beyond メディシールド・ライフ and may find other higher 補償 IPs too expensive. 特約s • Pay for the 共同保険 and 免責金額 portion of the IP, and may pay cash payouts upon 入院. Need for 自己負担s • 自己負担 is an important principle in the design of healthcare insurance. It encourages 保険契約者s and their doctors, to consider the necessity of the medical treatment and its cost, so that they can make an informed decision on the appropriate healthcare services. This encourages prudence and keeps healthcare cost, and health insurance 保険料s, affordable and sustainable in the long term. Terminating or Switching IP/Switching 保険者
▪ An IP 保険契約者 who cannot afford, or does not wish to continue paying the 保険料 for his IP, can switch his plan to a lower 補償 plan within the same 保険者 at any point in time, without additional 引受審査 involved.
▪ He can also terminate his IP at any point in time, and still be covered by メディシールド・ライフ which provides basic health insurance, regardless of any preexisting conditions.
▪ If the person with an existing IP switches to a new IP with another 保険者, Original IP with the previous 保険者 will be automatically terminated.
▪ Option to go back to the previous 保険者 within 30 days from the date of notification of termination from the previous 保険者. Risk-Loading by private 保険者s
▪ MOH（保健省） allows IP 保険者s to risk-load 被保険者 members with 既存疾病s for the private insurance component of the IPs. 給付s in having Pre- Authorisation for IP
▪ 保険者s can assess the medical necessity and cost of treatment to ensure it is within the terms and conditions of the policy’s 補償.
▪ Patients will have a peace of mind in knowing that their procedure is within their insurance 補償; and
▪ Healthcare providers have better clarity on the type of procedures covered by insurance to better advise their patients prior to the actual procedure. 保険契約者s are thus encouraged to seek 事前承認 for planned procedures as far as possible if the option is provided by the 保険者. 保険金請求s process of メディシールド・ライフ, Integrated Shield Plans, other 医療費保険 and メディセーブ Any 被保険者 CPF（中央積立基金） member who wishes to 保険金請求 from his メディシールド・ライフ, IP and/or メディセーブ will need to complete the Medical 保険金請求s Authorisation Form to:
▪ Allow the hospital to 保険金請求 from his 保険者; and
▪ Instruct the CPF（中央積立基金） Board to deduct from his MA to pay for his hospital bill, while the 保険者 settles the 保険金請求. For any 被保険者 CPF（中央積立基金） member who wants to 保険金請求 from メディシールド・ライフ, IP and/or メディセーブ, the hospital will submit the 保険金請求 on his behalf through the Medi保険金請求 online system after he has been discharged. If there is no 事前承認, the 被保険者 CPF（中央積立基金） member under an IP can approach the hospital to trigger a request for a 保証状 from his 保険者 upon hospital admission. It is important that the 被保険者 CPF（中央積立基金） member understands that a LOG may not mean that the 保険者 will pay for the specified amount. To obtain assurance that their bill will be covered, the 被保険者 CPF（中央積立基金） member may wish to apply for 事前承認 instead, if available. For someone who is covered by his employer medical 給付s or other private insurance plans, he will need to show his insurance card (provided by some 保険者s for certain 医療費保険 plans) to the hospital admission staff. The hospital may fully or partially waive the deposit in such a case.
▪ Some hospitals will send the 入院 bill directly to the CPF（中央積立基金） member’s non-IP private 保険者 or employer. In other cases, the 被保険者 CPF（中央積立基金） member will have to submit the bill himself to the 保険者. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Withdrawal Limits for 入院患者, 外来, Long-Term Care and Insurance 保険料s Please refer to the Study Text for the full list. メディシールド・ライフ 給付s Please refer to the Study Text for the full list. 保険金請求ing from メディシールド・ライフ/IP & メディセーブ Any patient who wishes to 保険金請求 from his メディシールド・ライフ, IP, and/or メディセーブ will need to complete the Medical 保険金請求s Authorisation Form to:
▪ allow the hospital to 保険金請求 from his 保険者; and
▪ instruct the CPF（中央積立基金） Board to deduct from his メディセーブ to pay for his hospital bill, while the 保険者 settles the 保険金請求, which may take some time. 保証状 Prior to a treatment episode, IP 保険契約者s can approach the medical institution to request for a LOG from his 保険者. The LOG is a service provided by IP 保険者s for 保険契約者s, to waive the upfront cash required by the medical institution, up to the eligible LOG amount. エルダーシールド & エルダーシールド Supplements エルダーシールド is a long-term care insurance scheme launched in September 2002. It provides basic financial protection to those who need long-term care, especially during old age. エルダーシールド Supplement plans complement the current エルダーシールド scheme, by offering additional 給付s at different pricing levels which increase the 被保険者’s 補償 through:
▪ increasing the monthly payout;
▪ extending the payout period; and/or
▪ increasing disability 補償 How エルダーシールド and エルダーシールド Supplements work? To pur地域医療支援スキームe and maintain an エルダーシールド Supplement, an 被保険者 must have an エルダーシールド policy. 保険料s for エルダーシールド Supplement can be paid using メディセーブ (up to a limit of S$600 per 被保険者 per calendar year) and/or by cash. As エルダーシールド and エルダーシールド Supplements are structured as stand-alone policies, 保険金請求s can be made under both policies if the 被保険者 fulfils the 保険金請求 eligibility criteria. Supplement plans are currently (as of August 2024) only offered by the three approved 保険者s who were administering エルダーシールド prior to 1 November 2021. Eligibility Criteria For Payment Of エルダーシールド 給付s
▪ Meets the 待機期間 requirement.
▪ Unable to perform the specified number of activities of daily living
▪ Meets the deferment period requirement (If he recovers from the disability within the period, then no 給付s are payable).
▪ Please also note on making a 保険金請求 under エルダーシールド and how the 給付s are paid. Features of エルダーシールド • Non-forfeiture feature.
▪ Guaranteed renewability on an annual basis.
▪ Worldwide 補償.
▪ Minimum (i.e. 40 years old) and maximum entry age (i.e. 64 years old).
▪ 猶予期間 of 75 days is allowed for payment of the overdue 保険料s.
▪ 復活 of the policy is allowed within 180 days from the expiry of the 猶予期間, subject to evidence of insurability at the 被保険者’s expense and payment of the overdue 保険料s with interest.
▪ 保険者 has the right to appoint an assessor to examine the 被保険者 periodically.
▪ Where the 被保険者 is overseas at the time of a 保険金請求, the 保険者 has the right to commute the 給付 payments to a single payment reflecting the present value of future 給付 payments, or withhold the 保険金請求 payment if it is unable to assess the 保険金請求 after having made reasonable attempts to do so.
▪ No cash surrender value.
▪ クーリングオフ期間 of 60 days from the policy commencement date during which the 被保険者 may cancel his policy, and receive a full refund of the 保険料s paid. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS 免責事由s of エルダーシールド • 自傷行為, suicide or attempted suicide;
▪ Alcoholism or drug addiction; or
▪ War, whether declared or undeclared.
▪ CPF（中央積立基金） members who are suffering from pre-existing disabilities, will not be covered under エルダーシールド. Termination of cover for エルダーシールド
▪ Expiry of the 猶予期間 if the エルダーシールド policy is not eligible for the nonforfeiture 給付 (i.e. it has been in force for too short a period for it to acquire sufficient reserves to be converted into a paid-up policy);
▪ Death of the 被保険者;
▪ Date in which the last 給付 payment has been received;
▪ No longer an SC/SPR from 1 Apr 2024 (following the closure of CPF（中央積立基金） foreigner accounts); or
▪ Date in which the written notice from the 被保険者 to cancel the policy is received by the 保険者 (if the policy does not have sufficient reserves, it will not be converted into a paid-up policy). エルダーシールド to ケアシールド・ライフ
▪ From 1 November 2021, the Government has taken over the administration of エルダーシールド from private 保険者s (Singlife, Great Eastern Life Assurance Co Ltd, and Income Insurance Limited). This allows エルダーシールド 保険契約者s to upgrade to ケアシールド・ライフ more smoothly, and 給付 from improvements to the 保険金請求s assessment process that is being implemented for ケアシールド・ライフ.
▪ エルダーシールド 保険契約者s who did not upgrade to ケアシールド・ライフ remain covered by their existing エルダーシールド policies. The Government administers the エルダーシールド scheme on a not-for-profit basis. In the event that the actual 保険金請求s experience turns out better than expected, there will continue to be 保険料 rebates for エルダーシールド 保険契約者s.
▪ Holders of エルダーシールド Supplements were not affected. They continue to be served by their existing エルダーシールド Supplement 保険者s. ケアシールド・ライフ & ケアシールド・ライフ Supplements ケアシールド・ライフ was introduced in 2020, as an enhancement of エルダーシールド and is administered by the Government on a not-for-profit basis, and offers;
▪ Better protection and higher payouts that increase over time, so that 保険金請求ants can better afford their basic long-term care needs; and
▪ Protection for life. Any ケアシールド・ライフ 保険契約者 who wishes to obtain higher long-term care insurance 補償 can also pur地域医療支援スキームe Supplements. Supplements are plans which complement one’s basic ケアシールド・ライフ plan by offering additional 給付s, such as higher monthly payout amounts, which enhances the 補償. ケアシールド・ライフ 保険料 subsidies and support To ensure that no one will lose ケアシールド・ライフ 補償 due to genuine inability to pay, the Government provides the following 保険料 subsidies and support:
▪ Means-tested 保険料 subsidies of up to 30% of 保険料s of ケアシールド・ライフ, to help lower- to middle-income households.
▪ Participation incentives of up to $2,500 for Singapore Citizens, if they join ケアシールド・ライフ by 31 December 2023, to encourage those born in 1979 or earlier to join ケアシールド・ライフ.
▪ Additional participation incentives of S$1,500 for Merdeka and パイオニア世代 citizens;
▪ Participation incentives of up to S$1,875 for Singapore Citizens if they join and are covered under ケアシールド・ライフ between 1 January 2024 and 31 December 2024. Additional participation incentives of S$1,125 are provided for Merdeka and パイオニア世代 citizens; and
▪ Additional 保険料 Support for Singaporeans who are unable to pay their ケアシールド・ライフ 保険料s even after 保険料 subsidies, so that no one will lose 補償 under ケアシールド・ライフ due to genuine inability to pay 保険料s. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Enhanced 給付s under ケアシールド・ライフ ケアシールド・ライフ features higher payouts that increase over time with no cap on payout duration, to provide better protection against the uncertainty of long-term care costs in the event of 重度障害.
▪ Lifetime cash payouts for as long as the 保険契約者 is severely disabled.
▪ Payouts increase until age 67 or when a 保険金請求 is made, whichever is earlier, with payouts starting at S$600 per month in 2020. ケアシールド・ライフ 保険料s • The ケアシールド・ライフ scheme makes use of pre-funded 保険料s i.e. the 被保険者 CPF（中央積立基金） members pay more than the cost of their health risk, while they are younger, to cover for the higher risk in their later years.
▪ For Singapore Residents born 1980 or later (future cohorts), 保険料s are paid from the age of enrolment until age 67. 保険料s will increase over time, to support payout increases.
▪ Singapore Residents born in 1979 or earlier (existing cohorts) will pay a base 保険料.
▪ Fully payable by メディセーブ. Eligibility Criteria for Payment of ケアシールド・ライフ 給付s Similar to that of エルダーシールド. Other key features of ケアシールド・ライフ
▪ 更新保証 on an annual basis.
▪ Worldwide 補償.
▪ Minimum entry age of 30 years old, but no maximum entry age.
▪ ケアシールド・ライフ policy will be terminated if the 被保険者 is no longer a Singapore Citizen or SPR.
▪ No cash surrender value. Other healthcare financing schemes set up by the Singapore government
▪ パイオニア世代 Package;
▪ ムルデカ世代 Package;
▪ マジュラパッケージ;
▪ Interim Disability Assistance Programme For The Elderly (傷害・健康保険販売事業体);
▪ エルダーファンド and
▪ メディファンド. Interim Disability Assistance Programme For The Elderly (傷害・健康保険販売事業体)
▪ 傷害・健康保険販売事業体 was launched by the Government to take care of Singaporeans who were not eligible to join the エルダーシールド scheme, when it was launched in September 2002, either because they exceeded the maximum entry age or they had a preexisting disability. Singaporean will qualify for 傷害・健康保険販売事業体 if:
▪ He is unable to perform three or more of the 6 Activities of Daily Living (ADL) - washing, feeding, dressing, toileting, mobility and transferring.
▪ He is a Singapore citizen born before 30 September 1932 OR born between 1 October 1932 and 30 September 1962 (both dates inclusive) but with preexisting disabilities as at 30 September 2002.
▪ His per capita household monthly income is $2,800 or less OR he has no household income and is living in a residence with Annual Value of $13,000 and below. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS パイオニア世代 Package The “パイオニア世代” refers to living Singaporeans who meet two criteria, namely: a. aged 16 years and above in 1965 (born on or before 31 December 1949, which also means that they were aged 65 years and above in 2014); and b. obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1986. The package provides the 給付s (as described below) which the パイオニア世代 will enjoy for life: 1. 外来 Care 2. メディシールド・ライフ 3. メディセーブ Top-Ups 4. パイオニア世代 Disability Assistance Scheme 5. ケアシールド・ライフ ムルデカ世代 Package The “ムルデカ世代” refers to living Singaporeans who: a. were born from 1 January 1950 to 31 December 1959, and b. had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996. The MGP is also extended to living Singaporeans who: a. were born on or before 31 December 1949, b. had obtained citizenship on or before 31 December 1996, and c. do not receive the パイオニア世代 Package. The package provides the 給付s (as described below) which the パイオニア世代 will enjoy for life: 1. Passion Silver Card Top-Ups 2. メディセーブ Top-Ups 3. 外来 Care 4. メディシールド・ライフ 5. ケアシールド・ライフ 傷害・健康保険販売事業体 Payout Per Capita Household Income* 傷害・健康保険販売事業体 Payout (With Effect From 1 July 2013) (a) S$0 to S$2,000 (a) S$250 per month for up to 72 months (b) S$2,001 to S$2,800 (b) S$150 per month for up to 72 months *An 傷害・健康保険販売事業体 applicant from a household with no income will qualify for the S$250 monthly payout if the annual value of his place of residence is S$13,000 or lower. エルダーファンド エルダーファンド is a discretionary assistance scheme launched on 31 January 2020. It is targeted at severely disabled lower-income Singapore Citizens aged 30 and older, who are not able to 給付 from ケアシールド・ライフ, エルダーシールド, and the Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (傷害・健康保険販売事業体), have low メディセーブ balances and have inadequate personal savings to meet their long-term care needs. メディファンド メディファンド is an endowment fund set up by the Government. It provides a safety net for needy Singaporeans who face difficulties paying for their remaining healthcare bills after Government subsidies, insurance and メディセーブ.

chapters.title: COMMON POLICY PROVISIONS
learningOutcomes: list the sections of a Health Insurance policy contract
know the information contained in a Health Insurance policy schedule
outline what an insuring clause is

define the following common terms under a Health Insurance policy: - insured/insured person - accident - emergency - hospital - covered charges - day of hospital confinement - registered medical practitioner/physician - medically necessary treatment - period of hospital confinement - pre-existing condition - policyholder/policy owner - reasonable expenses - waiting period - illness - pre-hospitalisation benefits - per policy year limit - lifetime limit - deductible and co-insurance - pro-ration factor

explain the general conditions sections that outline the rights of the policy owner and the insurer: - entire contract clause - effective date of cover - premium clauses - free-look period - actively at work - termination of cover - overseas treatment renewal - misstatement of age or gender - grace period - reinstatement - incontestability - change of occupation - co-ordination of benefits - cancellation - change of plan - currency - last payer status - nomination of beneficiaries - Policy Owners’ Protection Scheme
explain the various bases upon which Health Insurance policies can be issued: - cancellable - optionally renewable - conditionally renewable - guaranteed renewable - non-renewable
understand the purposes and functions of the benefit provisions section of the Health Insurance policy
list the common exclusions that are found in Health Insurance policies

explain some of the important general claim conditions commonly found in Health Insurance policies: - notification of claim condition - physical examination provision - mediation/arbitration and legal actions provision
explain what an endorsement is

paragraphs.text: A Health Insurance policy is the written evidence of a contract between the insurer and the policy owner who can be an individual or a corporation. It specifies the precise terms of the agreement between the two parties to the contract. The details of the contract terms are clearly laid out in various policy provisions. This chapter will describe the common and important provisions of Individual and Group Health Insurance contracts.
paragraphs.textJP: A Health Insurance policy is the written evidence of a contract between the 保険者 and the policy owner who can be an individual or a corporation. It specifies the precise terms of the agreement between the two parties to the contract. The details of the contract terms are clearly laid out in various policy provisions. This chapter will describe the common and important provisions of Individual and Group Health Insurance contracts.

paragraphs.text: It is very important to fully understand the various policy provisions to avoid any dispute later on.
paragraphs.textJP: It is very important to fully understand the various policy provisions to avoid any dispute later on.

paragraphs.text: There are various sections in a Health Insurance policy contract.
paragraphs.textJP: There are various sections in a Health Insurance policy contract.

paragraphs.text: The sequence or header of the sections in a policy document may vary among the different insurers.
paragraphs.textJP: The sequence or header of the sections in a policy document may vary among the different 保険者s.

paragraphs.text: The schedule contains details of the policy owner, insured person(s), as well as the insurance coverage, coverage limits and sum insured. The schedule is not a stand-alone document and must always be read together with the policy wording “as one contract”. The schedule will also outline additional clauses or exclusions specific to the policy owner’s own circumstances.
paragraphs.textJP: The schedule contains details of the policy owner, 被保険者 person(s), as well as the insurance 補償, 補償 limits and 保険金額. The schedule is not a stand-alone document and must always be read together with the policy wording “as one contract”. The schedule will also outline additional clauses or 免責事由s specific to the policy owner’s own circumstances.
