Among different age groups, there are differences in claim frequency and claim severity. For example, a 50-year-old person is more likely to need a longer recovery period than a 30-year-old person.
paragraphs.textJP: Age is a key parameter in arriving at the 保険料 rates, regardless of whether it is an individual or group plan. Among different age groups, there are differences in 保険金請求 frequency and 保険金請求 severity. For example, a 50-year-old person is more likely to need a longer recovery period than a 30-year-old person.

paragraphs.text: Statistics show that women in general made more medical claims than men. That is why a female pays a higher premium for Health Insurance such as Long-Term Care and Critical Illness as compared to a male of the same age. For simplicity, not all health insurance premiums are gender differentiated. For example, premiums for Medishield Life and Integrated Shield plans do not depend on gender but age.
paragraphs.textJP: Statistics show that women in general made more medical 保険金請求s than men. That is why a female pays a higher 保険料 for Health Insurance such as Long-Term Care and Critical Illness as compared to a male of the same age. For simplicity, not all health insurance 保険料s are gender differentiated. For example, 保険料s for メディシールド・ライフ and Integrated Shield plans do not depend on gender but age.

paragraphs.text: The health status of the proposed insured person is a major consideration in the policy pricing. An individual with a pre-existing medical condition can expect his proposal to attract special underwriting terms and conditions, or even be declined.
paragraphs.textJP: The health status of the proposed 被保険者 person is a major consideration in the policy pricing. An individual with a pre-existing medical condition can expect his proposal to attract special 引受審査 terms and conditions, or even be declined.

paragraphs.text: The lifestyle of the proposed insured can also influence the premium charge. Participation in a hazardous sport which exposes him to a higher risk of injuries (e.g. recreational flying, mountaineering or scuba-diving) will require extra premiums or coverage exclusions of the activities.
paragraphs.textJP: The lifestyle of the proposed 被保険者 can also influence the 保険料 charge. Participation in a hazardous sport which exposes him to a higher risk of injuries (e.g. recreational flying, mountaineering or scuba-diving) will require extra 保険料s or 補償 免責事由s of the activities.

paragraphs.text: The most important lifestyle factor for determining premium rate is smoking. A non-smoker can expect to pay a lower premium than a smoker.
paragraphs.textJP: The most important lifestyle factor for determining 保険料 rate is smoking. A non-smoker can expect to pay a lower 保険料 than a smoker.

paragraphs.text: A proposed insured’s occupation can have a strong influence on the risk that he presents. Some occupations may pose a higher risk of injuries or illnesses.
paragraphs.textJP: A proposed 被保険者’s occupation can have a strong influence on the risk that he presents. Some occupations may pose a higher risk of injuries or illnesses.

paragraphs.text: Insurers group occupations into various classes according to the risk of accident or illness and use it to establish premium rates based upon the occupational hazard. For example, a high-rise construction worker could be more likely to suffer an injury by reason of occupation than would an office administrator.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者s group occupations into various classes according to the risk of accident or illness and use it to establish 保険料 rates based upon the occupational hazard. For example, a high-rise construction worker could be more likely to suffer an injury by reason of occupation than would an office administrator.

paragraphs.text: Not every occupation can be found on the occupation risk class list but most jobs can be broadly classified.
paragraphs.textJP: Not every occupation can be found on the occupation risk class list but most jobs can be broadly classified.

paragraphs.text: As you would expect, the higher the risk of injury or illness resulting from an occupation, the higher the insurance premium is likely to be.
paragraphs.textJP: As you would expect, the higher the risk of injury or illness resulting from an occupation, the higher the insurance 保険料 is likely to be.

paragraphs.text: Persistency, which measures how long the customers renew their policies, is a parameter in pricing both Individual and Group Health Insurance. Persistency usually improves as the policies age.
paragraphs.textJP: 継続率, which measures how long the customers renew their policies, is a parameter in pricing both Individual and Group Health Insurance. 継続率 usually improves as the policies age.

paragraphs.text: Persistency also varies by age groups. A group of younger insureds (e.g. ages of 20 to 29 years) may have poorer persistency than a group of older insureds (e.g. ages of 50 to 59 years). The older insureds will tend to see the policy as more valuable, because they may have more difficulty satisfying the underwriting requirements associated with buying a new policy.
paragraphs.textJP: 継続率 also varies by age groups. A group of younger 被保険者s (e.g. ages of 20 to 29 years) may have poorer 継続率 than a group of older 被保険者s (e.g. ages of 50 to 59 years). The older 被保険者s will tend to see the policy as more valuable, because they may have more difficulty satisfying the 引受審査 requirements associated with buying a new policy.

paragraphs.text: If the persistency rate of a type of coverage is expected to be high, then the insurer will reduce the amount of premium charged.
paragraphs.textJP: If the 継続率 rate of a type of 補償 is expected to be high, then the 保険者 will reduce the amount of 保険料 charged.

paragraphs.text: Claims experience of the health insurance portfolio has great influence on its premiums. An actuary has to ensure that, above all other expenses, the collected premiums must be sufficient to pay expected claims. CLAIM FORMS
paragraphs.textJP: 保険金請求s experience of the health insurance portfolio has great influence on its 保険料s. An actuary has to ensure that, above all other expenses, the collected 保険料s must be sufficient to pay expected 保険金請求s. 保険金請求 FORMS

paragraphs.text: Total claims of the portfolio comprise of (i) paid claims; (ii) pending claims; (iii) Incurred but not reported claims (IBNR).
paragraphs.textJP: Total 保険金請求s of the portfolio comprise of (i) paid 保険金請求s; (ii) pending 保険金請求s; (iii) Incurred but not reported 保険金請求s (既発生未報告準備金).

paragraphs.text: IBNR are potential claims that have occurred but have not yet been reported to the insurer by the policy owner, such as due to delay reporting. Insurers have to accurately estimate the IBNR claims and set up a reserve to ensure they have sufficient funds to cover these potential future liabilities.
paragraphs.textJP: 既発生未報告準備金 are potential 保険金請求s that have occurred but have not yet been reported to the 保険者 by the policy owner, such as due to delay reporting. 保険者s have to accurately estimate the 既発生未報告準備金 保険金請求s and set up a reserve to ensure they have sufficient funds to cover these potential future liabilities.

paragraphs.text: For group plans, claims experience of the group is a key factor affecting the premium rate charged. For example, for group outpatient plans, claims experience demonstrates the general health condition of the group and the propensity of the group members to file claims. The importance of the claims experience data in pricing becomes greater as the group size gets bigger, and more years of data are available.
paragraphs.textJP: For group plans, 保険金請求s experience of the group is a key factor affecting the 保険料 rate charged. For example, for group 外来 plans, 保険金請求s experience demonstrates the general health condition of the group and the propensity of the group members to file 保険金請求s. The importance of the 保険金請求s experience data in pricing becomes greater as the group size gets bigger, and more years of data are available.

paragraphs.text: Some insurers use claims-based pricing to adjust individual’s health insurance premiums based on his claims on the policy. For example, a renewal discount may be given if no claims are made on the policy or if a claim is made, the insured is not penalised if they had attempted to keep claims cost low by electing to receive treatment at a public hospital.
paragraphs.textJP: Some 保険者s use 保険金請求s-based pricing to adjust individual’s health insurance 保険料s based on his 保険金請求s on the policy. For example, a renewal discount may be given if no 保険金請求s are made on the policy or if a 保険金請求 is made, the 被保険者 is not penalised if they had attempted to keep 保険金請求s cost low by electing to receive treatment at a public hospital.

paragraphs.text:
For voluntary group plans, the extent of participation by employees in the plan is an important parameter in the development of premium rates. If the participation is low, there is a greater chance that a higher-than-normal proportion of individuals with health issues will seek coverage. This is called anti-selection. If the participation level is high, there will likely be a good mix of healthy lives to compensate for anti-selection risk. Thus, many insurers vary premium rates based on the participation level. However, this strategy comes with a price – additions of each benefit actually increase the final premium that the insurer charges. ▪ Quarterly – once every three months; or
