▪ Long-Term Care Insurance
▪ Review the 給付s that are payable and the 給付 trigger point, the definitions used for the 日常生活動作 and the number of ADLs needed before the 給付s are payable.
▪ Find out if it is a 実費精算・償還払い type of 給付, where the payment is based on the actual amount of expenses incurred. Know what to do if the prospective client has a:
▪ 入院日額保険 Find out the daily 給付 and the maximum number of days that the 給付 is payable. Know what to do if the prospective client has a:
▪ Life Insurance & Employee 給付s Find out if the prospective client is covered under his company’s insurance and whether he has any Life Insurance policy. Quantify disability income protection needs
▪ Method 1 » Using the monthly salary, 75% of it is his income protection need in the event of his disability.
▪ Method 2 » This method uses monthly expenses instead of income to determine the 補償 needed.
▪ Method 3 » This method provides a lump sum 給付 should a person become totally and permanently disabled. Quantify medical and ancillary costs To quantify medical cost needs, the representative needs to discuss and make some assumptions with the prospective clients as follows:
▪ The most common conditions for a person to be hospitalised;
▪ The cost of treatment for any one particular illness, such as a heart condition;
▪ The hospital type and ward the client would opt for in the event of 入院;
▪ The need to provide 補償 for medical cost from the first dollar or can he accept a 自己負担;
▪ The continuous affordability of medical insurance 保険料s when he is no longer working. Quantify hospital cash income needs Estimated level of protection needed = Total monthly expense Less Existing hospital cash 給付 per month IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Quantify Critical Illness Insurance Calculation is really more of an art than a science, since there are many uncertainties. Some factors to consider for the prospective client are described below:
▪ Which 特定疾病 is he most susceptible to? His family medical history may shed some light on this answer. Therefore, medical costs can be approximated.
▪ His family’s needs and expenses.
▪ His family’s level of dependence on him.
▪ Does he already have sufficient 医療費保険 and/or Disability Income Insurance?
▪ His desired level of medical treatment, e.g. private or public hospital.
▪ Whether he wants to buy an Additional 給付 Type or Acceleration 給付 Type of CI Insurance. If he decides on the Additional 給付 Type, then he must be informed that the 補償 will normally cease at the age of 65 years, unlike the Acceleration 給付 Type. Since the Acceleration 給付 Type is usually attached to a Whole Life Insurance policy, this type of 給付 will last the whole of life.
▪ Does he want a 給付 on early diagnosis of a critical illness, does he want to be able to make multiple critical illness 保険金請求s, does he want to cover other conditions such as diabetes or mental illness?
▪ His preference and comfort level. Some prospective clients will require only S$50,000 CI Insurance cover, while others will feel that even S$500,000 cover is insufficient. Basic principles before making any product recommendation
▪ Recommend products only if the prospective client needs them.
▪ Recommend products which are only the most suitable for the prospective client, given his existing circumstances.
▪ If prospective client needs a product that the company you represent does not carry, let him know, so that he can find alternatives. Sample questions to ask for better understanding of the product
▪ What is the product range?
▪ What does the product serve to do? For example, 実費精算・償還払い of expenses or replacement of income?
▪ What are the eligibility criteria for making a 保険金請求 under the policy?
▪ Does the policy have any 待機期間? If so, find out the duration and effective date of the 待機期間.
▪ Is the policy 更新保証, optionally renewable, 条件付更新 or cancellable?
▪ What are the 免責事由s and limitations under the policy?
▪ Does the policy have any special features, such as the 生存期間要件 under a Critical Illness Insurance policy, and the requirement to inform the 保険者 of any change in occupation under a Disability Income Insurance policy?
▪ Is the policy subject to any Co-ordination of 給付 Clause?
▪ Does the policy have any 免責金額 or 共同保険 feature?
▪ Is there any per day, per year or per 通算限度額 under the policy? How much are the limits?
▪ When and how are the 給付s payable? For example, a Hospital Cash (Income) policy pays a flat amount for each day of 入院 capped at a certain number of days.
▪ What are the distribution costs, charges and expenses levied on the products?
▪ What is the duration of cover under the policy?
▪ What are the 保険料s and the frequency modes for 保険料 payments, and are they subject to changes?
▪ Is there a 猶予期間 allowed for the payment of 保険料 due and, if so, what is the time frame?
▪ What is the implication of non-payment of 保険料?
▪ What are the medical and non-medical limits (i.e. the 引受審査 guidelines on when a medical test is required for the assessment of an application) of the products?
▪ Which types of policies have non-forfeiture options?
▪ Is there any geographical limit imposed on the policy? IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Why is affordability an important factor when selecting a suitable product to recommend to the prospective client?
▪ Affordability is an important factor when selecting the most suitable product to recommend to the prospective client.
▪ Most policies require regular 保険料s to be paid. Being able to afford the initial 保険料 does not mean that he will be able to sustain the regular payments.
▪ The 保険者, the insurance representative and the prospective client do not 給付 from early policy lapses. » The prospective client loses the 補償. » The insurance representative loses the prospective client’s goodwill and the commission payable under the policy. » The 保険者 has to absorb the high initial cost of 引受審査 the policy. Important points on policy selection
▪ Ensure that the new recommended policy provides cover only for his 補償 shortfall to minimise 超過保険.
▪ Ensure that the prospective client has the longest 補償 possible.
▪ Ensure that the prospective client can afford the 保険料 in the long term.
▪ Ensure that the prospective client is not over-被保険者.
▪ Ensure that the policy insures the right life.
▪ Ensure that the prospective client with dependants has sufficient death protection in place.
▪ Recommend a 特約 if it can help to save 保険料s.
▪ For Critical Illness (CI) Insurance, take note of the two types of cover - Additional 給付 Type and Acceleration 給付 Type.
▪ Discuss the following with the client - expiry age, 保険料 difference, whether he should take up a CI 特約 if he has existing life policies.
▪ Consider to attach the CI Waiver of 保険料 特約 for the client who has other life insurance protection.
▪ For the prospective client who is covered under his employer-sponsored 医療費保険 (MEI) and has a need for additional MEI cover, ensure that the proposed plan complement the one provided by the employer.
▪ Always check for alternative policies to the one that you are recommending. Example of a systematic process of explaining recommendations to the prospective client
▪ State the purpose of the product (i.e. the need which is actually being met by this product).
▪ Give a description of the nature of the product.
▪ Brief the prospective client on the 給付s and limitations of the product.
▪ Give a detailed explanation on the options within the product.
▪ Give a summary of the reasons why the product is being recommended.
▪ Explain the 給付 illustration and highlight the guaranteed and nonguaranteed 給付s (if applicable).
▪ Disclose any distribution costs, charges and expenses under the policy. When the reviews for prospective client are necessary?
▪ A change in the client’s personal circumstances, such as the birth of a child.
▪ External developments, such as changes in the CPF（中央積立基金） ruling which can have an impact on the client’s financial position and/or the appropriateness of products already held.
▪ Original products pur地域医療支援スキームed by the client are not adequate to cover all his need.
▪ Launching of new products in the financial services market.

chapters.title: CASE STUDIES
learningOutcomes: apply the principles that you have learnt from the previous chapters so as to carry out a proper needs-based sales advisory process through looking at two case scenarios

paragraphs.text: In this chapter, we bring you through two case studies to illustrate how the whole needs-based sales process may be carried out.
paragraphs.textJP: In this chapter, we bring you through two case studies to illustrate how the whole needs-based sales process may be carried out.

paragraphs.text: The examples given here cannot be used as a comprehensive guide for every situation. You should also keep abreast of the LIA and GIA Guidelines On Disclosure Requirements and Needs-Based Sales Process for A&H Insurance Products. 2. CASE STUDY 1 – INDIVIDUAL HEALTH INSURANCE
paragraphs.textJP: The examples given here cannot be used as a comprehensive guide for every situation. You should also keep abreast of the LIA and GIA Guidelines On Disclosure Requirements and Needs-Based Sales Process for A&H Insurance Products. 2. CASE STUDY 1 – INDIVIDUAL HEALTH INSURANCE

paragraphs.text: Angela has done a fact-find with Tommy Tang, her new client, who is a Singapore Citizen. Below is a summary of the data that she has extracted from the Fact-Find Document: Mr Tang : 35 years old Mrs Tang : 35 years old, housewife Son : 5 years old Daughter : 1 year old
paragraphs.textJP: Angela has done a fact-find with Tommy Tang, her new client, who is a Singapore Citizen. Below is a summary of the data that she has extracted from the 事実調査書: Mr Tang : 35 years old Mrs Tang : 35 years old, housewife Son : 5 years old Daughter : 1 year old

paragraphs.text:
Existing Health Insurance Policies:
▪ Group Hospital & Surgical (H & S) Insurance (Plan A) (See Appendix 15A)
paragraphs.textJP:
Existing Health Insurance Policies:
▪ Group Hospital & Surgical (H & S) Insurance (Plan A) (See Appendix 15A)

paragraphs.text: Angela has also obtained the following information from Tommy: (a) Mr and Mrs Tang have sufficient cover for death protection. (b) His family’s average monthly expenditure is S$3,000 and his income is S$6,000. (c) He has not set aside any funds for protection planning, other than his savings which is intended to meet any emergency needs, such as hospitalisation. (d) He would like to have a Critical Illness Insurance policy of S$200,000 for himself and S$100,000 for his wife. (e) He thinks that he will need maintenance cost for 21 years for his wife in the event of his disability, as the youngest child should be independent by then. (f) The estimated expenses required for the children in the event of Mrs Tang’s disability are S$1,500 per month. (g) He and his family would stay in the A ward of a public hospital should they be hospitalised. (h) His budget for Health Insurance cover is S$700 per month.
paragraphs.textJP: Angela has also obtained the following information from Tommy: (a) Mr and Mrs Tang have sufficient cover for death protection. (b) His family’s average monthly expenditure is S$3,000 and his income is S$6,000. (c) He has not set aside any funds for protection planning, other than his savings which is intended to meet any emergency needs, such as 入院. (d) He would like to have a Critical Illness Insurance policy of S$200,000 for himself and S$100,000 for his wife. (e) He thinks that he will need maintenance cost for 21 years for his wife in the event of his disability, as the youngest child should be independent by then. (f) The estimated expenses required for the children in the event of Mrs Tang’s disability are S$1,500 per month. (g) He and his family would stay in the A ward of a public hospital should they be hospitalised. (h) His budget for Health Insurance cover is S$700 per month.

paragraphs.text: Having gathered the necessary information, Angela then proceeds to analyse the insurance needs of Tommy and his family members.
paragraphs.textJP: Having gathered the necessary information, Angela then proceeds to analyse the insurance needs of Tommy and his family members.

paragraphs.text: The most suitable policy for maintenance cost is Disability Income Insurance.
paragraphs.textJP: The most suitable policy for maintenance cost is Disability Income Insurance.

paragraphs.text: Tommy does not have a Disability Income Insurance policy. He has indicated in his Fact-Find Document that he has an urgent need for cover for loss of income resulting from accident or sickness, as he is the sole breadwinner in his family.
paragraphs.textJP: Tommy does not have a Disability Income Insurance policy. He has indicated in his 事実調査書 that he has an urgent need for cover for loss of income resulting from accident or sickness, as he is the sole breadwinner in his family.

paragraphs.text: As such, Angela proposes a Disability Income Insurance policy with benefit at 75% of Tommy’s monthly salary, i.e. S$4,500 and an escalation benefit of 5%. This will ensure that the family is adequately provided for, in the event of Tommy’s disability. The deferred period will be six months, as the employer will continue to pay Tommy’s salary up to six months in the event that he is disabled. Mrs Tang, a housewife, is not eligible for Disability Income Insurance. In her case, there are two possible ways to provide her with protection against disability, i.e. through the Total and Permanent Disability (TPD) Benefit under the Life Insurance policies, or through a Personal Accident Insurance policy.
paragraphs.textJP: As such, Angela proposes a Disability Income Insurance policy with 給付 at 75% of Tommy’s monthly salary, i.e. S$4,500 and an escalation 給付 of 5%. This will ensure that the family is adequately provided for, in the event of Tommy’s disability. The 据置期間 will be six months, as the employer will continue to pay Tommy’s salary up to six months in the event that he is disabled. Mrs Tang, a housewife, is not eligible for Disability Income Insurance. In her case, there are two possible ways to provide her with protection against disability, i.e. through the Total and Permanent Disability (TPD) 給付 under the Life Insurance policies, or through a Personal Accident Insurance policy.

paragraphs.text: The amount of TPD cover which she requires is as follows: Maintenance Costs Required On Mrs Tang’s Disablement Yearly income needed Number of years income is needed Expected annual rate of return from funds (adjusted for inflation) Estimated funds required to provide income Less Funds available Existing Insurance Funds required : S$1,500 (current monthly expenditure) x 12 = S$18,000 : 21 years : 3% (assume that rate of return is 5%, and inflation rate is 2%) : S$18,000 x 15.8775 (refer to Table A2 at end of this Study Guide) = S$285,795 : S$0 : S$285,795
paragraphs.textJP: The amount of TPD cover which she requires is as follows: Maintenance Costs Required On Mrs Tang’s Disablement Yearly income needed Number of years income is needed Expected annual rate of return from funds (adjusted for inflation) Estimated funds required to provide income Less Funds available Existing Insurance Funds required : S$1,500 (current monthly expenditure) x 12 = S$18,000 : 21 years : 3% (assume that rate of return is 5%, and inflation rate is 2%) : S$18,000 x 15.8775 (refer to Table A2 at end of this Study Guide) = S$285,795 : S$0 : S$285,795

paragraphs.text: Having determined the maintenance costs for the Tangs, Angela then goes on to determine whether the Tangs’ current Medical Expense Insurance policies are sufficient to meet their needs.
paragraphs.textJP: Having determined the maintenance costs for the Tangs, Angela then goes on to determine whether the Tangs’ current 医療費保険 policies are sufficient to meet their needs.

paragraphs.text: The policies that may be used to meet medical costs are: (a) Medical Expense Insurance; (b) Managed Healthcare Insurance; (c) Long-Term Care Insurance; (d) Critical Illness Insurance; (e) Hospital Cash Insurance; and (f) Personal Accident policy with medical expense benefit.
paragraphs.textJP: The policies that may be used to meet medical costs are: (a) 医療費保険; (b) Managed Healthcare Insurance; (c) Long-Term Care Insurance; (d) Critical Illness Insurance; (e) 入院日額保険; and (f) Personal Accident policy with medical expense 給付.

paragraphs.text: According to the fact-find results, Tommy only has Medical Expense Insurance coverage under his employer’s insurance. Angela informed Tommy that Group H&S is not portable and that he may lose it later or the coverage may change if he moves into a different job or retire. They agreed to consider Group H&S as a temporary (not lifetime) benefit instead. She then proceeds to determine the coverage he requires for Medical Expense Insurance policies to meet the Tangs’ needs in the event of hospitalisation.
paragraphs.textJP: According to the fact-find results, Tommy only has 医療費保険 補償 under his employer’s insurance. Angela informed Tommy that Group H&S is not portable and that he may lose it later or the 補償 may change if he moves into a different job or retire. They agreed to consider Group H&S as a temporary (not lifetime) 給付 instead. She then proceeds to determine the 補償 he requires for 医療費保険 policies to meet the Tangs’ needs in the event of 入院.

paragraphs.text: Angela went through the following to recommend a suitable Medical Expense Insurance: (a) Confirm his preference of stay in the event of hospitalisation. (b) Briefly compute what his Group H&S would have covered him in a specific medical condition to provide a view if it can cover a portion of co-insurance and deductible of a hospitalisation for as long as the GHS is valid. (c) Present a medical expense insurance plan that will provide cover for Public Ward A hospitalisation, showing the current and future annual premiums of the proposed plan. Please refer to the insurer’s website for the latest premium rates. (d) Tommy was informed that: (i) Premium rate is non-guaranteed. With medical inflation, the insurer will update the current premium table periodically and premium will get more expensive in the future. This is to give a practical view of the future affordability of premium on the chosen plan. (ii) While the benefits payable under Group H&S can be used to cover part of co-insurance and deductible of the average bill size of the medical condition, Medical Expense Insurance and Group H&S are both subject to the Co-ordination of Benefits Clause. Hence it may not be necessary to have first dollar cover for his individual medical expense insurance. This way, he can save some premiums to fund for future premiums. (iii) Once he loses the Group H&S, his co-payments for any hospitalisation has to be made out-of-pocket. (iv) Tommy acknowledged this and agreed to go with a plan that has deductible and co-insurance in order to lower the premiums.
paragraphs.textJP: Angela went through the following to recommend a suitable 医療費保険: (a) Confirm his preference of stay in the event of 入院. (b) Briefly compute what his Group H&S would have covered him in a specific medical condition to provide a view if it can cover a portion of 共同保険 and 免責金額 of a 入院 for as long as the GHS is valid. (c) Present a 医療費保険 plan that will provide cover for Public Ward A 入院, showing the current and future annual 保険料s of the proposed plan. Please refer to the 保険者’s website for the latest 保険料 rates. (d) Tommy was informed that: (i) 保険料 rate is non-guaranteed. With 医療インフレ, the 保険者 will update the current 保険料 table periodically and 保険料 will get more expensive in the future. This is to give a practical view of the future affordability of 保険料 on the chosen plan. (ii) While the 給付s payable under Group H&S can be used to cover part of 共同保険 and 免責金額 of the average bill size of the medical condition, 医療費保険 and Group H&S are both subject to the Co-ordination of 給付s Clause. Hence it may not be necessary to have first dollar cover for his individual 医療費保険. This way, he can save some 保険料s to fund for future 保険料s. (iii) Once he loses the Group H&S, his 自己負担s for any 入院 has to be made out-of-pocket. (iv) Tommy acknowledged this and agreed to go with a plan that has 免責金額 and 共同保険 in order to lower the 保険料s.

paragraphs.text: The discussion with Tommy on affordability should not only be on the present, but also the future sustainability to pay premiums, whether with Medisave or cash.
paragraphs.textJP: The discussion with Tommy on affordability should not only be on the present, but also the future sustainability to pay 保険料s, whether with メディセーブ or cash.

paragraphs.text: The Tangs also require Critical Illness Insurance cover, as the medical expenses incurred in the event of a critical illness may not be fully covered by Tommy’s policies. They will also need Long-Term Care Insurance cover (under ElderShield) if they can afford the premium, as the cover is not insured by his two existing policies.
paragraphs.textJP: The Tangs also require Critical Illness Insurance cover, as the medical expenses incurred in the event of a critical illness may not be fully covered by Tommy’s policies. They will also need Long-Term Care Insurance cover (under エルダーシールド) if they can afford the 保険料, as the cover is not 被保険者 by his two existing policies.

paragraphs.text: As the Tangs already have policies for protection against premature death, Angela proposed a 100% Acceleration Benefit type of Critical Illness Insurance policy packaged with a Whole Life Insurance policy, so that they can have the cover for life. This policy will also take care of Mrs Tang’s TPD needs. To summarise, the package for Mr and Mrs Tang is shown in Table 15.1 below: Table 15.1: Summary Of Insurance Package For Mr & Mrs Tang Mr Tang (Tommy) Type Of Plan & Sum Assured Monthly Premium Enhance Income Shield Advantage Plan S$500.63/year and Fully funded by Medisave 100% Acceleration Benefit Type of Critical Illness Insurance policy packaged with Whole Life Insurance policy - S$200,000 S$380.00 Disability Income Insurance of S$4,500, with a 5% escalation and six months’ deferred period, up to the age of 65 years S$125.50 Total Monthly Premium with cash S$505.50 Mrs Tang Type Of Plan & Sum Assured Monthly Premium Enhance Income Shield Advantage Plan S$500.63/year and Fully funded by Medisave 100% Acceleration Benefit Type of Critical Illness Insurance policy packaged with Whole Life Insurance policy - S$100,000 S$180.00 Personal Accident (PA) Insurance policy - S$50,000 S$10.00 Total Monthly Premium with cash S$190.00 Two Children Type Of Plan & Sum Assured Monthly Premium Enhance Income Shield Advantage Plan S$175.63/year per child and Fully funded by Medisave Source of IncomeShield Premium: https://www.income.com.sg/forms/general/enhanced-incomeshieldmain- premium-table
paragraphs.textJP: As the Tangs already have policies for protection against premature death, Angela proposed a 100% Acceleration 給付 type of Critical Illness Insurance policy packaged with a Whole Life Insurance policy, so that they can have the cover for life. This policy will also take care of Mrs Tang’s TPD needs. To summarise, the package for Mr and Mrs Tang is shown in Table 15.1 below: Table 15.1: Summary Of Insurance Package For Mr & Mrs Tang Mr Tang (Tommy) Type Of Plan & 保険金額 Monthly 保険料 Enhance Income Shield Advantage Plan S$500.63/year and Fully funded by メディセーブ 100% Acceleration 給付 Type of Critical Illness Insurance policy packaged with Whole Life Insurance policy - S$200,000 S$380.00 Disability Income Insurance of S$4,500, with a 5% escalation and six months’ 据置期間, up to the age of 65 years S$125.50 Total Monthly 保険料 with cash S$505.50 Mrs Tang Type Of Plan & 保険金額 Monthly 保険料 Enhance Income Shield Advantage Plan S$500.63/year and Fully funded by メディセーブ 100% Acceleration 給付 Type of Critical Illness Insurance policy packaged with Whole Life Insurance policy - S$100,000 S$180.00 Personal Accident (PA) Insurance policy - S$50,000 S$10.00 Total Monthly 保険料 with cash S$190.00 Two Children Type Of Plan & 保険金額 Monthly 保険料 Enhance Income Shield Advantage Plan S$175.63/year per child and Fully funded by メディセーブ Source of IncomeShield 保険料: https://www.income.com.sg/forms/general/enhanced-incomeshieldmain- 保険料-table

paragraphs.text: The total premium payable with cash works out to be S$695.50 (i.e. S$505.50 + S$190.00) and is within Tommy’s S$700 budget. As there is no excess premium for the Long-Term Care Insurance cover, Angela has decided not to propose it at this moment.
paragraphs.textJP: The total 保険料 payable with cash works out to be S$695.50 (i.e. S$505.50 + S$190.00) and is within Tommy’s S$700 budget. As there is no excess 保険料 for the Long-Term Care Insurance cover, Angela has decided not to propose it at this moment.

paragraphs.text:
Having decided on the types of policies that are suitable to Tommy, Angela next proceeds to complete the “Representative Recommendations” section of the Fact-Find Document. REPRESENTATIVE’S RECOMMENDATIONS Please note that the information that you provide will be the basis on which advice will be given. Please also note that any inaccurate or incomplete information provided may affect the suitability of the recommendations presented to you. Client’s Objective(s) Client’s concern, investment objectives, shortfall amount ($), time horizon, investment risk profile, where applicable He likes to have a Critical Illness Insurance policy of S$200,000 for himself and S$100,000 for his wife. REPRESENTATIVE’S RECOMMENDATIONS Plan(s) Recommendation Full name of plan and rider, duration, sum assured ($), premium ($)/frequency, fund name(s), investment amount and respective risk classification, where applicable Reason(s) for Recommendations
▪ State how the plan meets the Client’s need(s).
▪ State and explain features and benefits relating to the product sold. Risk / Limitation(s) of Plan
▪ State any possible risks relating to the product sold.
▪ State possible disadvantage(s) based on circumstances of the Client.  Medical Expense Insurance Proposed Enhanced IncomeShield Advantage Plan for the entire family which are fully payable with Medisave. This plan provides cover for up to Public Hospital Ward A in the event of hospitalisation. There is co-insurance and deductible on this policy to make the premium affordable.  Critical Illness Insurance Policy Tommy Proposed a 100% Acceleration Benefit Type of Critical Illness Insurance cover packaged with a Whole Life Insurance policy – sum assured of S$200,000 as specified by Tommy. This policy is suitable for Tommy’s budget. It will provide him with death, disability and critical illness coverage. It serves to supplement the Whole Life Insurance policy in providing for the family, in the event of his early demise. Reason(s) for Deviation(s) Reasons for any deviation from client’s profile, objectives and/or representative’s recommendations (where applicable)
▪ Premiums are more than Client’s budget
▪ Funds recommended (e.g. ILP sub-fund, par fund) are of a higher risk than client’s risk preference Client’s choice of product/funds differs from representative’s recommended plan/funds Furthermore, it provides life cover for him, ensuring that he is covered in his old age. Mrs Tang Proposed a 100% Acceleration Benefit Type of Critical Illness Insurance cover packaged with a Whole Life Insurance policy – sum assured of S$100,000 as specified by Mr Tang. This policy will give Mrs Tang the additional TPD cover that she needs. It will also provide her with life cover, thus ensuring that she will be covered in her old age when her need is the greatest. REPRESENTATIVE’S RECOMMENDATIONS  Disability Expense Protection Tommy Proposed a Disability Income Insurance policy of S$4,500 (75% of salary), with 5% escalation and a six months’ deferred period, up to the age of 65 years This policy is considered suitable for Tommy, because it will provide his family with a monthly income in the event that he is totally or partially disabled. Mrs Tang Has not proposed Disability Income Insurance policy, as she is not working and, hence, not eligible. Has proposed a PA Insurance policy of S$50,000 to make up the shortfall for TPD cover after she takes up the S$100,000 Critical Illness Insurance policy. This policy is recommended, as it will provide her with cover for death, TPD and medical expenses at a very low cost. This means that this policy may also help to take care of the shortfall in Medical Expense Insurance cover.
paragraphs.textJP:
Having decided on the types of policies that are suitable to Tommy, Angela next proceeds to complete the “Representative Recommendations” section of the 事実調査書. REPRESENTATIVE’S RECOMMENDATIONS Please note that the information that you provide will be the basis on which advice will be given. Please also note that any inaccurate or incomplete information provided may affect the 適合性 of the recommendations presented to you. Client’s Objective(s) Client’s concern, investment objectives, shortfall amount ($), time horizon, investment risk profile, where applicable He likes to have a Critical Illness Insurance policy of S$200,000 for himself and S$100,000 for his wife. REPRESENTATIVE’S RECOMMENDATIONS Plan(s) Recommendation Full name of plan and 特約, duration, 保険金額 ($), 保険料 ($)/frequency, fund name(s), investment amount and respective risk classification, where applicable Reason(s) for Recommendations
▪ State how the plan meets the Client’s need(s).
▪ State and explain features and 給付s relating to the product sold. Risk / Limitation(s) of Plan
▪ State any possible risks relating to the product sold.
▪ State possible disadvantage(s) based on circumstances of the Client.  医療費保険 Proposed Enhanced IncomeShield Advantage Plan for the entire family which are fully payable with メディセーブ. This plan provides cover for up to Public Hospital Ward A in the event of 入院. There is 共同保険 and 免責金額 on this policy to make the 保険料 affordable.  Critical Illness Insurance Policy Tommy Proposed a 100% Acceleration 給付 Type of Critical Illness Insurance cover packaged with a Whole Life Insurance policy – 保険金額 of S$200,000 as specified by Tommy. This policy is suitable for Tommy’s budget. It will provide him with death, disability and critical illness 補償. It serves to supplement the Whole Life Insurance policy in providing for the family, in the event of his early demise. Reason(s) for Deviation(s) Reasons for any deviation from client’s profile, objectives and/or representative’s recommendations (where applicable)
▪ 保険料s are more than Client’s budget
▪ Funds recommended (e.g. ILP sub-fund, par fund) are of a higher risk than client’s risk preference Client’s choice of product/funds differs from representative’s recommended plan/funds Furthermore, it provides life cover for him, ensuring that he is covered in his old age. Mrs Tang Proposed a 100% Acceleration 給付 Type of Critical Illness Insurance cover packaged with a Whole Life Insurance policy – 保険金額 of S$100,000 as specified by Mr Tang. This policy will give Mrs Tang the additional TPD cover that she needs. It will also provide her with life cover, thus ensuring that she will be covered in her old age when her need is the greatest. REPRESENTATIVE’S RECOMMENDATIONS  Disability Expense Protection Tommy Proposed a Disability Income Insurance policy of S$4,500 (75% of salary), with 5% escalation and a six months’ 据置期間, up to the age of 65 years This policy is considered suitable for Tommy, because it will provide his family with a monthly income in the event that he is totally or partially disabled. Mrs Tang Has not proposed Disability Income Insurance policy, as she is not working and, hence, not eligible. Has proposed a PA Insurance policy of S$50,000 to make up the shortfall for TPD cover after she takes up the S$100,000 Critical Illness Insurance policy. This policy is recommended, as it will provide her with cover for death, TPD and medical expenses at a very low cost. This means that this policy may also help to take care of the shortfall in 医療費保険 cover.

paragraphs.text: Having finalised the insurance package to be recommended to the client, Angela then arranges to meet up with his client, and presents the package to him.
paragraphs.textJP: Having finalised the insurance package to be recommended to the client, Angela then arranges to meet up with his client, and presents the package to him.

paragraphs.text: The case study illustrates one of the possible sets of recommendations among others. As a result, certain plausible suggestions have not been discussed, such as extending the client’s coverage to include accidental loss of limbs, or medical expenses arising from accidents.
paragraphs.textJP: The case study illustrates one of the possible sets of recommendations among others. As a result, certain plausible suggestions have not been discussed, such as extending the client’s 補償 to include accidental loss of limbs, or medical expenses arising from accidents.

paragraphs.text: You should bring these documents to the attention of your prospective client and discuss them with him. 3. CASE STUDY 2 – GROUP HEALTH INSURANCE
paragraphs.textJP: You should bring these documents to the attention of your prospective client and discuss them with him. 3. CASE STUDY 2 – GROUP HEALTH INSURANCE

paragraphs.text: We will now look at another case study focusing on a prospective client’s need for Group Health Insurance. Apex Services Company Private Limited has approached you for quotations on Group Term Life and Health Insurance policies to cover its employees as part of its employee benefit package. As the Company wants the policies to be tailored to their needs, you will have to get the Company to complete the Group Insurance Fact-Finding (GIFF) Form. Note that the format of the Form may vary for different insurers.
paragraphs.textJP: We will now look at another case study focusing on a prospective client’s need for Group Health Insurance. Apex Services Company Private Limited has approached you for quotations on Group Term Life and Health Insurance policies to cover its employees as part of its employee 給付 package. As the Company wants the policies to be tailored to their needs, you will have to get the Company to complete the 団体保険 Fact-Finding (GIFF) Form. Note that the format of the Form may vary for different 保険者s.

paragraphs.text: Before proceeding further, you need to find out whether the Company (i.e. the prospective client) meets your insurer’s underwriting requirements in terms of group size and group stability, etc.
paragraphs.textJP: Before proceeding further, you need to find out whether the Company (i.e. the prospective client) meets your 保険者’s 引受審査 requirements in terms of group size and group stability, etc.

paragraphs.text: Once you have established that the prospective client is eligible for the group coverage, you may then proceed to explain to your prospective client the following: (a) the types of policies available and the benefits offered; (b) the advantages of joining the Portable Medical Benefits Scheme, Transferable Medical Insurance Scheme or providing a shield plan for employees, as well as how these schemes work; and (c) the differences between compulsory and voluntary participation, as well as their advantages.
paragraphs.textJP: Once you have established that the prospective client is eligible for the group 補償, you may then proceed to explain to your prospective client the following: (a) the types of policies available and the 給付s offered; (b) the advantages of joining the Portable Medical 給付s Scheme, Transferable Medical Insurance Scheme or providing a shield plan for employees, as well as how these schemes work; and (c) the differences between compulsory and voluntary participation, as well as their advantages.

paragraphs.text: In addition, you will need to help your prospective client to determine the amount of sum assured for each category of employees. If your prospective client has an existing plan, they may wish to stick to the same amount of benefit, or make some minor adjustments to it. For a prospective client buying the Group Term Life Insurance for the first time, it is your responsibility to help the prospective client to determine the sum assured. There are two common methods which can be used, i.e.: (a) according to rank; or (b) number of times of the employee’s basic salary.
paragraphs.textJP: In addition, you will need to help your prospective client to determine the amount of 保険金額 for each category of employees. If your prospective client has an existing plan, they may wish to stick to the same amount of 給付, or make some minor adjustments to it. For a prospective client buying the 団体定期生命保険 for the first time, it is your responsibility to help the prospective client to determine the 保険金額. There are two common methods which can be used, i.e.: (a) according to rank; or (b) number of times of the employee’s basic salary.

paragraphs.text: The first method is easier to manage, as it goes according to the rank of the employee. However, it does not take into account the length of service of the staff concerned.
paragraphs.textJP: The first method is easier to manage, as it goes according to the rank of the employee. However, it does not take into account the length of service of the staff concerned.

paragraphs.text: For example, a new staff joining as an executive will get the same amount of benefit as one who has been with the company for two years, although his pay may be lower than the more experienced staff. The second method, on the other hand, takes into account the length of service, as the salary of a more experienced staff is likely to be higher than that of a new staff. Hence, this method may be more equitable as compared to the former method. You may also ask for the client’s budget, as this will also help to determine the sum assured to be covered.
paragraphs.textJP: For example, a new staff joining as an executive will get the same amount of 給付 as one who has been with the company for two years, although his pay may be lower than the more experienced staff. The second method, on the other hand, takes into account the length of service, as the salary of a more experienced staff is likely to be higher than that of a new staff. Hence, this method may be more equitable as compared to the former method. You may also ask for the client’s budget, as this will also help to determine the 保険金額 to be covered.

paragraphs.text: For instance, in this case, your prospective client has told you that the Company prefers to go by the ranking method. Based on the budget given, you can then design a plan, such as the one below: Category Sum Assured Per Person Term Assurance Living Assurance Senior Management S$500,000 S$250,000 Managers/Executives S$250,000 S$125,000 Assistant Executives S$100,000 S$50,000 Clerks S$50,000 S$25,000
paragraphs.textJP: For instance, in this case, your prospective client has told you that the Company prefers to go by the ranking method. Based on the budget given, you can then design a plan, such as the one below: Category 保険金額 Per Person Term Assurance Living Assurance Senior Management S$500,000 S$250,000 Managers/Executives S$250,000 S$125,000 Assistant Executives S$100,000 S$50,000 Clerks S$50,000 S$25,000

paragraphs.text: By attaching 50% Acceleration Critical Illness Insurance cover as a rider to the Group Term Life Insurance policy, it will help your prospective client to provide the benefit at an affordable amount (if there is a constraint in the amount that the Company can spend).
paragraphs.textJP: By attaching 50% Acceleration Critical Illness Insurance cover as a 特約 to the 団体定期生命保険 policy, it will help your prospective client to provide the 給付 at an affordable amount (if there is a constraint in the amount that the Company can spend).

paragraphs.text: For the Group Hospital & Surgical Insurance policy, you can also plan according to the ranking method as follows: Category Plan Senior Management Plan 1 – Room & Board S$450 Managers/Executives Plan 2 – Room & Board S$300 Others Plan 3 – Room & Board S$200
paragraphs.textJP: For the Group Hospital & Surgical Insurance policy, you can also plan according to the ranking method as follows: Category Plan Senior Management Plan 1 – Room & Board S$450 Managers/Executives Plan 2 – Room & Board S$300 Others Plan 3 – Room & Board S$200

paragraphs.text: Next, obtain the Company’s past three years of claims history and list of probable incurred but not yet reported claims. The claims history is very important, as this will determine the premium to be charged.
paragraphs.textJP: Next, obtain the Company’s past three years of 保険金請求s history and list of probable incurred but not yet reported 保険金請求s. The 保険金請求s history is very important, as this will determine the 保険料 to be charged.

paragraphs.text: Note also that your prospective client must duly complete and sign the GIFF Form. It is important that you sign this Form too. The insurer is not allowed to give any quotation, unless this Form has been duly completed and signed. Having completed the GIFF Form, submit it to the insurer to work out a quotation for your presentation to the prospective client.
paragraphs.textJP: Note also that your prospective client must duly complete and sign the 団体保険事実調査書. It is important that you sign this Form too. The 保険者 is not allowed to give any quotation, unless this Form has been duly completed and signed. Having completed the 団体保険事実調査書, submit it to the 保険者 to work out a quotation for your presentation to the prospective client.

paragraphs.text: The sales quotation will include the coverage, underwriting guidelines, premium chargeable, exclusions and waiting periods, etc. You have to go through the quotation and highlight the important points that your prospective client needs to be aware of, e.g. exclusions, limitations, restricted provisions and conditions, as well as no free-look period applicable to any Group Insurance policy.
paragraphs.textJP: The sales quotation will include the 補償, 引受審査 guidelines, 保険料 chargeable, 免責事由s and 待機期間s, etc. You have to go through the quotation and highlight the important points that your prospective client needs to be aware of, e.g. 免責事由s, limitations, restricted provisions and conditions, as well as no クーリングオフ期間 applicable to any 団体保険 policy.
